previous_plan.title: Residential Subdivision Permit Preparation Automation v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Node.js 20 Fastify automation tool that retrieves parcel GIS data from council APIs, populates planning application PDF templates using pdf-lib, and submits permit packages to council portals after manager approval. MongoDB 7 for application state, GCP Cloud Run for hosting.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Fastify API server on Cloud Run handles batch submission and approval workflow. PermitPreparationJob fetches GIS data via per-council GisAdapter implementations, populates templates with pdf-lib, uploads PDFs to Cloud Storage. PortalSubmissionJob submits approved packages to council portals. StatusPollingJob polls portals every 4 hours via Cloud Scheduler.
previous_plan.data_model: parcels, permit_applications, planning_templates, submission_receipts, application_statuses in MongoDB. PDF packages in GCP Cloud Storage.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Custom JWT auth, GCP Secret Manager for portal credentials, audit_events MongoDB collection for all submission actions.

target_output.thinking_summary: This revision addresses two blocking issues: ISS-001 (GIS rate limit enforcement) and ISS-002 (SAML portal submission). For ISS-001, I will add a gis_rate_limit_counters MongoDB collection with atomic findOneAndUpdate increments -- the PermitPreparationJob checks the counter before each GIS call and defers the lot to the next day's queue if the daily limit is reached. For ISS-002, I will introduce a SamlPortalClient that uses Playwright headless browser to automate the SAML SP form POST flow for councils that do not provide REST submission APIs, with the browser instance managed as a Cloud Run job resource.

fix_report.action_taken: Added gis_rate_limit_counters MongoDB collection (council_code, date, call_count, limit) with atomic findOneAndUpdate increment. PermitPreparationJob now calls GisRateLimitTracker.checkAndIncrement(council_code) before each GIS API call; if the returned call_count exceeds the council's rate_limit_per_day (from authority_contacts), the lot is written with lot_status=deferred and queued to the next calendar day's processing run. Daily rate limit counter documents have a TTL index of 7 days for automatic cleanup.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GIS rate limits are enforced atomically at the job level. Large batches that would exceed the daily limit are gracefully deferred rather than failing silently, and coordinators see lot_status=deferred with a scheduled_date for next processing.

fix_report.action_taken: Introduced SamlPortalClient class implementing the PortalClient interface using Playwright headless Chromium. The client launches a headless browser, navigates to the council's SAML SP URL, fills the submission form fields (mapped from authority_contacts.saml_field_map), uploads the PDF permit package, and submits the form. Session cookies are persisted to GCP Secret Manager between submissions to avoid re-authentication on each lot. The Playwright browser instance runs in the job-runner-service Cloud Run container with the playwright/chromium npm package. Authority_contacts schema extended with submission_method=saml, saml_portal_url, and saml_field_map OBJECT.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Two of three pilot councils with SAML submission portals can now receive automated permit submissions. The SamlPortalClient is tested against staging portal instances using Playwright fixture sessions before production deployment.

target_output.title: Residential Subdivision Permit Preparation Automation -- Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Node.js 20 Fastify automation tool for volume homebuilders that retrieves parcel zoning, easement, and utility data from municipal GIS APIs per jurisdiction, populates jurisdiction-specific planning application PDF templates using pdf-lib, and routes approved permit packages to local authority submission portals via REST or Playwright-automated SAML form POST. GIS API rate limits are enforced per-council per-day using atomic MongoDB counters that defer excess lots to the next processing day. Manager approval is required before portal submission. A Cloud Scheduler status polling agent tracks application_statuses and notifies coordinators on council responses. This revision resolves GIS rate limit enforcement and SAML portal submission architecture.
target_output.architecture_overview: Cloud Run-hosted Fastify API (api-service) and batch job runner (job-runner-service) with MongoDB 7 Atlas for state and GCP Cloud Storage for PDF permit packages. The PermitPreparationJob uses a per-council GisAdapter abstraction (WfsGisAdapter, RestGisAdapter, CsvGisAdapter) to fetch parcel data; before each GIS call, GisRateLimitTracker performs an atomic MongoDB findOneAndUpdate on the gis_rate_limit_counters collection to check and increment the daily call count -- lots exceeding the council's rate_limit_per_day are written with lot_status=deferred and re-queued to the next day via Cloud Tasks. After manager approval via POST /v1/batches/BATCH_ID/approve, the PortalSubmissionJob routes each permit package to the council's PortalClient implementation: RestPortalClient for REST-based councils, or SamlPortalClient (Playwright headless Chromium) for SAML SP form POST councils. The StatusPollingJob runs every 4 hours via Cloud Scheduler, querying each active application's council portal for status updates and writing application_statuses. All external GIS and portal calls use HTTPS; mTLS client certificates stored in GCP Secret Manager are presented where portal requires it.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify 4, pdf-lib 1.17 for PDF template field population, node-fetch for GIS/REST portal HTTP clients, Playwright 1.44 with headless Chromium for SAML portal submission, mongoose 8 for MongoDB ODM. Database: MongoDB 7 Atlas on GCP us-central1 with JSON Schema validation. File Storage: GCP Cloud Storage. Auth: Custom HS256 JWT with LDAP AD bind. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run (api-service and job-runner-service), Cloud Tasks for deferred lot queuing, Cloud Scheduler for status polling, Secret Manager for credentials, Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging, GitLab CI, Artifact Registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Parcel Batch Submission: coordinator posts CSV of parcel IDs to POST /v1/batches; tool creates permit_batch and enqueues PermitPreparationJob. GIS Rate-Limited Data Retrieval: GisRateLimitTracker checks and atomically increments per-council per-day counter; defers lot to next day if limit reached. Planning Template Population: pdf-lib maps GIS data fields to PDF form fields per jurisdiction's field_mappings configuration; generates permit package PDF. Manager Approval Gate: manager reviews populated field values vs GIS source in batch detail view and approves via POST /v1/batches/BATCH_ID/approve. Portal Submission: RestPortalClient posts PDF and metadata to REST endpoint; SamlPortalClient uses Playwright to submit via SAML form POST. Status Polling: Cloud Scheduler triggers StatusPollingJob every 4 hours; council portal queried for each active application; status_label written to application_statuses; email notification on status change. Jurisdiction Configuration: admin manages authority_contacts records including GIS adapter type, rate limit, portal submission method, field mappings.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run api-service (Fastify, min 0 max 10 instances, authenticated REST API). Cloud Run job-runner-service (batch jobs including Playwright headless Chromium, min 0 max 5 instances, Standard 2 vCPU for Playwright). Cloud Tasks queue (deferred lots scheduled for next-day GIS processing). Cloud Scheduler (status polling trigger every 4 hours). MongoDB Atlas (GCP us-central1, M10 cluster, VPC peering). GCP Cloud Storage (permit-packages-prod bucket, uniform access). GCP Secret Manager (portal credentials, mTLS certs, Playwright session cookies). Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging.
target_output.workflows: Permit Preparation Workflow: Coordinator posts parcel_ids CSV to POST /v1/batches. API service creates permit_batch and enqueues PermitPreparationJob on Cloud Tasks. Job runner starts, iterates each parcel_id. For each lot, determines jurisdiction_code from parcel_id prefix, instantiates correct GisAdapter. Calls GisRateLimitTracker.checkAndIncrement(council_code, date): MongoDB findOneAndUpdate increments call_count; if incremented count exceeds rate_limit_per_day from authority_contacts, writes lot_status=deferred and enqueues a Cloud Tasks task scheduled for tomorrow 08:00 UTC. For non-deferred lots, GisAdapter fetches zoning, easements, setbacks, and utility corridors. pdf-lib opens blank template PDF from Cloud Storage, maps GIS fields to PDF form field names per planning_templates.field_mappings, saves populated PDF to Cloud Storage, writes permit_applications with lot_status=ready. Portal Submission Workflow: Manager reviews batch summary showing populated fields vs GIS source data, calls POST /v1/batches/BATCH_ID/approve. API updates batch status=approved, enqueues PortalSubmissionJob. Job runner iterates ready permit_applications in batch. For each application, reads authority_contacts.submission_method. If rest: RestPortalClient sends multipart POST with PDF and metadata fields to portal_url using council credentials from Secret Manager. If saml: SamlPortalClient launches Playwright Chromium, loads session cookies from Secret Manager, navigates to saml_portal_url, fills saml_field_map fields with permit data, attaches PDF, submits form, captures portal reference number from success page, closes browser. Both clients write submission_receipts on success. Status Polling Workflow: Cloud Scheduler fires every 4 hours, triggers StatusPollingJob HTTP endpoint. Job queries active permit_applications (lot_status=submitted), calls each council's status check endpoint (REST or Playwright page scrape), maps response to application_statuses entry with status_label. On status_label change (e.g., 'Under Assessment' to 'Approved'), sends email via SendGrid to coordinator and manager.
target_output.data_model: parcels (id, parcel_id STRING, jurisdiction_code, address, area_sqm, gis_data_snapshot OBJECT, snapshot_fetched_at). gis_rate_limit_counters (id, council_code, date STRING YYYYMMDD, call_count INT, limit INT -- TTL 7 days, unique index on council_code+date). permit_applications (id, batch_id, parcel_id, jurisdiction_code, template_version, populated_fields OBJECT, pdf_cloud_storage_path, lot_status ENUM pending/preparing/ready/submitted/approved/rejected/deferred, deferred_until DATE, created_at, submitted_at). planning_templates (id, jurisdiction_code unique, template_name, version, field_mappings ARRAY of source_gis_field + pdf_field_name, pdf_blank_storage_path, updated_at). submission_receipts (id, application_id, submitted_at, portal_reference_number, raw_response OBJECT, submission_method ENUM rest/saml). application_statuses (id, application_id, polled_at, status_code, status_label, portal_notes). authority_contacts (id, jurisdiction_code unique, authority_name, portal_url, submission_method ENUM rest/saml, saml_portal_url, saml_field_map OBJECT, rate_limit_per_day INT, credential_secret_name, mtls_cert_secret_name). gis_data_snapshots (id, parcel_id, jurisdiction_code, fetched_at, raw_payload OBJECT -- TTL 3 years). audit_events (id, user_id, action, permit_application_id, jurisdiction_code, timestamp, detail OBJECT -- append-only, TTL 5 years).
target_output.api_design: Fastify REST API at /v1 with JWT Bearer auth. POST /v1/batches (coordinator, CSV body with parcel_ids) -- creates permit_batch, returns batch_id. GET /v1/batches/BATCH_ID -- returns batch status, lot counts by lot_status, deferred lot list with deferred_until dates. GET /v1/batches/BATCH_ID/packages -- lists permit packages with Cloud Storage presigned download URLs. POST /v1/batches/BATCH_ID/approve (manager role) -- triggers PortalSubmissionJob. GET /v1/applications/APP_ID/status -- returns latest application_statuses entry. GET /v1/applications/APP_ID/submission -- returns submission_receipt with portal reference number. GET /v1/admin/jurisdictions (admin role) -- returns authority_contacts list. PUT /v1/admin/jurisdictions/COUNCIL_CODE (admin role) -- updates authority_contacts including rate_limit_per_day and saml_field_map.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Custom HS256 JWT (8-hour expiry) issued on LDAP AD credential validation. Fastify role guard on all routes. GCP Secret Manager stores per-council portal credentials, mTLS client certificate PEMs, JWT signing key, MongoDB connection string, Playwright session cookie JSON, and SMTP credentials. Cloud Run service account has roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor scoped to only the secret resources used by each service. Audit_events collection is append-only (no delete index or permission) and records every GIS fetch, PDF generation, approval, portal submission, and status update. mTLS client certificates loaded from Secret Manager and presented in node-fetch TLS options for councils requiring it. HTTPS enforced for all outbound GIS and portal API calls. Internal API requires JWT for all routes with no public endpoints.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Two Cloud Run services in GCP us-central1: api-service (Fastify, scale 0-10 on request count) and job-runner-service (batch jobs with Playwright, scale 0-5, Standard 2 vCPU, 4 GB memory for Playwright Chromium). Cloud Tasks queue manages deferred lot processing with scheduled task execution time set to next business day 08:00 UTC. Cloud Scheduler job (every 4 hours) calls job-runner-service /jobs/status-poll endpoint. MongoDB Atlas M10 cluster with VPC peering to GCP VPC. GitLab CI: eslint, Jest unit tests with MongoDB memory server, integration tests against Atlas test cluster seeded with 3-jurisdiction fixture data, permit template accuracy assertions per jurisdiction, Docker build, Artifact Registry push, Cloud Run deploy. Playwright SamlPortalClient tested in CI against a mock SAML portal (Docker container with a test SAML IdP).
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring dashboards: Cloud Run request latency and error rate per service, Cloud Tasks queue depth, MongoDB Atlas connection pool utilisation. Custom Cloud Monitoring metrics (via Monitoring API): gis_calls_today by council_code (sourced from gis_rate_limit_counters), portal_submission_success_rate by council_code and submission_method, status_polling_latency_ms. Cloud Logging structured logs include permit_application_id, jurisdiction_code, lot_status, and job_type. Alerts: any portal submission failure triggers PagerDuty on-call (critical); GIS daily call count reaching 90% of council rate limit triggers email to coordinator (warning); Cloud Tasks dead-letter queue depth over 5 triggers email (medium).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ~$420/month at 300 lot submissions per month: Cloud Run (api + job-runner) ~$80, MongoDB Atlas M10 ~$190, Cloud Storage (50 GB PDFs) ~$15, Cloud Tasks ~$5, Secret Manager ~$10, Cloud Scheduler ~$5, Cloud Monitoring and Logging ~$60, Artifact Registry and networking ~$55. Playwright Chromium in job-runner requires 2 vCPU/4 GB Cloud Run instance; each SAML submission job scales to a dedicated instance and terminates after completion. Scaling: parallel batch preparation scales with Cloud Run job-runner instances up to 5 concurrent; GIS rate limit throttling prevents API abuse regardless of batch size.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- 3-Jurisdiction Pilot (8 weeks): GisAdapter implementations for Councils A, B, C (WFS, REST, CSV respectively), GisRateLimitTracker with MongoDB atomic counters, pdf-lib template population for each jurisdiction's planning forms, RestPortalClient for Council A, SamlPortalClient (Playwright) for Councils B and C, manager approval workflow, StatusPollingJob, JWT auth, Cloud Run deployment, GitLab CI with template accuracy tests. Phase 2 -- Scale to 15 Jurisdictions (12 weeks): Additional GisAdapter and PortalClient implementations per jurisdiction sprint, automated fee calculation and payment portal integration, land title registry pre-lodgement search integration. Phase 3 -- Market Expansion (future): EU GDPR compliance controls for UK/Ireland market, multilingual planning template support, mobile coordinator app for batch status monitoring.
target_output.development_guidelines: Each GisAdapter implementation must pass a unit test suite using recorded API response fixtures for its council (VCR-style with nock). SamlPortalClient Playwright tests must run against a local mock SAML IdP Docker container in CI -- no live council portal calls in the test suite. GisRateLimitTracker.checkAndIncrement must be tested with concurrent requests to verify atomic behaviour using MongoDB memory server. Planning template field_mappings are managed via admin API and must never be hardcoded in application logic. All new jurisdictions require a permit template accuracy test with a fixture parcel before production rollout.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Municipal GIS API schema changes break GisAdapter field mappings silently -- mitigation: each GisAdapter includes a schema version assertion that fails fast and alerts on unexpected response structure. Risk: Playwright Chromium in Cloud Run has higher cold start latency (~8 seconds for browser launch) compared to REST clients -- mitigation: pre-warm job-runner instances during business hours and batch SAML submissions together rather than per-lot. Risk: SAML portal session cookies expire mid-batch requiring re-authentication -- mitigation: SamlPortalClient checks session validity before each submission and re-authenticates if needed. Tradeoff: Playwright headless browser for SAML submission over a custom SAML SP library -- Playwright handles the full browser session reliably across council portal variations that custom SAML libraries struggle with, at the cost of higher runtime resource usage.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: gis_rate_limit_counters MongoDB collection with atomic findOneAndUpdate enforces per-council per-day limits; deferred lots are re-queued to the next business day via Cloud Tasks. ISS-002 resolved: SamlPortalClient using Playwright headless Chromium handles SAML SP form POST submission for Councils B and C; Playwright session cookies cached in Secret Manager to avoid re-authentication on each submission. Portal submission_method=saml field added to authority_contacts to route each council to the correct PortalClient implementation at runtime.

metadata.notes: Residential subdivision permit automation, revision 2 adding GIS rate limit enforcement and Playwright SAML portal submission client.

project_goal.value: Kubernetes-based container platform hosting construction microservices (scheduling, document management, cost control, site safety) for a national contractor, replacing a monolithic on-premise ERP
project_goal.rationale: Core infrastructure mandate to enable independent deployability of each construction domain service

target_users.value: Internal DevOps engineers and platform SREs managing construction software deployments
target_users.rationale: Platform consumers are internal engineering teams, not end users

project_class.rationale: Pure platform and environment architecture with no user-facing application surface

capabilities.rationale: Platform control plane and CI/CD orchestration are the primary capability domains

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant AKS cluster hosting independently deployed microservices with service mesh, secrets rotation, and canary rollouts

risk_level.rationale: Platform hosts confidential construction project data; a cluster compromise exposes all tenants

data_sensitivity.rationale: Construction schedules, cost data, and site safety records are commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: AKS cluster is private with no public ingress; accessed via Azure Private Link

access_model.value: Keycloak OIDC with RBAC mapped to Kubernetes RBAC roles per namespace
access_model.rationale: Namespace-scoped access prevents cross-tenant lateral movement

feature_scope.value: AKS cluster provisioning, Helm-based service deployment, Istio service mesh, Azure Key Vault secrets integration, canary rollout controller, multi-namespace tenant isolation
feature_scope.rationale: Full platform feature set for self-service microservice hosting

mvp_scope.value: AKS cluster with Istio, Helm GitOps via Flux, Azure Key Vault CSI driver, baseline RBAC, Prometheus/Grafana observability stack
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum platform for onboarding the first two microservices

security_baseline.value: Private AKS cluster, Pod Security Standards enforced, Workload Identity for Key Vault access, network policies blocking cross-namespace traffic, Azure Defender for Containers enabled
security_baseline.rationale: ISO 27001 and SOC 2 minimum platform controls

frontend_stack.value: none - infrastructure project; Grafana serves observability dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: No user-facing frontend surface; Grafana dashboards serve operational visibility needs

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum for platform control plane API; Helm and Flux for GitOps orchestration
backend_stack.rationale: Low-overhead control plane for cluster management operations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for platform state (Helm release history, deployment manifests); Redis 7 for distributed locks during rollouts
data_platform.rationale: Relational audit trail and atomic rollout coordination

hosting_target.value: AKS private cluster in Azure australiaeast region
hosting_target.rationale: Azure-first organisation; private cluster for confidential workloads

privacy_retention_policy.value: Platform audit logs retained 12 months; Helm release history retained indefinitely; deployment event logs purged after 24 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 audit evidence requirements and operational forensics needs

future_scope.value: Multi-cluster federation across regions, self-service namespace provisioning portal, cost chargeback per microservice team
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 platform maturity roadmap

constraints.value: All cluster nodes must remain within Azure australiaeast; no public endpoints; Terraform for all IaC; Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI
constraints.rationale: Data sovereignty and corporate tooling standards

observability_baseline.value: Prometheus scraping all namespaces, Grafana dashboards per service team, Alertmanager routing to PagerDuty, Loki for log aggregation
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate observability standard for AKS workloads

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure-as-code first with Terraform modules; GitOps via Flux for all Kubernetes manifests
execution_preference.rationale: Reproducible and auditable platform changes

compliance_context.value: ISO 27001 certification scope includes platform; SOC 2 Type II audit covers hosted multi-tenant services; annual penetration test required
compliance_context.rationale: National contractor clients require SOC 2 attestation before onboarding

project.goal: Replace on-premise ERP with independently deployable AKS-hosted microservices platform
project.timeline: Platform MVP Q2; full microservice migration Q4

backend.runtime: Rust with Axum for control plane API

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC mapped to Kubernetes RBAC
security.transport: mTLS via Istio for all service-to-service traffic
security.secrets: Azure Key Vault CSI driver with Workload Identity

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for platform state and audit

devops.ci: Azure DevOps Pipelines with Helm dry-run gate

constraints.tooling: Terraform for IaC, Flux for GitOps

reasoner_reviews.product: The platform scope correctly prioritises GitOps-driven deployments and namespace isolation. The canary rollout controller is essential for safe ERP migration where downtime on scheduling or cost-control services has direct operational impact on active construction sites.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: AKS private cluster with Istio is the right foundation. The control plane in Rust/Axum adds operational complexity but is justified by the low memory footprint needed when running alongside resource-constrained cluster workloads. Flux v2 for GitOps ensures all cluster state is version-controlled and auditable.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure Defender for Containers must be enabled from day one given the SOC 2 scope. Workload Identity eliminates secret sprawl from pod-level credentials. Network policies must default-deny within and across namespaces, with explicit allow rules only for documented service dependencies.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Terraform module structure should separate cluster provisioning from application namespace management to allow independent lifecycle management.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan did not specify a rollback trigger threshold for canary deployments. The issue ISS-001 flagged absence of concrete SLO breach criteria that would halt a canary and revert. This round must define the error-rate and latency SLO thresholds explicitly in the Flagger Canary resource configuration.

backend.service_design: Rust/Axum control plane exposes POST /api/v1/releases to initiate Helm upgrades, POST /api/v1/rollbacks to trigger rollbacks, GET /api/v1/namespaces for cluster topology. Connects to Flux webhook receiver for GitOps event correlation.
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST over HTTPS for control plane; Flux webhook events via signed HMAC payloads
backend.failure_handling: Control plane retries Kubernetes API calls with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts; failed Helm upgrades emit a deployment_failed event to audit PostgreSQL table and trigger PagerDuty alert
backend.backend_risks: Kubernetes API server throttling during mass concurrent deployments; mitigated by client-side rate limiting in Axum service layer

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm per environment; Kubernetes OIDC webhook authenticates tokens; ClusterRoleBindings scoped per namespace team
security.authorization_model: Namespace-scoped RBAC: platform-admin, namespace-owner, namespace-viewer. Cross-namespace API calls blocked by NetworkPolicy default-deny.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault CSI driver mounts secrets as tmpfs volumes; Workload Identity binds pod service accounts to Azure Managed Identities; no Kubernetes Secrets hold sensitive values
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Kubernetes audit policy logs all write operations at RequestResponse level shipped to Loki; PostgreSQL audit_events records Helm release changes with actor identity

data.entities: service_deployments, namespace_configs, secrets_vaults, helm_releases, cluster_nodes, service_mesh_policies, audit_events
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA; Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis for deployment lock acquisition
data.schema_notes: service_deployments.status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed/rolled_back; service_mesh_policies.policy_type ENUM traffic/retry/circuit_breaker; audit_events append-only with no UPDATE or DELETE permissions granted to application role
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_events retained 12 months with row-level archival to Azure Blob Storage; helm_releases retained indefinitely for compliance

devops.deployment_topology: AKS private cluster with 3 system node pools Standard_D4s_v5 and per-team user node pools; Istio ingress gateway as sole internal entry point; Azure Internal Load Balancer only
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: terraform plan on PR; helm lint and kubeval on chart PRs; helm upgrade dry-run against platform-staging; manual approval gate; Flux reconciliation trigger for prod. Canary via Flagger: 10% traffic increment every 5 minutes, automatic rollback if error rate exceeds 1% or p99 latency exceeds 800ms.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus Operator scraping all namespaces; Grafana with DORA metrics and pod restart rates per team; Loki for log aggregation; Alertmanager routes critical alerts to PagerDuty
devops.rollback_strategy: Flagger automated rollback on SLO breach; manual rollback via control plane POST /api/v1/rollbacks; all rollback events written to audit_events and paged via PagerDuty

ISS-001.title: Canary rollout SLO thresholds not defined
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan referenced canary rollout strategy but did not specify concrete error-rate or latency thresholds that trigger automatic rollback, leaving Flagger misconfigured for production use.

history.note: Canary strategy mentioned without Flagger threshold parameters

ISS-002.title: Cross-namespace network policy exceptions not enumerated
ISS-002.detail: Default-deny network policies are specified but the plan does not list the explicit allow rules needed for Prometheus scraping across namespaces and Istio control plane communication.

history.note: NetworkPolicy exceptions missing for observability and mesh traffic

focus_issues.title: Canary rollout SLO thresholds not defined
focus_issues.detail: Flagger Canary resource requires explicit analysis.metrics thresholds; absence means canary proceeds to 100% without safety gates.

latest_recommendations: Define Flagger Canary analysis metrics with error-rate threshold <=1% and p99 latency <=800ms for all production services
Enumerate NetworkPolicy egress rules permitting Prometheus cross-namespace scraping on port 9090 and Istio pilot discovery on port 15012

previous_audits.summary: Solid infrastructure foundation with correct GitOps and service mesh choices, but canary rollout safety gates are absent and network policy exceptions are under-specified.
strengths: Flux v2 GitOps pattern is correctly scoped per namespace
Workload Identity for Key Vault access eliminates static credential exposure
Azure Defender for Containers inclusion meets SOC 2 requirements
concerns: Flagger Canary resources have no analysis metrics defined; canary will proceed unchecked
NetworkPolicy default-deny specified without listing required observability and mesh exceptions
blocking_issues: Canary rollout SLO thresholds absent - Flagger cannot make rollback decisions without error-rate and latency metric definitions
recommendations: Add Flagger analysis metrics for istio_requests_total error rate <=1% and istio_request_duration_milliseconds p99 <=800ms
Document NetworkPolicy allow rules for prometheus-operator scraping port 9090 and istiod discovery port 15012

issue_updates.title: Canary rollout SLO thresholds not defined
issue_updates.detail: Flagger Canary manifest in plan lacks analysis metrics stanza entirely

issue_updates.title: Cross-namespace network policy exceptions not enumerated
issue_updates.detail: Plan says default-deny but does not list ingress exceptions for Prometheus and Istio control plane
