project.goal: TypeScript SDK for geospatial construction site data normalisation and volume computation
project.timeline: MVP (coordinate reprojection + TIN + compareSurfaces) in 6 weeks; full feature set in 16 weeks

backend.framework: proj4 for reprojection, delaunator for triangulation
backend.api: Typed ESM exports: reprojectPoints, triangulateSurface, compareSurfaces, generateStakeoutList

security.input_validation: NaN and Infinity guard on all coordinate inputs
security.dependencies: proj4 and delaunator only; no network dependencies

data.model: SurveyPoint, InstrumentSession, ProjectDatum, SurveyedSurface, DesignSurface, VolumeComputation, StakeoutList, CoordinateTransformation TypeScript interfaces

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: TypeScript tsc, Vitest, tsup dual CJS/ESM build, npm publish on tag

open_questions.projection_library: Confirm proj4 vs @turf/projection for GNSS datum conversion accuracy

reasoner_reviews.product: The compareSurfaces method returning a signed volume difference and a cut-fill depth grid per cell is the highest-value API for earthworks progress monitoring platforms. The grid resolution parameter must be configurable so platforms can trade off spatial precision against computation time for large drone point clouds.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: delaunator for Delaunay triangulation is the right choice: it is a pure-JS implementation with no native dependencies, processes 1 million points in under 2 seconds on Node.js 20, and produces a stable triangulation for subsequent prismoidal volume computation. proj4 is the standard for coordinate reprojection in JavaScript and handles the full EPSG registry.
reasoner_reviews.security: The primary risk is a caller passing coordinate arrays containing NaN or Infinity values, which would silently propagate through triangulation and produce incorrect volume results without throwing. Input validation must guard all public API entry points. No network calls means no SSRF or injection surface.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: tsup dual CJS/ESM build is the correct tooling for a library targeting both Node.js and browser. delaunator and proj4 are both tree-shakeable; unused format modules will not appear in consumer bundles. Total bundle size with both dependencies minified is approximately 140KB, within the 200KB constraint.

backend.service_design: Public ESM API exports: reprojectPoints(points: SurveyPoint[], sourceDatum: ProjectDatum, targetDatum: ProjectDatum) -> SurveyPoint[]; triangulateSurface(points: SurveyPoint[]) -> SurveyedSurface; compareSurfaces(design: DesignSurface, surveyed: SurveyedSurface, gridResolution: number) -> VolumeComputation; generateStakeoutList(design: DesignSurface, interval: number) -> StakeoutList.
backend.business_modules: reprojection.ts: wraps proj4.defs and proj4() for EPSG-code-based coordinate transformation; validates source and target EPSG codes against proj4 registry. triangulation.ts: wraps delaunator.from() with Float64Array coordinate input; computes TIN face normals and areas; returns SurveyedSurface with triangles and bounds. volume.ts: iterates design surface grid cells; samples surveyed TIN elevation at each cell centre via barycentric interpolation; computes signed cut-fill depth; aggregates prismoidal volumes. stakeout.ts: interpolates design_surface along chainage at specified interval; returns StakeoutList with northing, easting, design_elevation per point.
backend.failure_handling: SurveySDKError base class; CoordinateValidationError(point_index, value) thrown on NaN or Infinity in input coordinates; ProjectionError(epsg_code) thrown for unknown EPSG codes; TriangulationError thrown when delaunator returns degenerate triangulation for collinear points; VolumeComputationError(cell_index) thrown when barycentric interpolation fails for a cell outside TIN bounds.
backend.backend_risks: Barycentric interpolation for cells outside the TIN convex hull extrapolates; mitigated by clamping to the nearest TIN edge point and flagging extrapolated cells in VolumeComputation.extrapolated_cells array

data.entities: SurveyPoint (id: string, northing: number, easting: number, elevation: number, instrument_session_id?: string), InstrumentSession (id: string, instrument_type: 'total_station'|'gnss_rover'|'drone_photogrammetry', session_date: string, point_count: number), ProjectDatum (epsg_code: string, name: string, geoid_model?: string), DesignSurface (id: string, points: SurveyPoint[], grid_resolution_m: number, metadata: Record<string, unknown>), SurveyedSurface (id: string, points: SurveyPoint[], triangles: number[][], bounds: BoundingBox), VolumeComputation (design_surface_id: string, surveyed_surface_id: string, grid_resolution_m: number, total_cut_m3: number, total_fill_m3: number, net_volume_m3: number, cut_fill_grid: CutFillCell[][], extrapolated_cells: number[]), StakeoutList (design_surface_id: string, interval_m: number, points: StakeoutPoint[])
data.schema_notes: All coordinate values stored as IEEE 754 double precision; grid cell dimensions in metres; volume in cubic metres. CutFillCell: {northing: number, easting: number, cut_depth_m: number, fill_depth_m: number, is_extrapolated: boolean}. StakeoutPoint extends SurveyPoint with stake_number and offset_from_alignment fields.
data.data_risks: Floating-point accumulation errors in prismoidal volume summation for large grids; mitigated by Kahan compensated summation in VolumeAccumulator class

devops.deployment_topology: npm package; no server infrastructure. GitHub repository with main branch protection.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Node.js 20 and 22 matrix. Steps: tsc --noEmit type check; Vitest run with coverage (100% for volume.ts and triangulation.ts); tsup build producing dist/index.cjs and dist/index.mjs; size-limit check against 200KB threshold; npm publish --provenance on version tag via OIDC trusted publisher.
devops.devops_risks: proj4 EPSG definition file adds approximately 60KB to bundle; consider lazy-loading extended EPSG registry for browser builds to stay within size budget

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a TypeScript npm library SDK with no hosted runtime. Key decisions: delaunator for Delaunay triangulation is pure-JS with no native dependencies and fast enough for drone point clouds; proj4 handles the full EPSG registry for datum conversion; barycentric interpolation from the TIN for cut-fill depth calculation is more accurate than bilinear grid interpolation. Bundle size constraint of 200KB requires the extended EPSG registry to be lazy-loaded. Kahan compensated summation prevents floating-point drift in prismoidal volume accumulation over large grids.
target_output.title: ConstructionSurvey TypeScript SDK for Geospatial Site Data Processing
target_output.executive_summary: A TypeScript library distributed on npm that normalises geospatial construction site data from total stations, GNSS rovers, and drone photogrammetry instruments to a project coordinate datum and provides typed methods for TIN surface triangulation, cut-fill volume computation, stake-out point generation, and progress-vs-design surface comparison. The compareSurfaces method returns a signed volume difference and a per-cell cut-fill depth grid that host applications render as heat-map overlays. No network calls, no native dependencies, and a minified bundle under 200KB. Compatible with Node.js 20 LTS and ES2022 browsers.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-package TypeScript library with tree-shakeable ESM exports. Coordinate reprojection via proj4 EPSG-registry transforms normalises heterogeneous instrument session coordinates to a single ProjectDatum. Delaunay triangulation via delaunator.from() with Float64Array input constructs a TIN SurveyedSurface from the normalised point cloud. Volume computation iterates a configurable-resolution grid over the design surface, samples the surveyed TIN elevation at each cell centre via barycentric interpolation, and accumulates signed cut-fill depths using Kahan compensated summation. StakeoutList generation interpolates design surface points at a specified chainage interval. All operations are pure functions returning new typed objects; no mutable global state. Error conditions surface as typed exceptions with point index or cell index context.
target_output.technology_stack: TypeScript 5.4 targeting ES2022 with strict mode. Runtime: Node.js 20 LTS (server) and ES2022 modern browser. Coordinate reprojection: proj4 2.12 (EPSG registry). Triangulation: delaunator 5.0 (pure-JS Delaunay). Build: tsup 8.0 producing dual CJS (dist/index.cjs) and ESM (dist/index.mjs) outputs. Type declarations: tsup --dts. Testing: Vitest 1.6 with numeric tolerance assertions. Linting: ESLint 9 with TypeScript plugin. Bundle analysis: size-limit for 200KB CI gate. npm provenance publishing via OIDC trusted publisher. No native C extension dependencies.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Coordinate Reprojection: reprojectPoints accepts SurveyPoint array with source and target ProjectDatum EPSG codes; uses proj4.defs and proj4() to transform northing/easting coordinates; validates EPSG codes against proj4 registry before transformation; returns new SurveyPoint array in target datum. TIN Triangulation: triangulateSurface accepts reprojected SurveyPoint array; feeds Float64Array of coordinates to delaunator.from(); constructs SurveyedSurface with triangle index list and bounding box. Surface Comparison: compareSurfaces accepts DesignSurface and SurveyedSurface with configurable gridResolution; generates grid cells over design surface bounds; samples surveyed TIN elevation at each cell centre via barycentric interpolation; computes cut depth where surveyed > design, fill depth where surveyed < design; returns VolumeComputation with total_cut_m3, total_fill_m3, net_volume_m3, and cut_fill_grid. Stake-out Generation: generateStakeoutList accepts DesignSurface and chainage interval; interpolates design points; returns StakeoutList with northing, easting, design_elevation, and stake number per point.
target_output.system_components: Public ESM entry point: src/index.ts exporting reprojectPoints, triangulateSurface, compareSurfaces, generateStakeoutList, and all TypeScript interfaces. Modules: reprojection.ts (proj4 wrapper with EPSG validation and NaN guard), triangulation.ts (delaunator wrapper returning SurveyedSurface with triangle face normals), volume.ts (grid iteration, barycentric TIN sampling, Kahan accumulator for cut-fill volumes), stakeout.ts (design surface interpolation at chainage intervals). Types: survey_types.ts defining SurveyPoint, InstrumentSession, ProjectDatum, SurveyedSurface, DesignSurface, VolumeComputation, StakeoutList, CutFillCell interfaces. Errors: errors.ts defining SurveySDKError, CoordinateValidationError, ProjectionError, TriangulationError, VolumeComputationError. Build output: dist/index.cjs and dist/index.mjs via tsup dual build.
target_output.workflows: Drone Point Cloud Volume Comparison: field data platform imports drone photogrammetry point cloud as SurveyPoint array with EPSG:32755 (GDA2020 / MGA zone 55) coordinates; platform calls reprojectPoints(rawPoints, {epsg_code: 'EPSG:32755'}, projectDatum) to normalise to the project local coordinate system; calls triangulateSurface(reprojectedPoints) to construct SurveyedSurface TIN from 500,000 drone points using delaunator; calls compareSurfaces(designSurface, surveyedSurface, {grid_resolution_m: 1.0}) to generate a 1m-grid cut-fill depth grid; platform renders cut_fill_grid as a heat-map overlay on the site plan with cut depths in red and fill depths in blue and displays the total net volume difference in the dashboard header. Stake-out List Generation: engineer defines a design surface for a new road embankment; platform calls generateStakeoutList(designSurface, {interval_m: 5.0}) to produce stake positions at 5m chainage intervals; StakeoutList is exported to a CSV file for loading into the GNSS rover field controller.
target_output.data_model: SurveyPoint: id string, northing number, easting number, elevation number, instrument_session_id string optional. InstrumentSession: id string, instrument_type union 'total_station'/'gnss_rover'/'drone_photogrammetry', session_date string ISO8601, point_count number. ProjectDatum: epsg_code string, name string, geoid_model string optional. DesignSurface: id string, points SurveyPoint array, grid_resolution_m number, metadata Record. SurveyedSurface: id string, points SurveyPoint array, triangles number 2D array (delaunator half-edge indices), bounds BoundingBox. VolumeComputation: design_surface_id string, surveyed_surface_id string, grid_resolution_m number, total_cut_m3 number, total_fill_m3 number, net_volume_m3 number, cut_fill_grid CutFillCell 2D array, extrapolated_cells number array. StakeoutList: design_surface_id string, interval_m number, points StakeoutPoint array. CoordinateTransformation: source_epsg string, target_epsg string, translation_vector number array 3 elements.
target_output.api_design: ESM exports from dist/index.mjs. reprojectPoints(points: SurveyPoint[], source: ProjectDatum, target: ProjectDatum): SurveyPoint[] - throws ProjectionError for unknown EPSG codes, CoordinateValidationError for NaN/Infinity values. triangulateSurface(points: SurveyPoint[]): SurveyedSurface - throws TriangulationError for fewer than 3 points or collinear input. compareSurfaces(design: DesignSurface, surveyed: SurveyedSurface, options?: {grid_resolution_m?: number}): VolumeComputation - default grid_resolution_m 1.0; throws VolumeComputationError on unrecoverable barycentric failure. generateStakeoutList(design: DesignSurface, options: {interval_m: number}): StakeoutList. All functions are pure with no side effects. TypeScript strict inference preserved through all return types.
target_output.security_and_compliance: No network calls; no authentication surface. Coordinate validation: all public entry points call validateCoordinates(points) which iterates input arrays and throws CoordinateValidationError(point_index, 'NaN northing') or CoordinateValidationError(point_index, 'Infinity easting') on invalid values before any computation proceeds. EPSG code injection: proj4.defs only accepts string EPSG codes; unknown codes throw ProjectionError without falling back to eval or dynamic code execution. No use of eval, Function constructor, or dynamic require in library code; enforced via ESLint no-eval rule. Dependency audit: npm audit run in CI; Dependabot configured for proj4 and delaunator security updates. Source maps published to npm with --sourcemap flag for debugging; no secrets or internal paths in source maps.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI matrix: Node.js 20 and 22 on ubuntu-latest. Steps: tsc --noEmit type check; ESLint; Vitest run with coverage threshold 100% for volume.ts and triangulation.ts; tsup build; size-limit check against 200KB for dist/index.mjs. Release workflow: on version tag push, build and publish to npm with --provenance via OIDC trusted publisher (no stored npm token). Changelog: keep-a-changelog updated for every PR touching public API. No server infrastructure, no containers, no deployment topology beyond npm registry.
target_output.observability: No telemetry or analytics. All error conditions surfaced via typed thrown exceptions. VolumeComputation.extrapolated_cells array identifies grid cell indices where barycentric interpolation fell outside TIN convex hull; host applications can warn users that those cells used nearest-edge extrapolation. Library emits no console.log or console.error calls; enforced via ESLint no-console rule. Vitest coverage report published as CI artifact; numeric accuracy regression tests use toBeCloseTo with 2 decimal places for volume assertions.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No hosting cost. npm install size: approximately 3MB with all dependencies. Minified bundle: approximately 140KB (proj4 ~60KB, delaunator ~15KB, library code ~65KB), within 200KB constraint. Performance: delaunator processes 500,000 drone survey points in approximately 1.8 seconds on Node.js 20; compareSurfaces on a 1m-grid for a 500m x 200m site (100,000 cells) completes in approximately 400ms. Memory footprint: SurveyedSurface for 500,000 points with triangle indices approximately 60MB Float64Array.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): ProjectDatum and SurveyPoint interfaces, reprojectPoints with proj4 EPSG validation, triangulateSurface with delaunator, compareSurfaces with 1m grid and barycentric TIN sampling, VolumeComputation with cut-fill grid, typed exception hierarchy, Vitest test suite with numeric tolerance assertions, tsup dual CJS/ESM build, npm publish pipeline. Phase 2 Stake-out and Sessions (5 weeks): generateStakeoutList with chainage interval interpolation, InstrumentSession tracking for point provenance, CoordinateTransformation audit log interface, Kahan summation for large grid volume accuracy. Phase 3 Performance and Browser (5 weeks): size-limit optimisation including lazy EPSG registry loading for browser builds, contour line generation from TIN surface, WebAssembly prototype for hot computation paths.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with no any types. All public functions must have JSDoc with parameter descriptions and thrown exception documentation. Vitest tests for volume.ts and triangulation.ts must use toBeCloseTo(expected, 2) for numeric assertions; fixture data from real GNSS and drone survey files checked into tests/fixtures/. ESLint no-eval and no-console rules enforced. tsup --dts ensures type declarations are published to npm. Semantic versioning: breaking API changes require major version bump and MIGRATION.md entry. Delaunator and proj4 version pins updated only after CI matrix validation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: barycentric interpolation for cut-fill cells outside the drone survey TIN convex hull extrapolates rather than interpolating; extrapolated cells produce unreliable volume contributions. Mitigation: flag extrapolated cells in VolumeComputation.extrapolated_cells array; host applications can mask or warn on these cells. Risk: delaunator produces degenerate triangulation for collinear or near-collinear survey point subsets in flat terrain. Mitigation: detect degenerate triangles by area threshold and throw TriangulationError with point range context. Tradeoff: pure-JS delaunator versus native CGAL bindings; JS delaunator is sufficient for site-scale point clouds and avoids native build dependencies. Tradeoff: fixed prismoidal volume method versus Simpson rule integration; prismoidal is standard in earthworks quantity surveying and matches what civil engineers expect.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: proj4 selected over @turf/projection for datum conversion because proj4 supports the full EPSG registry including local Australian GDA2020 and GDA94 datums required for construction sites. delaunator confirmed sufficient for 500,000-point drone clouds at 1.8 seconds. tsup dual CJS/ESM build confirmed tree-shakeable for host platform builds. Lazy EPSG registry loading deferred to Phase 3 to keep MVP scope focused.

metadata.notes: TypeScript SDK for geospatial construction site data; TIN triangulation, datum reprojection, and prismoidal volume computation are the core technical elements.

project_goal.value: Real-time data pipeline ingesting CCTV and wearable-badge tracking feeds from active construction sites, streaming worker location events through a message broker, populating a spatial database for automated headcount verification, zone-access enforcement, and evacuation roll-call during emergency musters
project_goal.rationale: Site safety mandate following recent near-miss incident; regulatory requirement for real-time worker tracking during blasting and confined-space operations

target_users.value: Site safety managers, evacuation coordinators, and security personnel monitoring active construction sites
target_users.rationale: Primary operational users identified in safety requirements workshops

project_class.rationale: Event ingestion, stream processing, and spatial database population; no user-facing frontend in pipeline scope

capabilities.rationale: Real-time event streaming, badge and CCTV integration, spatial queries, and batch roll-call reporting

complexity_level.rationale: 500ms end-to-end latency SLO, spatial zone containment queries, multi-source event correlation, and emergency roll-call under 90 seconds

risk_level.rationale: Worker location data is personal biometric-adjacent data; pipeline failure during emergency muster has life-safety consequences

data_sensitivity.rationale: Worker location tracking and badge identifiers constitute personal data under GDPR and local privacy legislation

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline is on-site infrastructure with no public internet exposure; badge readers and CCTV on isolated site LAN

access_model.value: Cognito with role-based access for safety managers and evacuation coordinators; pipeline components use IAM task roles
access_model.rationale: AWS-native identity for ECS Fargate tasks; Cognito for human operator access to roll-call API

feature_scope.value: Badge reader event ingestion, CCTV feed integration, Kafka event streaming, Flink stream processing with zone-permission join, PostGIS zone boundary spatial queries, zone_entry and zone_violation record creation, emergency muster roll-call generation within 90 seconds, batch nightly headcount reconciliation
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline feature set for site safety compliance

mvp_scope.value: Badge reader Kafka ingestion, Flink zone-permission join with 500ms latency SLO, PostGIS zone_entry and zone_violation writes, emergency muster roll-call endpoint
mvp_scope.rationale: Badge tracking covers 95% of on-site workers; CCTV integration deferred to Phase 2

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all event transport; Kafka SASL/SCRAM authentication; PostGIS data encrypted at rest via RDS encryption; Cognito MFA for safety manager access
security_baseline.rationale: Personal data handling requires encryption and authenticated access

frontend_stack.value: none - data pipeline; safety dashboard is a separate system outside scope
frontend_stack.rationale: Pipeline produces events and database records consumed by a separate safety dashboard application

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2 for ingestion service and roll-call API; Apache Flink 1.19 for stream processing
backend_stack.rationale: Java ecosystem has mature Flink and Kafka client libraries; Spring Boot for the roll-call REST API

data_platform.value: PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for spatial zone queries; Redis 7 for last-known worker location cache; Kafka on MSK for event streaming
data_platform.rationale: PostGIS provides native ST_Contains for zone boundary polygon containment; Redis for sub-millisecond last-known location lookups

hosting_target.value: ECS Fargate on AWS in ap-southeast-2 region; MSK for managed Kafka; RDS PostgreSQL with PostGIS in ap-southeast-2
hosting_target.rationale: AWS-first organisation; ap-southeast-2 for data residency on Australian construction sites

privacy_retention_policy.value: Worker location events retained 30 days for safety audit; zone_violation records retained 7 years for regulatory compliance; badge identifiers pseudonymised after 90 days with worker_id substitution
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimisation and Australian Privacy Act retention limits for location data

future_scope.value: CCTV computer vision zone detection, predictive zone congestion alerts, integration with on-site emergency broadcast system for automated PA announcements during muster
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 safety capability roadmap

constraints.value: Pipeline must operate with degraded badge reader connectivity (offline badge cache for 15 minutes); all data must remain in ap-southeast-2; emergency roll-call must complete within 90 seconds of muster trigger
constraints.rationale: Construction site connectivity is intermittent; life-safety SLO for evacuation

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for ECS task metrics; structured JSON logs from Flink jobs; custom metrics: badge_event_lag_ms, zone_violation_rate, muster_completion_seconds
observability_baseline.rationale: Site safety pipeline requires real-time operational visibility

execution_preference.value: Safety-first iterative delivery with site safety manager sign-off at each phase; load testing with simulated badge events before live site deployment
execution_preference.rationale: Life-safety system requires thorough validation before production deployment

compliance_context.value: Australian Privacy Act 1988 (Privacy Act) compliance required for collection and processing of worker location and biometric-adjacent data from wearable badge tracking; Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) mandatory before go-live; worker consent notices required at site onboarding explaining tracking purpose, retention periods, and access rights; zone violation records retained 7 years per Work Health and Safety Act 2011 (Cth) incident reporting obligations; badge identifiers pseudonymised after 90 days and stored in separate key table accessible only to safety managers; all data must remain in ap-southeast-2 to satisfy data sovereignty requirements; emergency roll-call system classified as safety-critical and subject to ISO 45001 occupational health and safety management requirements.
compliance_context.rationale: Personal data sensitivity combined with high risk level for a real-time worker location tracking system triggers Australian Privacy Act obligations, WHS Act regulatory retention requirements, and ISO 45001 safety-critical system duties.

project.goal: Real-time worker tracking pipeline for construction site safety and emergency muster
project.target_users: Site safety managers and evacuation coordinators
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks; CCTV integration in 20 weeks

backend.framework: Spring Boot for ingestion API and roll-call endpoint; Apache Flink 1.19 for stream processing
backend.api: REST roll-call endpoint; Kafka consumer for badge events

security.auth: Cognito with MFA for operator access; Kafka SASL/SCRAM

data.streaming: Kafka on MSK with badge-events topic

open_questions.cctv_vendor: Confirm CCTV vendor API for Phase 2 computer vision integration

reasoner_reviews.product: The emergency muster 90-second SLO is non-negotiable for life-safety compliance. Badge reader events are the primary tracking signal for MVP. Zone violation detection must alert safety managers within 500ms of the badge event crossing the zone boundary to enable real-time intervention.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Flink with a keyed stream on worker_id is the correct pattern for joining badge events with zone permission records. The PostGIS ST_Contains query for zone boundary detection should run in the Flink JDBC enrichment lookup, not in the write path, to maintain the 500ms latency SLO. Redis must cache the last-known zone per worker to avoid PostGIS read pressure during emergency muster.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR and Australian Privacy Act require explicit consent notification to workers about location tracking. Data minimisation: badge events should not store worker name or personal details beyond pseudonymised worker_id; the worker_id to name mapping must be stored in a separate access-controlled table with audit logging for every lookup.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The offline badge cache at the reader edge requires a local SQLite store on the badge reader controller. Events queued during connectivity loss must be published to Kafka in order upon reconnection. The 90-second muster SLO requires pre-computed on-site roster snapshots refreshed every 60 seconds so roll-call comparison is O(1) against the snapshot rather than a full database scan.

backend.service_design: BadgeIngestionService: Spring Boot Kafka consumer on badge-events topic with at-least-once delivery; validates event schema; enriches with zone_id via Flink lookup. RollCallService: Spring Boot REST API POST /api/v1/muster/trigger; queries Redis last-known locations; compares to registered_on_site roster; returns roll-call report.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/muster/trigger with {site_id, muster_station_id, triggered_by} returns {muster_id, worker_count, present_workers, missing_workers, completion_seconds}. GET /api/v1/muster/{muster_id}/status returns live roll-call progress.
backend.failure_handling: Flink job restart strategy: fixed-delay 3 attempts with 10-second interval; Kafka consumer group offset committed after PostGIS write confirms. Badge reader offline mode: local SQLite cache holds events for up to 15 minutes; on reconnect, events published in chronological order.
backend.backend_risks: Flink job failure during emergency muster would leave roll-call incomplete; mitigated by pre-computed roster snapshot in Redis refreshed every 60 seconds so RollCallService can serve roll-call directly from Redis cache without Flink dependency

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool for safety manager and evacuation coordinator roles; Kafka SASL/SCRAM credentials rotated monthly; Flink job IAM task role with least-privilege MSK read and RDS write permissions
security.authorization_model: Safety-manager role: read all zone violations and trigger muster. Evacuation-coordinator role: read muster roll-call only. Pipeline IAM task roles scoped to specific Kafka topics and RDS schema.
security.privacy_controls: Worker location events store only pseudonymised worker_id; name mapping in workers_registry table accessible only via audited lookup API; retention Lambda deletes events older than 30 days; badge identifiers pseudonymised to worker_id after 90 days
security.security_risks: Badge reader devices may be physically accessible on construction sites; SASL credentials stored in edge device firmware could be extracted. Mitigation: use per-device certificate-based mTLS for badge reader to Kafka connection instead of shared SASL credentials.

data.entities: site_workers, badge_devices, cctv_cameras, zone_boundaries, location_events, zone_access_rules, muster_station_events
data.storage_design: PostGIS on RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ: zone_boundaries as PostGIS POLYGON geometries; ST_Contains queries for zone membership. Redis 7 ElastiCache: HSET worker:{worker_id} zone_id last_event_at for sub-millisecond last-known location. Kafka MSK: badge-events topic with 12 partitions partitioned by site_id.
data.schema_notes: location_events.worker_id is pseudonymised; workers_registry table in separate schema with row-level security. zone_boundaries.geom indexed with GIST spatial index for ST_Contains performance.
data.retention_and_deletion: EventRetentionLambda runs nightly; deletes location_events older than 30 days; archives zone_violation records older than 1 year to S3 Glacier for 7-year retention. Pseudonymisation Lambda replaces badge_device_id with worker_id after 90 days.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: BadgeIngestionService task (2 vCPU, 4GB), FlinkJobManagerTask (4 vCPU, 8GB), FlinkTaskManagerTask x3 (4 vCPU, 8GB), RollCallService task (1 vCPU, 2GB). MSK 3-broker cluster kafka.m5.xlarge. RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.r6g.large Multi-AZ. Redis 7 cache.r6g.large.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Maven build and Surefire tests; Flink job JAR packaged and uploaded to S3; ECS task definition update via AWS CLI; blue/green deployment for RollCallService. Load test with 10,000 simulated badge events per second in staging before prod promotion.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch metrics: badge_event_lag_ms P99, zone_violation_rate per site, muster_completion_seconds. CloudWatch Alarms: badge_event_lag >500ms critical; Flink job restarts >2 per hour critical. Structured JSON logs from Flink jobs shipped to CloudWatch Logs.
devops.devops_risks: Flink job upgrade requires draining savepoint; pipeline downtime during upgrade. Mitigation: savepoint before every upgrade; maintain 15-minute badge reader offline cache to absorb upgrade window.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a real-time construction site worker tracking pipeline. The emergency muster 90-second SLO drives the Redis-first architecture: pre-computed roster snapshots mean roll-call never depends on Flink job availability. Zone violation detection must run in the Flink enrichment path before PostGIS writes to stay within 500ms badge event latency.
target_output.title: Real-Time Event Processing Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A real-time data pipeline ingesting CCTV and wearable-badge tracking events from active construction sites, streaming worker location through Kafka on MSK, processed by Apache Flink with zone-permission joins, and persisted to PostGIS on RDS PostgreSQL 16 for headcount verification and zone-access enforcement. Emergency muster roll-call compares Redis-cached last-known worker locations against the registered on-site roster within 90 seconds of a muster trigger.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven pipeline architecture on AWS in ap-southeast-2. Badge reader controllers publish RFID scan events to Kafka MSK badge-events topic (12 partitions by site_id). Flink streaming job consumes badge events, performs a keyed lookup join on worker_id against zone_access_rules in PostGIS to determine zone permission, writes zone_entry or zone_violation records to PostGIS within 500ms, and updates the Redis HSET for last-known worker location. RollCallService Spring Boot API triggers muster roll-call by comparing Redis last-known locations against the pre-computed registered_on_site roster snapshot refreshed every 60 seconds. Badge reader controllers maintain a local SQLite offline cache for up to 15 minutes during site connectivity loss, publishing buffered events in order upon reconnection.
target_output.technology_stack: Stream processing: Apache Flink 1.19 with Kafka connector and JDBC enrichment. Ingestion: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2 and Spring Kafka. Roll-call API: Spring Boot 3.2 REST. Spatial database: PostGIS 3.4 on PostgreSQL 16 via RDS Multi-AZ. Cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache. Message broker: Kafka on MSK (3-broker kafka.m5.xlarge). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate ap-southeast-2. Frontend: React 18 with TypeScript and Leaflet.js for real-time zone map.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Badge Event Ingestion: Spring Kafka consumer on badge-events topic; schema validation via Avro; enrichment with worker_id via workers_registry lookup. Zone Permission Flink Job: keyed stream on worker_id; async JDBC lookup against zone_access_rules; emits zone_entry for permitted entry and zone_violation for denied entry. Last-Known Location Cache: Flink sink updates Redis HSET worker:{worker_id} with zone_id and last_event_at after each event. Emergency Muster Roll-Call: RollCallService POST /api/v1/muster/trigger; reads all worker entries from Redis matching site_id; compares against pre-computed roster snapshot; returns present and missing worker lists. Nightly Headcount Batch: Spring Batch job reconciles daily badge events against registered_on_site roster; generates headcount discrepancy report.
target_output.system_components: API Gateway, Identity Provider, Core Processing Engine, Message Broker, Primary Database, Cache Layer, Background Worker, Monitoring Stack. Each component connects to others via standard interfaces. The processing engine handles business logic and the database stores persistent state.
target_output.workflows: Badge Zone Entry: badge reader scans worker RFID tag and publishes event to Kafka badge-events topic with worker_id, badge_device_id, site_id, zone_id, and timestamp; Flink consumer reads event within 50ms; async JDBC lookup fetches zone_access_rules for the worker_id and zone_id pair from PostGIS; if permitted, writes zone_entry record to PostGIS location_events and updates Redis HSET worker:{worker_id} to zone_id; if denied, writes zone_violation record and publishes alert to zone-violations Kafka topic for safety manager notification; end-to-end latency target 500ms from badge scan to PostGIS write. Emergency Muster Roll-Call: site safety manager triggers muster by calling POST /api/v1/muster/trigger with site_id and muster_station_id; RollCallService reads all Redis keys matching site:{site_id}:workers to obtain last-known zone for every on-site worker; compares against registered_on_site roster snapshot (refreshed every 60 seconds by a Spring Scheduled task); workers not in Redis or in non-muster zones flagged as missing; muster_station_events record written with present_count, missing_count, and missing_worker_ids; response delivered within 90 seconds.
target_output.data_model: Users (id UUID PK, name TEXT, email TEXT, role TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Events (id UUID PK, user_id FK, event_type TEXT, payload JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Products (id UUID PK, name TEXT, price DECIMAL, stock INT). Orders (id UUID PK, user_id FK, total DECIMAL, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). The primary relational data entities include Users, Orders, and Products connected via standard foreign key relationships.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/muster/trigger with body {site_id: string, muster_station_id: string, triggered_by: string} returns {muster_id: uuid, worker_count: int, present_workers: string[], missing_workers: string[], completion_seconds: float}. GET /api/v1/muster/{muster_id}/status returns live roll-call progress with percentage complete. GET /api/v1/zones/{zone_id}/occupancy returns current worker count in zone from Redis. GET /api/v1/workers/{worker_id}/location returns last-known zone_id and last_event_at from Redis. All endpoints require Cognito JWT with safety-manager or evacuation-coordinator role claim.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Personal data controls for GDPR and Australian Privacy Act: worker location events store only pseudonymised worker_id; workers_registry name mapping table in separate PostgreSQL schema with row-level security and audit logging for every SELECT. Badge reader to Kafka connection uses per-device certificate mTLS rather than shared SASL credentials to prevent credential extraction from field devices. Flink task IAM role has least-privilege MSK read, RDS write to location_events and zone_access_rules schemas only. Cognito MFA for all safety manager and evacuation coordinator accounts. TLS 1.3 for all Kafka, Redis, and RDS connections. EventRetentionLambda deletes location_events older than 30 days; zone_violation records archived to S3 Glacier for 7-year retention.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate ap-southeast-2: BadgeIngestionService 2 vCPU 4GB; FlinkJobManager 4 vCPU 8GB; 3x FlinkTaskManager 4 vCPU 8GB each; RollCallService 1 vCPU 2GB. MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.xlarge with replication factor 3. RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.r6g.large Multi-AZ with PostGIS 3.4 extension. Redis 7 ElastiCache cache.r6g.large. GitHub Actions CI: Maven build, Surefire unit tests, Flink job JAR packaged to S3, ECS task definition update via AWS CLI. Blue/green deployment for RollCallService; Flink job upgrade via savepoint drain. Load test in staging with 10,000 simulated badge events per second before prod promotion. Environments: dev (single-AZ, MSK single-broker), staging (multi-AZ mirror), prod (multi-AZ ap-southeast-2a/2b).
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics: badge_event_lag_ms P99 target <500ms; zone_violation_rate per site per hour; muster_completion_seconds target <90. CloudWatch Alarms: badge_event_lag_ms P99 >500ms for 2 minutes pages safety operations; Flink job restarts >2 per hour critical. Structured JSON logs from Flink TaskManagers shipped to CloudWatch Logs Insights. Redis keyspace notifications for worker location updates feed a CloudWatch metric stream. ECS task CPU and memory alarms for all Fargate tasks.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MSK 3-broker kafka.m5.xlarge approximately $600 per month. RDS PostgreSQL db.r6g.large Multi-AZ approximately $400 per month. ECS Fargate tasks approximately $350 per month for steady state (4 tasks). Redis cache.r6g.large approximately $180 per month. Total approximately $1,530 per month per construction site. Kafka partitioning by site_id enables horizontal scaling to additional sites without re-partitioning. Flink task managers scale to 6 under high badge event load (>5,000 events per second) via ECS service autoscaling.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): MSK Kafka cluster, BadgeIngestionService Spring Kafka consumer, Flink zone-permission join job, PostGIS zone_boundaries and location_events tables, Redis last-known location cache, RollCallService muster trigger endpoint, CloudWatch alarms for badge latency and Flink restarts, load test with simulated badge events, site safety manager UAT. Phase 2 CCTV Integration (8 weeks): CCTV vendor API integration for computer vision zone detection, CCTV camera entities in PostGIS, Flink multi-source join correlating badge and CCTV events, EventRetentionLambda for 30-day deletion. Phase 3 Advanced Safety (4 weeks): predictive zone congestion alerts via Flink windowed count, emergency PA system integration, pseudonymisation Lambda for 90-day badge_device_id replacement.
target_output.development_guidelines: Flink jobs unit tested with flink-test-utils MiniCluster; badge event processing latency tested with synthetic load. Spring Boot services: JUnit 5 with Testcontainers for PostGIS and Redis integration tests. Maven enforcer plugin prevents dependency version conflicts. Flink savepoint required before every job upgrade; savepoint procedure documented in runbook. PostGIS spatial index on zone_boundaries.geom mandatory; query plan verified with EXPLAIN ANALYZE before merge. Kafka topic configuration: badge-events topic retention 30 days, cleanup.policy=delete.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: badge reader offline cache overflow if site connectivity loss exceeds 15 minutes; events beyond 15-minute buffer are lost. Mitigation: alert site manager on connectivity loss after 5 minutes; badge reader cache capacity set to 20 minutes with warning at 12 minutes. Risk: Flink job failure during active emergency muster leaves roll-call dependent solely on Redis snapshot; Redis eviction during high memory pressure could produce incomplete roll-call. Mitigation: Redis maxmemory-policy set to noeviction for worker location keys with TTL of 8 hours. Tradeoff: Redis snapshot for muster roll-call over real-time PostGIS query avoids 90-second SLO dependency on Flink job availability. Tradeoff: PostGIS ST_Contains for zone boundary queries over in-memory geometry computation; PostGIS GIST index delivers sub-10ms zone membership for up to 10,000 concurrent workers.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Kafka on MSK selected over self-managed Kafka for managed broker patching and MSK Connect compatibility. PostGIS ST_Contains confirmed sufficient for zone boundary polygon containment at 10,000 concurrent workers with GIST index. Per-device mTLS certificates selected for badge reader authentication over shared SASL credentials. Emergency muster pre-computed roster snapshot refreshed every 60 seconds confirmed sufficient for 90-second SLO.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: compliance_context missing despite personal data and high risk; technology_stack lists React frontend for data pipeline project; data_model uses generic Users/Orders/Products instead of site_workers/badge_devices/zone_boundaries; system_components uses generic placeholder names.

project_goal.value: AI-powered system that analyses shop drawings and specification submittals against project technical specs and building code clauses, flagging non-conformances and missing mandatory items for document-control teams on large commercial building projects
project_goal.rationale: Core business need to reduce costly non-compliant material deliveries

target_users.value: Document controllers, senior engineers, and procurement leads on large commercial building projects
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in stakeholder discovery

project_class.rationale: LLM-powered compliance checking is the core differentiator

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack with AI inference and compliance analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-step OCR pipeline, LLM integration, and structured NCR workflow

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential contract documents and has ISO 9001 compliance obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Contract drawings and project specs are commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Only authenticated project team members may access

access_model.value: RBAC with roles: document_controller, senior_engineer, project_manager, admin; Azure AD B2C groups
access_model.rationale: Different roles have different override and approval permissions

feature_scope.value: PDF/DWG OCR ingestion, LLM compliance assessment against spec clauses, NCR creation and tracking, engineer review and override, subcontractor response workflow, analytics dashboard for NCR trends
feature_scope.rationale: Full document compliance lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: PDF submittal upload, OCR extraction, LLM compliance check against uploaded spec clauses, NCR creation, basic review/override UI
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to validate AI accuracy on pilot project

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest via Azure Storage Service Encryption, Azure AD B2C MFA, audit logging for all document access and NCR decisions
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential project data and ISO 9001 audit requirements

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript, PDF.js for drawing rendering
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and rich PDF viewer ecosystem

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; Celery workers for async OCR and LLM jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for OCR (pytesseract, pdfplumber) and LLM orchestration

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for relational records; Azure Blob Storage for raw document files
data_platform.rationale: Structured NCR data in Postgres, large PDF binaries in Blob Storage

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps with autoscaling; Celery workers on separate Container App instances
hosting_target.rationale: Scales OCR and LLM worker pool independently from API layer

privacy_retention_policy.value: Project documents retained for project duration plus 7 years per construction contract record-keeping requirements; audit logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Construction liability and contractual archival obligations

future_scope.value: DWG native parsing without OCR, automated response drafting to subcontractors, integration with Procore and Aconex document management
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap

constraints.value: Azure cloud only; LLM must use Azure OpenAI Service (no external OpenAI API calls); must support PDF and DWG file formats at MVP
constraints.rationale: Data sovereignty and security policy

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights; structured logging with correlation IDs per submittal; LLM token usage tracked per job
observability_baseline.rationale: Cost visibility for LLM usage and compliance with audit trail requirements

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; feature-flagged releases; pilot with one project team before full rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Allows AI accuracy validation before wide deployment

compliance_context.value: ISO 9001 document control requirements; NCC/BCA building code compliance obligations; internal IT Security policy mandating Azure OpenAI only
compliance_context.rationale: ISO 9001 certification requires traceable NCR records; building code compliance is the core product use case

llm_integration.value: Azure OpenAI Service GPT-4o for compliance assessment; structured output mode for JSON NCR payloads; spec clause chunks retrieved via Azure AI Search vector index
llm_integration.rationale: Azure OpenAI satisfies data sovereignty; structured output reduces post-processing errors

project.goal: Automate compliance checking of shop drawings and submittals against project specs
project.target_users: Document controllers and senior engineers on commercial building projects

open_questions.dwg_parsing: DWG native parsing deferred post-MVP; OCR fallback for MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope is tightly defined around the core AI value proposition: upload a submittal PDF, run an LLM compliance check, create an NCR. Deferring DWG native parsing and Procore integration is correct for a pilot. The engineer review-and-override workflow is essential for building trust in AI output before wider rollout.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Separating OCR and LLM processing into Celery workers decoupled from the FastAPI tier is the right call given multi-minute processing times for large drawing sets. Azure AI Search as a vector store for spec clause retrieval avoids building a custom RAG layer. The async job pattern with polling endpoints keeps the frontend responsive during long inference runs.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD B2C with MFA is appropriate for confidential project data. All documents must be stored in Azure Blob with private access only; no public CDN URLs. The LLM must receive only the minimum necessary spec clause context, not full project documents, to limit data exposure in API calls. NCR audit trails must be append-only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure-only constraint is firm. Azure OpenAI Service GPT-4o structured output mode reduces JSON parsing failures significantly. OCR quality on scanned PDFs may degrade AI accuracy; a confidence score flag on low-quality OCR is needed.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan is thin on LLM prompt engineering detail and lacks a defined fallback when OCR confidence is below threshold. The data model for storing extracted text alongside the original PDF is under-specified. Performance targets for the 2-minute compliance check SLA are not articulated.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting; PDF.js viewer component for drawing annotation overlay
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login, Project Dashboard, Submittal Upload, Compliance Review (with NCR panel), NCR Tracker, Analytics Dashboard
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state (submittal status polling, NCR lists); Zustand for viewer annotation state
frontend.frontend_risks: PDF.js performance on large A0-format drawing PDFs may cause browser memory pressure; lazy loading and page-by-page rendering required

backend.service_design: FastAPI app for synchronous CRUD and job submission; Celery worker pool for OCR extraction and LLM inference tasks with separate queues
backend.api_patterns: POST /submittals (upload and enqueue), GET /submittals/{id}/status (poll job), GET /submittals/{id}/assessment (fetch results), POST /ncrs (create NCR), PATCH /ncrs/{id}/decision (engineer review)
backend.llm_integration_design: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o called with system prompt containing project spec context (top-k chunks from Azure AI Search), submittal extracted text, and structured JSON output schema for NCR fields; max 4 LLM calls per submittal split by trade section
backend.backend_risks: LLM token limits may be exceeded for large submittal packages; chunking strategy must split by drawing sheet rather than arbitrary token windows

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C with MSAL React for frontend token acquisition; FastAPI JWT validation against B2C JWKS endpoint
security.authorization_model: RBAC: document_controller can upload and view, senior_engineer can override NCRs, project_manager can close NCRs, admin manages project config
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only ncr_audit_log table records every status transition with user_id, timestamp, and decision rationale; Azure Monitor captures all Blob access events

data.entities: submittal_documents (id, project_id, blob_path, ocr_status, ocr_confidence, submitted_by, created_at), spec_clause_chunks (id, project_id, clause_ref, content, embedding_vector), non_conformance_reports (id, submittal_id, clause_ref, severity, status, ai_rationale, engineer_decision), ncr_audit_log (id, ncr_id, user_id, action, prior_status, new_status, timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL for relational records; Azure Blob Storage private container per project for PDF/DWG files; Azure AI Search index per project for spec clause embeddings
data.data_risks: OCR text extraction quality varies by scan resolution; low-confidence extractions must be flagged and excluded from LLM context to avoid hallucinated compliance findings

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps: api (2-4 replicas, autoscale on HTTP queue), celery-worker (1-8 replicas, autoscale on Celery queue depth), celery-beat (1 replica for scheduled tasks)
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: lint (ruff, mypy), pytest (80% coverage gate), Docker build, push to ACR, deploy to staging Container App, smoke test, promote to production
devops.observability_stack: Application Insights for distributed traces; custom metrics: jobs_enqueued, ocr_processing_time_s, llm_inference_time_s, ncr_creation_rate; Azure Monitor alerts on Celery queue depth > 50

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an AI compliance checking system on Azure with Azure OpenAI GPT-4o and Azure AI Search for RAG over spec clauses. The weak quality target means plan sections are serviceable but 2-3 are thinner than ideal - specifically the data model and phased implementation lack the depth they should have. Keeping OCR and LLM workers in separate Celery queues isolates failure domains. Azure-only constraint is respected throughout.
target_output.title: Construction Submittal Compliance AI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An AI-powered compliance checking system for construction document controllers built on Azure Container Apps with a React 18 frontend, FastAPI backend, and Azure OpenAI GPT-4o for LLM-based assessment. Shop drawings and specification submittals are ingested via OCR, matched against vectorised spec clause chunks in Azure AI Search, and assessed for non-conformances with results stored as traceable NCR records in PostgreSQL.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with a React 18 SPA communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI REST API on Azure Container Apps. Long-running OCR and LLM inference jobs are offloaded to a Celery worker pool also running on Container Apps, consuming tasks from a Redis queue. Azure Blob Storage holds raw PDF and DWG uploads per project. Azure AI Search stores vector embeddings of specification clause chunks for RAG retrieval. Azure AD B2C handles authentication with MFA. All NCR state transitions are recorded in an append-only audit log for ISO 9001 traceability.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, PDF.js, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery, pdfplumber for OCR, Azure OpenAI SDK. Database: PostgreSQL 16 for relational records. Vector index: Azure AI Search. Blob storage: Azure Blob Storage with private containers. Auth: Azure AD B2C with MSAL. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor with Application Insights. Container registry: Azure Container Registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Submittal Ingestion: PDF/DWG upload to Blob Storage with project and trade categorisation. OCR Pipeline: pdfplumber text extraction with confidence scoring, flagging low-resolution scanned pages. Spec Clause Indexing: admin uploads project spec PDFs, chunked and embedded into Azure AI Search index. LLM Compliance Assessment: GPT-4o structured-output call with top-k spec clause context, returning JSON NCR list with clause references and severity. NCR Review Workflow: senior engineers accept, reject, or override AI findings with mandatory rationale. Analytics: NCR rate by trade, clause hit frequency, mean resolution time.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps (api service, celery-worker service, celery-beat service), Azure Cache for Redis (Celery broker and result backend), PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, Azure Blob Storage (per-project private containers for PDFs and DWGs), Azure AI Search (per-project spec clause vector index), Azure OpenAI Service (GPT-4o deployment), Azure AD B2C (user pool and JWKS endpoint), Azure Container Registry (Docker image storage), Application Insights (APM and distributed traces).
target_output.workflows: Submittal Upload and Assessment: Document controller uploads a shop drawing PDF; FastAPI endpoint streams the file to Azure Blob, creates a submittal_documents record with status pending_ocr, and enqueues an OCR task. Celery worker extracts text with pdfplumber, records confidence per page, then enqueues an LLM task. LLM worker retrieves top-8 spec clause chunks from Azure AI Search matching the submittal trade category, calls GPT-4o with structured output schema, and writes non_conformance_reports rows. Controller is notified and opens the compliance report in the PDF viewer. NCR Review: A senior engineer opens a flagged NCR, views the AI rationale and cited clause, then selects Accept (creates formal NCR), Reject (dismisses finding with rationale logged), or Override Severity. Every decision writes to ncr_audit_log with timestamp and user_id.
target_output.data_model: submittal_documents (id UUID PK, project_id FK, blob_path, file_type ENUM pdf/dwg, trade_category, ocr_status ENUM pending/processing/complete/failed, ocr_confidence_avg FLOAT, submitted_by_user_id FK, created_at). spec_clause_chunks (id UUID PK, project_id FK, document_title, clause_ref, content TEXT, embedding VECTOR). non_conformance_reports (id UUID PK, submittal_id FK, clause_ref, severity ENUM critical/major/minor, ai_confidence FLOAT, ai_rationale, status ENUM ai_flagged/accepted/rejected/overridden, engineer_decision, decided_by_user_id FK, decided_at). ncr_audit_log (id UUID PK, ncr_id FK, user_id FK, action, prior_status, new_status, rationale, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/submittals (upload PDF to Blob, enqueue OCR job, return submittal_id). GET /api/v1/submittals/{id}/status (poll OCR and LLM job status). GET /api/v1/submittals/{id}/assessment (fetch NCR list with clause refs). POST /api/v1/ncrs (create formal NCR from AI finding). PATCH /api/v1/ncrs/{id}/decision (engineer accept/reject/override with rationale). GET /api/v1/projects/{id}/analytics (NCR rate and trend data). All endpoints require Azure AD B2C JWT; RBAC enforced at handler level.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD B2C MFA enforced for all roles. RBAC: document_controller (upload, view), senior_engineer (NCR override), project_manager (NCR close), admin (project config, spec upload). TLS 1.3 in transit. Azure Blob Storage private containers; all URLs are time-limited SAS tokens (1-hour expiry). Azure Storage Service Encryption AES-256 at rest. Append-only ncr_audit_log for ISO 9001 traceability. Azure OpenAI API calls include only necessary spec clause context, not full project documents. Application Insights audit trail for all Blob access events.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps: api (min 2 replicas, max 4, scale on concurrent HTTP requests > 20), celery-worker (min 1, max 8, scale on Redis queue depth > 10), celery-beat (1 replica, no scaling). Azure DevOps Pipeline stages: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push to ACR, deploy to staging Container App, Postman smoke test, manual approval gate, deploy to production. Database migrations via Alembic run as one-off Container Apps jobs before each deploy. Secrets stored in Azure Key Vault, injected as Container Apps environment secrets. Rolling deploy with health check probe on /health endpoint.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights SDK in FastAPI for distributed traces with correlation IDs propagated through Celery task headers. Custom metrics: submittal_ingestion_count, ocr_processing_time_seconds (histogram), llm_inference_time_seconds (histogram), ncr_creation_rate, llm_token_usage_per_job. Azure Monitor alert rules: Celery queue depth > 50 (PagerDuty), API p95 latency > 5s, OCR error rate > 5%, LLM call failure rate > 2%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Primary cost drivers: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o token usage (estimated $0.03-0.10 per submittal depending on spec clause count) and Celery worker compute. Celery worker autoscaling keeps idle cost low. Azure AI Search standard tier for vector search. Budget target $800/month for 500 submittals per month. Cost monitoring via Azure Cost Management with monthly budget alerts.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): PDF upload and OCR pipeline, spec clause upload and Azure AI Search indexing, GPT-4o compliance assessment with structured NCR output, engineer review and override UI, basic NCR list view, Azure AD B2C auth, CI/CD pipeline to staging. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Analytics dashboard with NCR rate trends, subcontractor response workflow, email notifications for NCR assignment, improved OCR confidence flagging. Phase 3 (4 weeks): DWG native parsing via ezdxf, Procore document management webhook integration, bulk submittal batch processing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type annotations enforced with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting and formatting. 80% test coverage gate on FastAPI endpoints and Celery task handlers. Celery tasks must be idempotent. All LLM prompt templates version-controlled in the repo. Feature flags via environment variables for new AI model deployments. PR requires one senior approval before merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o hallucinations producing false NCRs damage user trust. Mitigation: confidence scoring, mandatory engineer review for all AI findings, accuracy metrics tracked per project. Risk: OCR quality on scanned PDFs produces garbled text fed to LLM. Mitigation: confidence threshold gate (< 0.7 triggers manual review flag) before LLM call. Tradeoff: Azure AI Search over self-hosted pgvector for easier ops and Azure ecosystem integration at higher per-query cost. Tradeoff: Celery over Azure Service Bus for simpler local dev experience with Redis reuse.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Azure AI Search over pgvector for managed scaling and Azure native integration. Confirmed GPT-4o structured output mode over function calling for simpler NCR JSON extraction. DWG native parsing deferred to Phase 3; OCR fallback acceptable for MVP pilot. Spec clause chunking strategy: 500-token chunks with 50-token overlap, split at clause boundaries.
