project.target_users: Estimating software vendors and QS platforms

backend.framework: Chi router with custom middleware chain

security.auth: API key in Authorization header; JWT claims for premium tier
security.rate_limiting: 100 req/min free tier, 1000 req/min premium tier, token bucket in Redis

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with versioned pricing tables
data.cache: Redis 7 on ElastiCache, 1-hour TTL for cost index responses

constraints.immutability: No UPDATE or DELETE on published price_versions or child rows

confirmed_decisions.versioning: Each monthly update creates a new price_version record
confirmed_decisions.cache_invalidation: Redis keys flushed by version pattern on new version publish

reasoner_reviews.product: The immutable versioning constraint is critical for estimating workflows - quantity surveyors must reproduce tender pricing from months past. The tiered API key model suits the subscription revenue strategy well and avoids over-engineering OAuth2 for M2M integrations.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Chi is appropriate for this read-heavy, latency-sensitive API. The Redis cache-aside pattern keyed by trade_code + region_code + version_id will serve the majority of queries without hitting PostgreSQL. Consider partitioning cost_indices by revision_year to contain query scan ranges as the version table grows.
reasoner_reviews.security: API key hashing before storage is correct. Rate limiting must be evaluated before Redis lookup to prevent cache stampede from a single abusive key. The admin ingestion endpoint must be IP-restricted at the security group level, not just application middleware.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Immutable versioning is the right design but will require explicit query filters for 'latest version' to avoid table scans. An indexed is_latest boolean on price_versions with a partial index will make current-pricing queries fast without scanning all historical records.

backend.service_design: Single Go binary with domain packages: pricing (query handlers for indices, labour rates, price factors), ingestion (admin batch import from publisher CSVs), auth (API key validation middleware), analytics (usage event aggregation)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1; all routes prefixed /v1/; responses include X-Data-Version and X-Rate-Limit-Remaining headers; errors follow RFC 7807 problem+json
backend.background_jobs: Monthly ingestion job reads publisher CSV from S3, validates trade codes and regions, inserts new price_version record and child cost_indices rows in a transaction, flushes Redis keys matching version pattern
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on PostgreSQL reads via sqlx; Redis miss falls through to DB; degraded mode returns last cached response with Stale-Content-Warning header and 203 status

security.auth_design: API key stored as SHA-256 hash in api_subscribers table; key validated in Chi middleware before handler dispatch; premium tier receives JWT with tier claim
security.abuse_prevention: Token bucket rate limiting implemented in Redis with INCR/EXPIRE; keys triggering >5 consecutive 4xx errors per minute flagged for review; AWS WAF on ALB for IP-level blocking of repeated violation IPs
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured request logs include key_id, endpoint, trade_code, region_code, price_version_id, response_ms, status_code; shipped to CloudWatch Logs; no pricing values logged to prevent data leakage in log systems

data.entities: cost_indices (id UUID, trade_code VARCHAR(20), region_code VARCHAR(10), unit VARCHAR(30), price_version_id UUID FK, value NUMERIC(12,4), currency CHAR(3), UNIQUE(trade_code, region_code, price_version_id)), labour_rates (id UUID, trade_code, region_code, award_classification VARCHAR(50), hourly_rate NUMERIC(10,4), price_version_id UUID FK), regional_price_factors (id UUID, region_code, trade_code, factor_multiplier NUMERIC(8,6), price_version_id UUID FK), price_versions (id UUID, revision_label VARCHAR(20), published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_latest BOOLEAN), api_subscribers (id UUID, email VARCHAR(254), key_hash CHAR(64), tier ENUM free/premium, is_active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL with composite unique constraints on pricing tables; partial index on price_versions WHERE is_latest=TRUE; Redis keys as v1:ci:{trade}:{region}:{version_id}
data.retention_and_deletion: Price version rows never deleted or updated; subscriber API keys soft-deleted on cancellation; request log entries purged from CloudWatch after 12 months

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service behind ALB with WAF in ap-southeast-2; minimum 2 tasks across 2 AZs; auto-scaling policy on ALB RequestCountPerTarget > 500 req/min up to 8 tasks
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test ./... with -race flag, go build, docker build, push to ECR, ECS rolling update with 100% minimum healthy; migration step runs alembic equivalent via ECS one-off task
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch custom metrics namespace ANZ/CostAPI: cache_hit_rate, pricing_query_p99_ms, ingestion_duration_seconds, rate_limit_rejections_per_min; CloudWatch Alarms on p99>200ms and error_rate>2%

target_output.thinking_summary: Read-heavy public pricing API where data immutability and version consistency are paramount. Go/Chi chosen for low-latency throughput; Redis cache-aside keyed by version prevents stale pricing responses; PostgreSQL versioned records preserve historical estimate reproducibility. Weak plan quality means some sections are deliberately thin.
target_output.title: ANZ Construction Cost Index API
target_output.executive_summary: A public REST API built in Go 1.22 on AWS ECS Fargate that serves versioned Australian and New Zealand construction material cost indices, labour rates, and regional price factors to estimating software platforms. API key authentication with tiered rate limits gates access, while a monthly ingestion pipeline imports publisher data feeds and creates immutable version records preserving full pricing history.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-service Go API behind an AWS ALB with WAF, deployed on ECS Fargate in ap-southeast-2. Chi router dispatches pricing, ingestion, and analytics handlers. PostgreSQL 16 stores versioned cost data in append-only price_version records with child tables for indices, labour rates, and regional factors. Redis 7 provides a cache-aside layer for hot cost index queries, keys scoped to version_id to eliminate stale response risk. API keys are validated in Chi middleware before handler dispatch. CloudWatch collects structured request logs and service metrics for the operator dashboard.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Chi router, pgx/v5 PostgreSQL driver, go-redis v9, AWS SDK v2 for S3 and Secrets Manager. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ in ap-southeast-2. Cache: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with AWS WAF, ECR, Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint and docker build pipeline.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Cost Index Query: GET /v1/cost-indices by trade_code and region_code returning current or version-pinned rates. Labour Rate Query: GET /v1/labour-rates by trade and region returning award classification rates. Regional Price Factor: GET /v1/price-factors for location multipliers. Version Listing: GET /v1/versions showing published revisions. Usage Analytics: GET /v1/usage for premium subscribers. Admin Ingestion: internal endpoint triggered by monthly batch job.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF filtering malformed query parameters, ECS Fargate tasks running the Go service, ElastiCache Redis for cost index caching, RDS PostgreSQL for versioned pricing tables, S3 bucket for inbound publisher CSV feeds, Secrets Manager for DB credentials and API key hash salt, CloudWatch Logs and Metrics for observability.
target_output.workflows: Pricing Query: Client sends GET /v1/cost-indices?trade=CONC&region=SYD with Authorization header; Chi middleware validates key hash against api_subscribers; handler checks Redis for key v1:ci:CONC:SYD:{latest_version_id}; on cache miss queries PostgreSQL cost_indices join price_versions WHERE is_latest=TRUE; stores result in Redis with 3600s TTL; returns JSON body with X-Data-Version header. Monthly Ingestion: Admin triggers ECS one-off task; job reads publisher CSV from S3; validates trade codes and regions against lookup tables; opens PostgreSQL transaction, inserts new price_versions row with is_latest=TRUE, flips previous is_latest to FALSE, inserts child cost_indices and labour_rates rows; commits; flushes all Redis keys containing the previous version_id; publishes SNS notification to webhook subscriber Lambda.
target_output.data_model: cost_indices (id UUID PK, trade_code VARCHAR(20), region_code VARCHAR(10), unit VARCHAR(30), price_version_id UUID FK REFERENCES price_versions, value NUMERIC(12,4), currency CHAR(3), effective_date DATE, UNIQUE(trade_code, region_code, price_version_id)). labour_rates (id UUID PK, trade_code, region_code, award_classification VARCHAR(50), hourly_rate NUMERIC(10,4), price_version_id UUID FK). regional_price_factors (id UUID PK, region_code, trade_code, factor_multiplier NUMERIC(8,6), price_version_id UUID FK). price_versions (id UUID PK, revision_label VARCHAR(20), published_at TIMESTAMPTZ, is_latest BOOLEAN, partial index WHERE is_latest=TRUE). api_subscribers (id UUID PK, email VARCHAR(254), key_hash CHAR(64), tier VARCHAR(10), is_active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/cost-indices?trade={code}&region={code}&version={id} - 200 JSON array of matching cost index records or 404 if trade/region unknown. GET /v1/labour-rates?trade={code}&region={code} - 200 array of award classification rates. GET /v1/price-factors?region={code} - 200 array of trade multipliers for region. GET /v1/versions - 200 array of all price_versions sorted by published_at desc. GET /v1/usage?from={date}&to={date} - premium tier only, 403 for free tier. Auth: Authorization: ApiKey {key} header on all endpoints. Errors: 401 missing/invalid key, 429 rate limit, 422 invalid trade/region code.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API keys stored as SHA-256 hashes with a per-instance salt from Secrets Manager; plaintext never logged or stored. Token bucket rate limiting in Redis: 100 req/min free, 1000 req/min premium; burst window of 10 seconds. AWS WAF on ALB blocks query string injection patterns. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB listener. Admin ingestion endpoint restricted by EC2 security group to GitHub Actions NAT IP ranges only. Australian Privacy Act: subscriber email collection minimised; logs purged after 12 months.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service in ap-southeast-2 with ALB target group spanning ap-southeast-2a and ap-southeast-2b; minimum 2 tasks, auto-scale on RequestCountPerTarget > 500 req/min up to 8 tasks. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: golangci-lint checks, go test ./... with -race, docker build, push to ECR with commit SHA tag, ECS rolling update with 100% minimum healthy. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ. ElastiCache single-node Redis for MVP. Secrets Manager for DB credentials with 30-day automatic rotation. Schema migrations applied via ECS one-off task before service rollout.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics namespace ANZ/CostAPI: cache_hit_rate (target >85%), pricing_query_p99_ms (SLO <50ms), ingestion_duration_seconds, rate_limit_rejections_per_min. Structured JSON request logs include key_id, trade_code, region_code, version_id, status_code, response_ms. CloudWatch Alarms: p99 > 200ms and error_rate > 2% both alert to PagerDuty.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $280-350/month: 2 Fargate 0.5vCPU tasks, RDS db.t3.small Multi-AZ, ElastiCache cache.t3.micro, ALB. Cache hit rate target of 85% keeps RDS under minimal load. Auto-scaling adds tasks proportionally to subscriber growth without over-provisioning at low traffic.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): PostgreSQL schema with versioned pricing tables, API key auth middleware, cost index and labour rate query endpoints, Redis caching, monthly ingestion CLI, OpenAPI 3.1 spec, GitHub Actions CI/CD. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Regional price factors endpoint, premium JWT tier, usage analytics endpoint, CloudWatch dashboards and alarms. Phase 3 (future): GraphQL endpoint, webhook subscriptions for version events, bulk export endpoint.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum committed. golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck enabled in CI. Table-driven tests for all query handlers. Integration tests via testcontainers-go spinning up PostgreSQL and Redis. No UPDATE or DELETE statements permitted on price_versions, cost_indices, or labour_rates tables by application code.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Publisher CSV format changes silently break ingestion. Mitigation: strict schema validation before any DB write, alerting on any parse failure before transaction begins. Risk: Redis cache returning previous version after failed key flush. Mitigation: version-keyed cache entries mean stale keys are simply not requested once clients upgrade to the new version_id. Tradeoff: single-region for data residency simplicity at the cost of no multi-region failover in MVP.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected API key over OAuth2 for simpler M2M vendor integration. Chose Chi over Gin to minimise dependency footprint. Regional price factors modelled as separate table to allow independent update cycles from cost indices. GraphQL deferred to v2 to meet initial delivery timeline.

metadata.notes: ANZ construction pricing API with versioned immutable cost data; weak plan with deliberately thin sections in system_components and cost_and_scaling.

project_goal.value: A Rust crate that parses AGS 4 format borehole data files into typed Rust structs for each soil group and test result, with WASM compilation so web-based site investigation platforms can process borehole logs client-side without a server round-trip
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates bespoke per-platform AGS parsers across the geotechnical software ecosystem; WASM target enables browser-side processing

target_users.value: Rust and TypeScript developers building site investigation platforms, geotechnical data management tools, and BIM-integrated ground model viewers
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are software developers integrating the library, not end-user geotechnicians

project_class.rationale: Distributed as Rust crate on crates.io and WASM npm package; no hosted service

capabilities.rationale: Pure library: parsing logic and typed data structures; no frontend or hosted backend

complexity_level.rationale: AGS 4 group schema parsing with validation and WASM compilation target adds meaningful complexity beyond a trivial CSV parser

risk_level.rationale: No hosted service, no user authentication, no sensitive data storage; library processes files provided by the calling application

data_sensitivity.rationale: Library itself stores no data; AGS file contents are the calling application's responsibility

external_exposure.rationale: Published on crates.io and npm as public open-source packages

access_model.value: No authentication; public package download from crates.io and npm registry
access_model.rationale: Open-source library with no access restrictions

feature_scope.value: AGS 4 group header and data row parsing, typed Rust structs for SAMP, GRAG, ISPT, TREG, TRIG, ATCH, WSTB groups, structured ValidationReport for non-conformant files, serde JSON serialisation, WASM bindings via wasm-bindgen
feature_scope.rationale: Core AGS groups required by geotechnical lab result consumers and site investigation platforms

mvp_scope.value: Parse SAMP, GRAG, ISPT groups with full validation and typed structs; WASM build producing npm-compatible package; ValidationReport with group, row, and field-level error details
mvp_scope.rationale: Three most-used AGS groups cover the majority of borehole log use cases

security_baseline.value: No unsafe Rust blocks in parsing logic; fuzz testing via cargo-fuzz for malformed AGS input; supply chain audit via cargo-audit in CI
security_baseline.rationale: Library accepts untrusted file input; memory safety and supply-chain integrity are the relevant security controls

frontend_stack.value: Not applicable - library_sdk with no UI component
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend; WASM bindings expose typed JS/TS interface for host application consumption

backend_stack.value: Rust (stable toolchain), wasm-bindgen for WASM targets, serde and serde_json for serialisation
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides memory safety and WASM compilation without runtime overhead

data_platform.value: No persistent storage; in-memory data structures only
data_platform.rationale: Library parses and returns structured data; persistence is the host application's responsibility

hosting_target.value: crates.io for Rust crate, npm registry for WASM package via wasm-pack
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Rust and JS package distribution channels

privacy_retention_policy.value: Library retains no data; no privacy obligations apply to the library itself
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No hosted service, no data storage, no user interaction

future_scope.value: Additional AGS groups (CONG, CONS, DREM, LPDN), streaming parser for large files, Python bindings via PyO3
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP expansion to cover full AGS 4 group catalogue and additional language targets

constraints.value: WASM bundle must be under 500 KB gzipped; no std::io dependencies in WASM path; stable Rust toolchain only
constraints.rationale: Browser WASM size budget; WASM environment lacks filesystem and network access; stability requirement for downstream crate consumers

observability_baseline.value: CI test coverage report via cargo-tarpaulin; benchmark suite via criterion for parse throughput on representative AGS files
observability_baseline.rationale: Library quality measured by test coverage and parse performance rather than runtime observability

execution_preference.value: Rust crate release cycle with semver versioning; GitHub Actions for CI; CHANGELOG maintained per release
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source library release discipline

project.goal: AGS 4 borehole data parsing library with WASM compilation for browser-side use

security.memory_safety: no unsafe blocks in parser logic
security.fuzz: cargo-fuzz corpus for malformed AGS input

data.serialisation: serde Serialize/Deserialize on all public types

devops.publishing: cargo publish to crates.io, wasm-pack publish to npm

constraints.no_std_io: WASM build must use no_std compatible I/O

open_questions.streaming_parser: Large file streaming deferred to post-MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP group selection (SAMP, GRAG, ISPT) covers soil sampling, grading, and SPT blow counts which are the most queried groups in geotechnical reporting workflows. ValidationReport error granularity to row and field level is essential for developer adoption; vague parse errors cause integrators to abandon the library.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The no_std compatible I/O constraint for the WASM path requires careful layering: file I/O should be handled through a trait abstraction so the native Rust target uses std::io::Read while the WASM target accepts a byte slice or JS ArrayBuffer. wasm-bindgen with serde-wasm-bindgen for complex type passing is preferable to manual JsValue conversion for the ValidationReport and parsed group structs.
reasoner_reviews.security: cargo-fuzz corpus should include deliberately malformed AGS files: truncated group headers, invalid UNIT rows, non-UTF-8 bytes, and excessively long field values. cargo-audit should run on every PR not only on main merges. No unsafe blocks policy should be enforced via a clippy deny(unsafe_code) attribute in lib.rs.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous round plan was thin on the WASM build pipeline specifics and did not describe how the npm package would be structured for TypeScript consumers. wasm-pack generates JS glue code that TypeScript projects import; the plan should describe the generated TypeScript type definitions and how the ValidationReport maps to TypeScript interfaces.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The crate must compile to WASM32 target with no_std for browser-based AGS file parsing — file I/O must be abstracted behind a trait to allow WASM consumers to pass byte slices directly. Minimum supported Rust edition is 2021; MSRV is 1.74 to align with the geotechnical tooling ecosystem. Crate must not take any dependencies beyond the Rust standard library to keep the WASM binary under 200KB gzipped.

backend.service_design: Single Rust library crate (ags4-rs) with modules: parser (AGS 4 tokeniser and group router), groups (typed structs per AGS group with serde derives), validation (ValidationReport builder, AGS 4 rule checker), wasm (wasm-bindgen entry points, JS-friendly error types). No binary target; library only.
backend.api_patterns: Public Rust API: parse(input: &[u8]) -> Result<AgsDocument, AgsError>; validate(doc: &AgsDocument) -> ValidationReport. WASM API: parseAgs(buffer: Uint8Array) -> AgsParseResult (JS object with groups and validation fields). All public types derive serde Serialize/Deserialize and wasm_bindgen where applicable.
backend.failure_handling: AgsError enum with variants: InvalidEncoding, MissingGroupHeader{group}, UnknownGroup{name}, DataRowMismatch{group, row, expected_fields, actual_fields}, UnsupportedVersion{found}. ValidationReport accumulates all validation violations rather than failing on first error, enabling integrators to display a complete error list to end users.
backend.backend_risks: serde-wasm-bindgen version alignment with wasm-bindgen is fragile; pin exact versions in Cargo.toml. WASM bundle size may exceed 500 KB if serde_json is included; evaluate miniserde or custom serialisation for WASM-only path.

security.auth_design: No authentication; library processes caller-supplied byte slices
security.secrets_management: No secrets; CI publishing uses crates.io API token and npm token stored as GitHub Actions secrets
security.abuse_prevention: cargo-fuzz harness for parse() entry point with AFL++ corpus covering malformed AGS input; cargo-audit on every PR checks for known CVEs in dependency tree
security.security_risks: Dependency supply chain risk from transitive serde dependencies. Mitigation: cargo-deny for license and advisory checking in CI; pin major versions.

data.entities: AgsDocument (groups: HashMap<String, AgsGroup>), AgsGroup (name: String, headings: Vec<String>, units: Vec<String>, types: Vec<AgsDataType>, rows: Vec<AgsRow>), AgsRow (fields: Vec<Option<String>>), typed group structs: BoreholeHeader (borehole_headers), SoilSample (soil_samples), SptResult (spt_results), TriaxialTest (triaxial_tests), AtterbergLimits (atterberg_limits), ParticleSizeDistribution (particle_size_distributions), GroundwaterReading (groundwater_readings), ValidationReport (violations: Vec<ValidationViolation>), ValidationViolation (group, row_index, field, message, severity).
data.schema_notes: AGS 4 defines fixed group-level schemas; library encodes each supported group schema as a Rust struct with Option<T> fields for optional AGS columns. Unknown groups are parsed as generic AgsGroup structs so the document is never silently dropped.
data.data_risks: AGS files from different lab software vendors have inconsistent whitespace and encoding; parser must handle BOM-prefixed UTF-8 and trailing CR+LF without panicking. Large AGS files with tens of thousands of rows may cause memory pressure in WASM environment with 4 GB heap limit.

devops.deployment_topology: No deployed infrastructure; artifacts published to crates.io and npm
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --deny warnings --deny unsafe_code, cargo test --all-features, cargo-tarpaulin coverage (80% gate), criterion benchmarks on representative 5000-row AGS file, wasm-pack build --target bundler, wasm-pack build --target nodejs, cargo-fuzz run parse_fuzz -- -max_total_time=60 on PR to main, cargo publish on version tag, wasm-pack publish on version tag.
devops.devops_risks: crates.io publish is irreversible; yanked versions remain visible. Enforce version bump check in CI to prevent accidental republish of existing version.

target_output.title: AGS4 Borehole Parser Rust Crate - Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Rust library crate (ags4-rs) that parses AGS 4 format geotechnical borehole files into typed Rust structs for soil sample groups including SAMP, GRAG, ISPT, TREG, TRIG, ATCH, and WSTB. Compiled to WASM via wasm-pack and published as an npm package so TypeScript-based site investigation platforms can parse borehole logs client-side. ValidationReport collects all group, row, and field-level violations without early termination so integrators can surface complete error lists.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Rust library crate with #![deny(unsafe_code)] at crate root. Internal module tree: parser (AGS 4 tokeniser consuming &[u8] slices via a no_std-compatible reader trait), groups (typed structs for SAMP, GRAG, ISPT and other supported groups with serde derives), validation (ValidationReport builder implementing AGS 4 rule checks), wasm (wasm-bindgen entry points wrapping typed Rust results in JS-compatible representations). Native Rust target uses std::io::Read via the reader trait; WASM target uses a no_std byte-slice implementation of the same trait so no filesystem dependency enters the WASM build.
target_output.technology_stack: Rust stable toolchain. Core dependencies: serde 1.0 with serde_json, wasm-bindgen 0.2, serde-wasm-bindgen for complex type passing to JS. Dev dependencies: criterion for benchmarks, cargo-fuzz for fuzzing harness. Build tooling: wasm-pack 0.12, cargo-tarpaulin for coverage, cargo-audit for CVE scanning, cargo-deny for license and advisory policy. Published to: crates.io (Rust native), npm registry (WASM bundler and nodejs packages).
target_output.functional_feature_map: AGS 4 tokeniser: reads GROUP, HEADING, UNIT, TYPE, and DATA rows; routes each group to its typed handler or generic AgsGroup fallback. Typed group parsers: SoilSample (SAMP group), SoilGrading (GRAG), SptResult (ISPT), TriaxialTest (TREG/TRIG), AtterbergLimits (ATCH), GroundwaterReading (WSTB). ValidationReport engine: validates presence of mandatory headings per group, unit row cardinality, data type coercions, and AGS version header. WASM bindings: parseAgs(buffer: Uint8Array) exported function returning JS object with groups dict and validation array. serde JSON round-trip for all public types.
target_output.system_components: ags4-rs Rust crate on crates.io. ags4-wasm npm package built from bundler target (for Webpack/Vite consumers) and nodejs target (for server-side Node.js use). GitHub Actions CI runner with rust-toolchain.toml pinning stable channel. cargo-fuzz harness binary targeting parse() entry point.
target_output.workflows: Native parse workflow: calling application reads AGS file bytes, passes &[u8] to parse(), receives Result<AgsDocument, AgsError>. On Ok, application iterates doc.groups to extract typed SAMP or ISPT structs. On Err, application inspects AgsError variant for structured error details. WASM workflow: JavaScript host imports parseAgs from ags4-wasm npm package, calls parseAgs(new Uint8Array(fileBuffer)), receives {groups: {SAMP: [...], ISPT: [...]}, validation: [{group, row_index, field, message, severity}, ...]}. TypeScript consumers use generated ags4-wasm.d.ts type definitions; AgsParseResult and ValidationViolation interfaces are emitted by wasm-bindgen TypeScript output mode.
target_output.data_model: AgsDocument: groups HashMap<String, AgsGroup>. AgsGroup: name String, headings Vec<String>, units Vec<String>, types Vec<AgsDataType>, rows Vec<AgsRow>. AgsRow: fields Vec<Option<String>>. Typed structs: borehole_headers (BoreholeHeader with hole_id, location, ground_level, depth), soil_samples (SoilSample with sample_ref, depth_top, depth_base, sample_type, description), spt_results (SptResult with sample_ref, depth, blow_count_30mm INT[6], total_blows INT), triaxial_tests (TriaxialTest with sample_ref, cell_pressure, effective_cohesion, friction_angle), atterberg_limits (AtterbergLimits with sample_ref, liquid_limit, plastic_limit, plasticity_index), particle_size_distributions (ParticleSizeDistribution with sample_ref, gravel_pct, sand_pct, silt_pct, clay_pct), groundwater_readings (GroundwaterReading with hole_id, reading_date, depth_to_water). ValidationReport: violations Vec<ValidationViolation> where each has group String, row_index Option<usize>, field Option<String>, message String, severity ENUM error/warning.
target_output.api_design: Rust public API: fn parse(input: &[u8]) -> Result<AgsDocument, AgsError>; fn validate(doc: &AgsDocument) -> ValidationReport; typed accessor methods on AgsDocument: doc.samp() -> Vec<SoilSample>, doc.ispt() -> Vec<SptResult>, doc.grag() -> Vec<SoilGrading>. WASM/TypeScript API exported from ags4-wasm: parseAgs(buffer: Uint8Array): AgsParseResult where AgsParseResult = {groups: {[groupName: string]: object[]}, validation: ValidationViolation[]}; ValidationViolation = {group: string, row_index: number | null, field: string | null, message: string, severity: 'error' | 'warning'}. Generated d.ts bundled in npm package via wasm-bindgen --typescript flag.
target_output.security_and_compliance: #![deny(unsafe_code)] attribute at lib.rs crate root prevents any unsafe block at compile time. cargo clippy -- --deny unsafe_code run in CI as additional gate. cargo-fuzz harness exercises parse() with AFL++ corpus including: truncated files, invalid GROUP header capitalisation, non-UTF-8 bytes at various positions, DATA rows with wrong field count, and files exceeding 100 MB. cargo-audit runs on every PR checking Rust advisory database. cargo-deny enforces license allowlist (MIT, Apache-2.0) and blocks dependencies with known advisories.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline on every PR and main push: rust-toolchain.toml enforces stable channel, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- --deny warnings --deny unsafe_code, cargo test --all-features, cargo-tarpaulin --fail-under 80, criterion benchmark run with regression alert if p50 parse throughput drops >10% vs baseline, cargo-audit --deny warnings, cargo-deny check, cargo-fuzz run parse_fuzz -- -max_total_time=60. Release pipeline on version tag: cargo publish to crates.io, wasm-pack build --target bundler --out-dir pkg/bundler, wasm-pack build --target nodejs --out-dir pkg/nodejs, publish both as separate npm package tags (ags4-wasm and ags4-wasm-node). npm package structure: pkg/bundler/ags4_wasm_bg.wasm, pkg/bundler/ags4_wasm.js, pkg/bundler/ags4_wasm.d.ts; consumers import {parseAgs} from 'ags4-wasm'.
target_output.observability: cargo-tarpaulin HTML report published as GitHub Actions artifact on each CI run; 80% line coverage gate blocks merge. criterion benchmark results stored as JSON artifact; regression detection via critcmp on PR runs comparing against main branch baseline. cargo-audit summary printed in CI log with advisory counts; --deny warnings fails the build on new CVEs. No runtime observability for library; telemetry is the host application's responsibility.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost; crates.io and npm registry are free for open-source packages. GitHub Actions free tier covers expected CI minutes for a library project. At large AGS file sizes (>50 MB), WASM 4 GB heap limit becomes relevant; document recommended chunking approach in README for files above 10 MB.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): SAMP, GRAG, ISPT group parsers, ValidationReport with group and row level errors, WASM bundler target package, GitHub Actions CI with coverage and fuzz gates, crates.io and npm publish on tag. Phase 2 (6 weeks): TREG, TRIG, ATCH, WSTB group parsers, field-level validation detail, nodejs WASM target, Python bindings via PyO3. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Streaming parser for files >10 MB using nom streaming combinators, CONG and CONS groups for consolidation test data.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public types must derive serde Serialize/Deserialize and implement Debug. New AGS group support requires: typed struct in groups module, parser registration in group router, validation rules in validation module, unit tests with a real AGS 4 sample file containing that group, and criterion benchmark entry. No unsafe blocks enforced by compiler attribute. CHANGELOG updated on every release following Keep a Changelog format. Semver: breaking changes to public API bump major version.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: wasm-bindgen and serde-wasm-bindgen version skew breaks compilation. Mitigation: pin exact versions in Cargo.toml and include lock file in repo. Risk: AGS files from some lab software vendors include non-standard whitespace or BOM prefixes causing parse failures. Mitigation: fuzz corpus includes BOM-prefixed inputs; parser strips leading BOM before tokenising. Tradeoff: serde_json in WASM path increases bundle size; evaluate miniserde or custom serialisation if bundle exceeds 500 KB threshold during CI wasm-pack build step.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved WASM target modes: bundler for browser/Vite consumers, nodejs for server-side Node.js. Resolved TypeScript type generation: wasm-bindgen --typescript mode emits d.ts; bundled in npm package. Resolved no-unsafe enforcement: #![deny(unsafe_code)] in lib.rs plus --deny unsafe_code in clippy CI invocation. Streaming parser for large files deferred to Phase 3 pending user demand confirmation.

metadata.notes: AGS4 borehole parser Rust crate revision addressing WASM pipeline and unsafe enforcement gaps; some non-critical sections remain thin consistent with weak quality.

project_goal.value: Cross-platform desktop app replacing legacy Fortran stormwater routing program with graphical node-link editor, HEC-RAS integration, and results viewer for hydrographs and flood extents
project_goal.rationale: Replace unmaintainable Fortran codebase with modern native UI while preserving solver accuracy

target_users.value: Hydraulic engineers performing urban drainage network modelling and flood impact assessments
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in stakeholder interviews

project_class.rationale: Local-only computation, no cloud hosting required, native cross-platform distribution

capabilities.rationale: UI layer, Rust core logic, SQLite local storage - no auth or public API needed

complexity_level.rationale: HEC-RAS subprocess integration, spatial canvas rendering, complex hydrological computation pipeline

risk_level.rationale: Internal tooling with no public exposure; incorrect hydraulic results could influence design decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Drainage network models are internal engineering data, not public or regulated

external_exposure.rationale: Fully offline desktop application, no network endpoints

access_model.value: Local license file validation; no per-user auth; Windows LDAP for domain-joined machines optionally
access_model.rationale: Offline-first tool used by individual engineers on workstations

feature_scope.value: Graphical node-link drainage network editor, HEC-RAS solver integration via subprocess, hydrograph and flood-extent result visualisation, scenario comparison, CSV survey data import
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope derived from replacing legacy Fortran tool capabilities

mvp_scope.value: Network editor with conduit/node placement, single HEC-RAS run execution, hydrograph viewer, and SQLite project save/load
mvp_scope.rationale: Core hydraulic workflow required for initial pilot with drainage team

security_baseline.value: Local license file validation, Sentry crash reporting with no PII, no remote code execution vectors
security_baseline.rationale: Offline app; primary security concern is license integrity and crash telemetry privacy

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2 with React 18 and TypeScript; Konva.js for canvas network editor
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native window with small bundle; Konva enables performant 2D canvas rendering

backend_stack.value: Rust (Tauri core) for IPC and file I/O; Python 3.12 subprocess for HEC-RAS execution and hydrological calculations
backend_stack.rationale: Rust handles native performance; Python wraps existing HEC-RAS tooling via subprocess

data_platform.value: SQLite via rusqlite for local project storage; CSV import for survey data
data_platform.rationale: Fully local, no server required; SQLite sufficient for project file persistence

hosting_target.value: Cross-platform installer via Tauri bundler (NSIS for Windows, DMG for macOS)
hosting_target.rationale: Signed installers distributed via internal file server and direct download

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; Sentry crash reports stripped of user paths before transmission; local project files retained per user discretion
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Offline desktop tool processes no personal data

future_scope.value: Cloud project sync, collaborative multi-user editing, integration with GIS layers for spatial flood extent visualisation
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap items pending cloud infrastructure decisions

constraints.value: Must bundle HEC-RAS 6.x; Windows primary target, macOS secondary; no internet dependency for core computation
constraints.rationale: HEC-RAS is the mandated solver; firm has mixed Windows/macOS engineering workstations

observability_baseline.value: Sentry desktop SDK for crash reporting; local structured log file per session; no remote APM
observability_baseline.rationale: Offline app cannot use cloud APM; crash reports sufficient for defect triage

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with engineer feedback loops; GitHub Actions CI for cross-platform builds on each PR
execution_preference.rationale: Small team; fast feedback on HEC-RAS integration correctness essential

project.goal: Replace legacy Fortran stormwater tool with cross-platform desktop app

data.file_formats: HEC-RAS .prj/.g##/.u## project files, CSV survey import

open_questions.hecras_path: Engineer-configurable HEC-RAS install path or bundled?

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope correctly prioritises the core hydraulic workflow: draw a network, run HEC-RAS, view hydrographs. Scenario comparison is a high-value differentiator from the legacy Fortran tool and should remain in Phase 1 scope rather than being deferred. The survey CSV import is essential for real-world use since engineers always start from field data.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri with Rust IPC is appropriate for a desktop-native app that needs low overhead. The key integration risk is the HEC-RAS subprocess: the app must manage process lifecycle, capture stdout for progress streaming, and parse .hdf output files reliably. The previous plan did not specify how HEC-RAS install path resolution works or how computation errors are surfaced to the React UI.
reasoner_reviews.security: License file validation must resist trivial tampering via HMAC-signed license payloads validated at startup. Sentry integration must scrub local file paths from stack traces before transmitting. Code-signing for both Windows (EV certificate) and macOS (notarisation) is required to prevent Gatekeeper and SmartScreen blocks on install.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Bundling HEC-RAS 6.x is the largest distribution constraint: the installer will be 300-500 MB. Consider a two-stage install where HEC-RAS is a separate download. macOS notarisation lead time must be factored into release schedules.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan was unclear on Python subprocess lifecycle management and gave no detail on how hydrograph data is parsed from HEC-RAS .hdf output files into TypeScript-renderable datasets. The data_model section listed generic entities and missed conduit_elements and design_storms. These gaps were raised as ISS-001 and ISS-002.

backend.service_design: Tauri command handlers in Rust: open_project, save_project, run_simulation, import_survey_csv, get_hydrograph_data. Python subprocess manager wraps HEC-RAS CLI, spawns process with configured executable path, streams stdout lines to Tauri event emitter for progress display, and parses .hdf output files into hydrograph JSON on completion.
backend.api_patterns: Tauri IPC commands exposed to React frontend via invoke(). Simulation progress streamed via emit() events. No HTTP API; all IPC is in-process.
backend.failure_handling: HEC-RAS subprocess exit code != 0 surfaces error message from stderr to UI; timeout kills process after 30 min; corrupted output files trigger typed ComputationError with parse context.
backend.backend_risks: HEC-RAS output format changes between patch versions could break parser; Python subprocess startup latency on macOS; SQLite WAL mode required to prevent lock contention during long simulation runs.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri React app with route-based panels: NetworkEditor (Konva canvas), ResultsViewer (hydrograph charts), ScenarioManager, ProjectSettings.
frontend.pages_and_flows: NetworkEditor: drag-to-place conduit_elements and catchment_nodes, property panel for pipe diameter/slope/roughness; ResultsViewer: select node or conduit, view time-series hydrograph with peak annotation; ScenarioCompare: overlay two result sets on shared time axis.
frontend.performance_notes: Konva canvas virtualises off-screen elements for networks with 200+ nodes. Hydrograph data downsampled to 2000 points max before rendering to prevent canvas jank. HEC-RAS runs in subprocess so UI thread remains responsive.
frontend.frontend_risks: Konva performance degrades with very large networks (1000+ elements); needs virtualisation layer. React re-render on every canvas drag event must be debounced.

security.auth_design: HMAC-SHA256 signed license file validated at startup; license payload contains machine fingerprint hash, expiry date, and permitted feature flags. App exits with diagnostic message if license is invalid or expired.
security.secrets_management: No secrets stored; HEC-RAS install path stored in platform-native config dir (AppData on Windows, ~/Library on macOS).
security.security_risks: License bypass via binary patching is a risk for commercial distribution; obfuscation and code-signing reduce but do not eliminate this.

data.entities: drainage_networks (id INTEGER PK, project_id, name TEXT, epsg_code INTEGER, created_at). conduit_elements (id INTEGER PK, network_id FK, label TEXT, from_node_id FK, to_node_id FK, conduit_type ENUM pipe/culvert/open_channel, diameter_mm REAL, slope_pct REAL, roughness_n REAL). catchment_nodes (id INTEGER PK, network_id FK, label TEXT, node_type ENUM junction/outfall/storage, catchment_area_ha REAL, tc_min REAL). design_storms (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, label TEXT, return_period_yr INTEGER, duration_min INTEGER, peak_intensity_mmhr REAL). hydrographs (id INTEGER PK, scenario_id FK, node_label TEXT, time_steps_json TEXT, flow_m3s_json TEXT, peak_flow_m3s REAL). model_scenarios (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, name TEXT, hecras_project_dir TEXT, storm_id FK, created_at).
data.storage_design: SQLite database per project file; engineer opens/saves .hdm project files which are SQLite databases renamed with custom extension. HEC-RAS input/output files stored in sibling directory keyed by scenario ID.
data.data_risks: SQLite file corruption if HEC-RAS subprocess writes to the same directory during concurrent edits; mitigated by locking project file during simulation runs.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix: ubuntu-latest for unit tests only, windows-latest and macos-latest for installer builds. Tauri bundler produces NSIS .exe and .dmg with code signing via stored secrets.
devops.ci_cd_design: On PR: cargo test, pytest for Python HEC-RAS parser, vitest for React components, Tauri build dry-run on Windows. On tag: full signed build, upload artifacts to GitHub Release.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous installer version retained on GitHub Releases; engineers can downgrade by re-running prior installer. No auto-update mechanism in MVP.
devops.devops_risks: macOS notarisation requires Apple Developer account with active subscription; EV certificate procurement for Windows SmartScreen requires 3-5 day lead time.

previous_plan.title: Urban Drainage Desktop Application Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A cross-platform desktop application built with Tauri, React 18, and a Rust/Python backend replacing a legacy Fortran stormwater tool. Provides a graphical node-link editor for drainage networks and launches HEC-RAS simulations, displaying results in an interactive hydrograph viewer.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 desktop app with React 18 frontend communicating to Rust backend via IPC. Python 3.12 subprocess handles HEC-RAS execution. SQLite stores project data locally.
previous_plan.data_model: drainage_networks, model_scenarios, hydrographs (partial -- missing conduit_elements, design_storms).
previous_plan.workflows: Engineer draws network nodes and conduits on Konva canvas, saves project to SQLite, triggers HEC-RAS run via toolbar button.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a flawed revision round for a hydraulic desktop app. ISS-001 (HEC-RAS subprocess) is addressed but incompletely: output parsing pipeline is described but the specific Python HDF5 library dependency is not named, leaving an implementation gap. ISS-002 is resolved with full entity definitions. The intentional flaw: the technology_stack and observability sections reference Datadog APM which contradicts the local_only external_exposure contract -- the plan should use Sentry desktop SDK per contract but mistakenly includes Datadog.

target_output.title: Urban Drainage Desktop Application Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A cross-platform Tauri 2 desktop application replacing a legacy Fortran stormwater routing tool for hydraulic engineers. React 18 provides the graphical drainage network editor (Konva.js canvas) and results viewer; a Rust Tauri backend manages project persistence in SQLite and orchestrates HEC-RAS 6.x via Python 3.12 subprocess with stdout-streamed progress and .hdf output parsing into hydrograph datasets. Engineers model urban catchment drainage networks, run design storm simulations, and compare hydrographs across scenarios without any network dependency.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-layer desktop architecture: React 18 UI layer running in a Tauri WebView communicates with a Rust backend via Tauri IPC commands. The Rust layer handles all file I/O, SQLite project persistence via rusqlite, and subprocess lifecycle management. HEC-RAS 6.x is invoked as a child process by a Python 3.12 script that constructs HEC-RAS project files from SQLite model data, launches the HEC-RAS CLI, streams stdout progress lines back to Rust via pipe, and parses the .hdf results file into a JSON hydrograph payload forwarded to the React results viewer. Konva.js renders the node-link drainage network canvas with virtualised element culling for large networks. Sentry desktop SDK captures crash reports with local file path scrubbing before transmission.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Konva.js 9 for canvas, Recharts for hydrograph time-series, Zustand for network model state, Tauri 2 WebView host. Backend: Rust (Tauri commands via #[tauri::command]), rusqlite 0.31 for SQLite, serde_json for IPC serialisation; Python 3.12 subprocess for HEC-RAS orchestration and .hdf output parsing. Database: SQLite 3.45 per-project file with WAL mode. Build: Tauri bundler, GitHub Actions matrix (windows-latest, macos-latest), NSIS installer for Windows, DMG for macOS. Observability: Datadog APM agent (FLAW: incompatible with local_only desktop context; Sentry desktop SDK is the contract-compliant tool).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Drainage Network Editor: place and connect conduit_elements (pipes, culverts, open channels) and catchment_nodes on Konva canvas; assign HEC-RAS geometry parameters via side-panel property forms. Design Storm Configuration: select return period and temporal pattern for each design_storms record. Simulation Runner: invoke HEC-RAS via Python subprocess with real-time stdout progress bar; surface computation errors from HEC-RAS error log. Hydrograph Viewer: render time-series flow curves per node/conduit from parsed .hdf output; annotate peak flow and time-to-peak. Scenario Comparison: overlay hydrographs from two model_scenarios on a shared time axis to visualise peak flow attenuation.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2 WebView shell (cross-platform native window, menu, file dialogs), React 18 SPA (network editor, results viewer, scenario manager panels), Rust IPC command layer (open_project, save_project, run_simulation, import_survey_csv, get_hydrograph_data), Python 3.12 subprocess manager (HEC-RAS project file builder, CLI launcher, stdout streamer, .hdf parser), SQLite project database (WAL mode, one file per project), HEC-RAS 6.x executable (bundled or path-configured), Sentry Desktop SDK (crash capture with path scrubbing), Konva.js canvas engine (virtualised 2D network rendering).
target_output.workflows: Drainage Network Modelling: Engineer opens or creates a project file (SQLite .hdm), places catchment_nodes and conduit_elements on the Konva canvas using the toolbar, sets pipe diameter, slope, and Manning roughness via property panel, then saves -- Rust serialises the full network to SQLite. Simulation Execution: Engineer selects a design_storms entry and clicks Run; Rust invokes the Python subprocess which reads conduit/node data from SQLite, generates HEC-RAS geometry and flow files, launches the HEC-RAS CLI, streams each stdout progress line to React via Tauri emit('simulation-progress', line), waits for exit, then parses the .hdf results file into hydrograph_payload JSON and returns it to the Rust command handler which stores it in the hydrographs table and resolves the IPC promise. Scenario Comparison: Engineer saves the current run as a named scenario, loads a second scenario, and opens the ScenarioCompare panel which fetches both hydrograph datasets from SQLite and renders them overlaid on a shared Recharts time axis with peak-flow annotations.
target_output.data_model: drainage_networks (id INTEGER PK, project_id, name TEXT, epsg_code INTEGER, created_at). conduit_elements (id INTEGER PK, network_id FK, label TEXT, from_node_id FK, to_node_id FK, conduit_type ENUM pipe/culvert/open_channel, diameter_mm REAL, width_mm REAL, length_m REAL, slope_pct REAL, roughness_n REAL). catchment_nodes (id INTEGER PK, network_id FK, label TEXT, node_type ENUM junction/outfall/storage, x_canvas REAL, y_canvas REAL, catchment_area_ha REAL, tc_min REAL, invert_rl_m REAL). design_storms (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, label TEXT, return_period_yr INTEGER, duration_min INTEGER, temporal_pattern_id TEXT, peak_intensity_mmhr REAL). hydrographs (id INTEGER PK, scenario_id FK, node_label TEXT, time_steps_json TEXT, flow_m3s_json TEXT, peak_flow_m3s REAL, time_to_peak_min REAL). model_scenarios (id INTEGER PK, project_id FK, name TEXT, hecras_project_dir TEXT, storm_id FK, created_at, notes TEXT). flood_extents (id INTEGER PK, scenario_id FK, wse_grid_json TEXT, max_depth_m REAL, affected_area_ha REAL).
target_output.api_design: Tauri IPC commands (invoke() from React): open_project(path: string) -> ProjectMeta; save_project() -> void; import_survey_csv(path: string) -> SurveyImportResult {node_count, warning_count}; run_simulation(scenario_id: number, storm_id: number) -> SimulationResult {hydrograph_payload | error_detail}; get_hydrograph_data(scenario_id: number) -> HydrographDataset[]; compare_scenarios(a_id: number, b_id: number) -> ScenarioComparison. Tauri events during simulation: simulation-progress (stdout line string), simulation-complete (result payload), simulation-error (error message).
target_output.security_and_compliance: License validation: HMAC-SHA256 signed license file read at startup; payload contains machine fingerprint (MAC + volume serial hash), expiry date, and feature flags; app exits with user-facing error if signature invalid or machine fingerprint mismatch. Code signing: Windows NSIS installer signed with EV certificate (removes SmartScreen warning); macOS DMG signed and notarised via Apple Developer ID (required for Gatekeeper). Crash reporting privacy: Sentry Rust SDK configured with beforeSend hook that redacts absolute file paths from stack frames before transmission. No network sockets opened by the app; HEC-RAS subprocess runs as child process of the same user session.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: on PR, matrix runs cargo test (Rust), pytest (Python HEC-RAS parser unit tests), vitest (React components), and tauri build debug dry-run on windows-latest and macos-latest runners. On version tag push: full release workflow signs Windows installer using secrets.WINDOWS_CERT_P12 and macOS build using secrets.APPLE_CERTIFICATE via Tauri Action, uploads artifacts to GitHub Release. Installer size target under 450 MB including bundled HEC-RAS. Engineers install via NSIS wizard (Windows) or drag-to-Applications DMG (macOS). No auto-update in MVP; engineers check GitHub Releases for new versions. Rollback by re-running prior version installer from GitHub Releases.
target_output.observability: Sentry Rust SDK captures panics and unhandled errors with stack traces (file paths scrubbed). Local session log written to platform log directory (AppData/Local on Windows, ~/Library/Logs on macOS) in structured JSON: one entry per user action, simulation run, and IPC command with duration_ms. Log retained for 30 days by file rotation. Note: technology_stack section erroneously references Datadog APM -- this is a contract violation as Datadog APM requires network egress incompatible with local_only external_exposure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure costs. GitHub Actions minutes for CI: approximately 60 minutes per release build across two matrix targets. Code signing certificates: Windows EV certificate approximately USD 300/year; Apple Developer Program USD 99/year. Sentry free tier sufficient for crash volume from a small engineering team. SQLite handles projects with 500+ conduit elements within acceptable query latency; no horizontal scaling concern.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Tauri project scaffold with React 18, Konva canvas network editor with conduit and node placement and property panel, SQLite project save/load, Python subprocess HEC-RAS runner with stdout streaming, hydrograph viewer with Recharts, signed Windows installer. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Design storm library, scenario save/load, scenario comparison overlay panel, survey CSV import for catchment_nodes from total station data, macOS DMG with notarisation. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Flood extent grid visualisation, PDF report export with embedded hydrograph SVGs, batch run of multiple design_storms scenarios.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust code follows Clippy lint rules enforced in CI with zero warnings policy. Python HEC-RAS parser has 90% pytest coverage for all supported .hdf output versions. React components tested with Vitest and Testing Library. TypeScript strict mode enforced. IPC command signatures versioned -- breaking changes require a new command name. HEC-RAS output parser isolated in its own Python module with typed dataclasses to contain format-change risk.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HEC-RAS .hdf output format changes between patch versions breaking the Python parser. Mitigation: parser versioned against HEC-RAS minor version, unit-tested against captured output files from each supported version. Risk: Konva canvas performance degradation with large networks (500+ conduit elements). Mitigation: virtual culling renders only elements within viewport; network density limit documented. Tradeoff: bundling HEC-RAS produces large installers (400-450 MB) vs engineer-managed HEC-RAS installs requiring path configuration UI -- bundled chosen for simplicity with path-override setting retained.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: HEC-RAS bundled in installer by default with engineer-override path setting for managed deployments. Output format .hdf preferred over legacy .dss for all HEC-RAS 6.x runs. Hydrograph downsampling to 2000 points for React rendering confirmed as acceptable resolution. macOS notarisation handled by Tauri Action in GitHub Actions release workflow.

metadata.notes: Flawed: technology_stack and observability sections incorrectly reference Datadog APM which is incompatible with local_only external_exposure; Sentry desktop SDK is the contract-compliant tool.

project_goal.value: Migrate construction project cost and progress data from legacy Oracle ERP into a Snowflake cloud data warehouse, unifying actuals, commitments, and budget revisions across dozens of legacy project codes into a standardised cost-reporting schema for executive dashboards and auditor packages
project_goal.rationale: Replace brittle Oracle reports and manual data extracts with a governed, auditable data warehouse

target_users.value: CFOs, finance controllers, project directors, and external auditors consuming executive cost dashboards and audit packages
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of the unified cost reporting layer

project_class.rationale: ETL/ELT pipeline with no end-user frontend; outputs feed BI tools

capabilities.rationale: ELT pipeline with nightly batch jobs, Oracle integration, and Snowflake analytics layer

complexity_level.rationale: Legacy Oracle schema complexity, multi-project code reconciliation, and SOX controls add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: SOX financial reporting controls and external audit access requirements

data_sensitivity.rationale: Contains project budgets, committed costs, actual expenditures, and variance data

external_exposure.rationale: Data warehouse access restricted to internal authenticated users via Okta SSO

access_model.value: Okta SSO for Snowflake role-based access; read-only roles for auditors, read-write for data engineers
access_model.rationale: SOX requires segregation of duties between pipeline operators and data consumers

feature_scope.value: Nightly Oracle ERP extraction, dbt transformation to unified cost schema, Snowflake reporting layer, data quality reconciliation gates, executive dashboard feeds, auditor-accessible read-only views
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end pipeline from Oracle source to BI consumption

mvp_scope.value: Nightly extraction of actuals and commitments from Oracle, dbt transformation to project_budgets and actual_expenditures marts, reconciliation against Oracle control totals
mvp_scope.rationale: Priority is replacing the manual finance extract process before next audit cycle

security_baseline.value: Snowflake network policy restricting access to corporate VPN IP ranges, column-level masking on project_budget amounts for non-finance roles, Airflow secret management via AWS Secrets Manager
security_baseline.rationale: SOX and financial data protection controls

frontend_stack.value: none - pipeline only; dashboards served by Tableau connected to Snowflake
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom frontend; BI tooling connects directly to Snowflake views

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with dbt-core and Apache Airflow 2.9
backend_stack.rationale: dbt for transformation logic and lineage, Airflow for scheduling and dependency management

data_platform.value: Snowflake (Data Cloud) as target warehouse; Oracle 19c as source via JDBC
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake chosen for elastic compute, time-travel for audit, and Okta SSO integration

hosting_target.value: EC2 for Airflow scheduler and workers, Snowflake managed compute, AWS S3 for landing zone
hosting_target.rationale: Airflow on EC2 for Oracle JDBC connectivity; Snowflake manages its own compute

privacy_retention_policy.value: Financial records retained 7 years per accounting standards; raw Oracle extracts in S3 purged after 90 days once loaded to Snowflake
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Statutory financial record retention requirements

future_scope.value: Real-time cost streaming via Oracle GoldenGate CDC, automated variance commentary using LLM, contractor self-service cost query portal
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap to reduce reporting lag from nightly to near-real-time

constraints.value: Oracle ERP is read-only; no schema changes permitted to source system; pipeline must not impact Oracle production performance during business hours
constraints.rationale: Oracle is shared with live operational users; extract window is 02:00-05:00 AEST

observability_baseline.value: Monte Carlo data observability for Snowflake table freshness and row count anomalies; Airflow built-in task logging; PagerDuty for DAG failure alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Data quality monitoring is as important as pipeline uptime for financial reporting

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints; dbt models promoted through dev/staging/prod Snowflake environments
execution_preference.rationale: Environment promotion aligns with SOX change management requirements

compliance_context.value: SOX financial reporting controls with external audit access; Oracle extract audit trail required; Snowflake time-travel used for point-in-time audit reproductions
compliance_context.rationale: SOX and financial data sensitivity mandate comprehensive audit controls

project.goal: Replace manual Oracle cost extracts with governed Snowflake data warehouse
project.target_users: Finance controllers, project directors, external auditors

security.auth: Okta SSO for Snowflake; Airflow LDAP integration
security.secrets: AWS Secrets Manager for Oracle JDBC credentials and Snowflake service account key
security.network: Snowflake network policy: corporate VPN IP range only

data.landing: S3 bucket for raw CSV extracts with 90-day lifecycle

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for dbt and Airflow DAG deployments
devops.hosting: EC2 m5.large for Airflow, Snowflake managed

reasoner_reviews.product: The reconciliation gate against Oracle control totals is the most critical feature for audit credibility - without it, the warehouse will not be accepted by external auditors. Dashboard consumers need a clear data freshness indicator showing when the last successful load completed.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: dbt with Snowflake is a well-matched stack. The extraction approach via JDBC during the maintenance window is correct but needs explicit retry logic in the Airflow DAG for transient Oracle connection failures. The S3 landing zone adds a useful recovery point if a Snowflake load fails mid-run.
reasoner_reviews.security: ISS-001 is a blocker: Snowflake column-level masking policies for budget amounts are not yet defined for the non-finance auditor role. ISS-002: the Airflow EC2 instance needs instance profile IAM role for S3 and Secrets Manager access rather than static credentials in environment variables.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 02:00-05:00 extract window is tight for a full Oracle delta extract of 5 years of cost history in the initial load. Recommend a backfill DAG with configurable date ranges to complete the historical load separately from the nightly delta DAG.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG set: oracle_extract_dag (nightly delta), snowflake_load_dag (triggered on extract success), dbt_transform_dag (triggered on load success), reconciliation_dag (validates actuals totals against Oracle control sums)
backend.business_modules: dbt project structure: staging models (stg_oracle_cost_transactions, stg_oracle_project_codes), intermediate models (int_unified_cost_codes), mart models (fct_actual_expenditures, fct_cost_commitments, dim_project_hierarchy, dim_cost_codes)
backend.background_jobs: Nightly extract job runs oracle_extract_dag at 02:00 AEST; backfill job is manually triggered with start_date/end_date params for historical loads
backend.failure_handling: Airflow DAG with retry=3 and retry_delay=5min for Oracle extract tasks; reconciliation failure halts dbt_transform_dag via XCom gate; PagerDuty alert on any DAG failure

security.auth_design: Okta SSO configured as Snowflake identity provider; Snowflake roles: DATA_ENGINEER (read-write raw and staging schemas), FINANCE_ANALYST (read marts), AUDITOR (read marts with masked budget columns)
security.secrets_management: Oracle JDBC URL, username, and password in AWS Secrets Manager; Snowflake private key for service account in Secrets Manager; Airflow connections reference Secrets Manager backend, no plaintext in Airflow Variables
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Airflow task instance logs to CloudWatch; Snowflake QUERY_HISTORY and ACCESS_HISTORY views retained 365 days for SOX audit; dbt run results stored in S3 for lineage audit trail

data.entities: project_budgets (project_code, wbs_level, approved_budget, revised_budget, reporting_period), cost_commitments (commitment_id, project_code, cost_code, vendor, committed_amount, commitment_date, status), actual_expenditures (transaction_id, project_code, cost_code, amount, transaction_date, gl_account, oracle_source_id), budget_revisions (revision_id, project_code, revision_date, prior_budget, new_budget, approver), cost_codes (code, description, trade, category), project_hierarchies (project_code, programme, portfolio, region)
data.storage_design: Snowflake three-layer architecture: RAW schema (raw CSV data from S3 via Snowpipe or COPY INTO), STAGING schema (dbt stg_ models), MARTS schema (dbt fct_ and dim_ models consumed by Tableau)
data.consistency_notes: Reconciliation dbt test compares SUM(actual_expenditures.amount) per project_code against Oracle control total extracted into raw.oracle_control_totals; any variance > $1 fails the dbt test and blocks mart promotion

devops.deployment_topology: EC2 m5.large running Airflow in ap-southeast-2 behind VPC, no public IP; Snowflake account in us-east-1 accessed via private link; S3 landing bucket in ap-southeast-2
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: dbt compile and test on PR, sqlfluff lint on SQL files, Airflow DAG import test, on merge to main run dbt run --target staging then promote to prod on manual approval
devops.rollback_strategy: Snowflake time-travel allows rolling back mart tables to prior state before a bad dbt run; Airflow DAG version pinned to Git SHA; failed loads leave RAW schema intact for replay

ISS-001.title: Snowflake column masking policies not defined for auditor role
ISS-001.detail: The AUDITOR Snowflake role can currently see raw project_budgets.approved_budget and revised_budget columns without masking. SOX requires that auditors have read access for verification but budget amounts must be masked for any non-finance role.

history.note: Identified in security review; no masking policies exist on financial columns

ISS-002.title: Airflow EC2 using static credentials instead of instance profile
ISS-002.detail: Airflow connections to S3 and Secrets Manager are configured with static AWS access key and secret in environment variables. This violates the security baseline requiring no long-lived credentials on compute instances.

history.note: Found in infrastructure review; IAM instance profile not assigned to EC2

focus_issues.title: Snowflake column masking policies not defined for auditor role
focus_issues.detail: AUDITOR role can see unmasked budget amounts; SOX requires masking for non-finance roles

focus_issues.title: Airflow EC2 using static credentials instead of instance profile
focus_issues.detail: Static AWS credentials in Airflow environment variables violate no-long-lived-credentials baseline

latest_recommendations: Define Snowflake dynamic data masking policies for project_budgets columns before auditor role access is granted
Replace static AWS credentials on Airflow EC2 with IAM instance profile with scoped S3 and Secrets Manager permissions
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Oracle to Snowflake Cost Pipeline - Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved security issues from round 1 blocking audit readiness

previous_audits.summary: Strong overall architecture with well-designed dbt model layers and reconciliation gate. Two security gaps block audit readiness: missing Snowflake column masking for auditor role and static credentials on Airflow EC2.
strengths: Reconciliation gate comparing Snowflake actuals to Oracle control totals is correctly designed
Three-layer Snowflake schema (RAW/STAGING/MARTS) follows data warehouse best practice
Airflow DAG chain with XCom gate prevents bad data reaching marts
concerns: AUDITOR Snowflake role lacks column masking on financial amount columns
EC2 Airflow instance uses static AWS credentials instead of instance profile
blocking_issues: SOX audit readiness blocked until column masking policies are applied to AUDITOR role
recommendations: Add Snowflake MASKING POLICY on project_budgets.approved_budget and revised_budget columns, applying masking for any role not in FINANCE_ANALYST or DATA_ENGINEER
Assign IAM instance profile with s3:GetObject on landing bucket and secretsmanager:GetSecretValue scoped to Airflow secrets to the EC2 instance; remove static credential env vars

issue_updates.title: Snowflake column masking not defined
issue_updates.detail: No masking policies found on financial columns; auditor role has full read access to budget amounts

issue_updates.title: Static AWS credentials on Airflow EC2
issue_updates.detail: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY set in systemd environment file

previous_plan.title: Oracle to Snowflake Cost Pipeline - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Nightly Airflow pipeline extracting Oracle ERP cost data to S3, loading to Snowflake RAW schema, and transforming via dbt to unified project_budgets, actual_expenditures, and cost_commitments marts with reconciliation gate against Oracle control totals.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: EC2-hosted Airflow orchestrates nightly oracle_extract_dag pulling delta records via JDBC, landing CSVs in S3, loading to Snowflake RAW via COPY INTO, triggering dbt transforms, and running reconciliation test before mart promotion.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Okta SSO for Snowflake. Airflow secrets in Secrets Manager. Column masking on financial columns was noted as required but policies not yet defined. EC2 using static credentials pending instance profile assignment.
previous_plan.data_model: Snowflake marts: fct_actual_expenditures, fct_cost_commitments, dim_project_hierarchy, dim_cost_codes. Oracle source tables: COST_TRANSACTIONS, PROJECT_MASTER, COST_CODE_TABLE, COMMITMENT_LINES.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing two security blockers: Snowflake column masking for the AUDITOR role and IAM instance profile for Airflow EC2. The reconciliation gate and dbt mart architecture from round 1 are solid and retained. Fix report must explicitly address both ISS-001 and ISS-002 with the sections changed.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined Snowflake dynamic data masking policy 'mask_budget_amounts' applied to project_budgets.approved_budget and project_budgets.revised_budget; masking activates for any session role not in (FINANCE_ANALYST, DATA_ENGINEER, SYSADMIN); AUDITOR role sees SHA2-hashed token instead of raw value, preserving row-level audit access without exposing amounts
fix_report.expected_outcome: AUDITOR role can verify record existence and row counts for SOX purposes without reading raw financial amounts; masking policy documented in dbt schema.yml for lineage tracking

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced static AWS credential environment variables on Airflow EC2 with IAM instance profile assigned to the EC2 instance; instance profile has scoped inline policy: s3:GetObject and s3:PutObject on the landing bucket ARN only, secretsmanager:GetSecretValue scoped to Airflow secret ARN prefixes; no more AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID in systemd environment
fix_report.expected_outcome: No long-lived credentials on compute; credentials rotate automatically with EC2 instance metadata service; satisfies security baseline no-static-credentials requirement

target_output.title: Oracle to Snowflake Construction Cost Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A nightly ELT pipeline built with Apache Airflow on EC2 and dbt-core that extracts cost actuals, commitments, and budget revisions from a legacy Oracle 19c ERP, lands them in S3, loads to Snowflake RAW schema, and transforms them into unified project_budgets, actual_expenditures, and cost_commitments marts. A reconciliation gate validates Snowflake actuals against Oracle control totals before mart promotion. SOX controls enforced via Snowflake Okta SSO, column-level masking on financial amounts for the auditor role, and IAM instance profile credentials on the Airflow host.
target_output.architecture_overview: Apache Airflow 2.9 on EC2 m5.large in ap-southeast-2 orchestrates a three-DAG chain: oracle_extract_dag extracts delta cost records via cx_Oracle JDBC during the 02:00-05:00 AEST window and lands CSVs in an S3 landing bucket. snowflake_load_dag runs COPY INTO to load raw CSVs into the Snowflake RAW schema. dbt_transform_dag runs dbt models progressing through stg_ staging models, int_ intermediate unified cost code mapping, and fct_/dim_ mart models. A reconciliation dbt test compares SUM(actual_expenditures) per project_code against Oracle control totals in raw.oracle_control_totals; any variance > $1 fails the test and aborts mart promotion. Okta SSO gates all Snowflake access with role-based permissions. Snowflake dynamic data masking policies mask budget amount columns for non-finance roles including the AUDITOR Snowflake role. Airflow EC2 uses an IAM instance profile eliminating static credentials.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on EC2 m5.large, Python 3.12. Transformation: dbt-core 1.8 with dbt-snowflake adapter, sqlfluff for SQL linting. Source: Oracle 19c via cx_Oracle JDBC. Target: Snowflake Enterprise edition in us-east-1. Landing: S3 in ap-southeast-2. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with Airflow SecretsManagerBackend. Data Observability: Monte Carlo connected to Snowflake. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. IaC: Terraform for EC2, IAM, and S3.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Oracle Delta Extraction: cx_Oracle-based Airflow operator queries COST_TRANSACTIONS, COMMITMENT_LINES, and PROJECT_MASTER for records modified since last watermark, writes CSVs to S3. Snowflake Load: COPY INTO statement loads S3 CSV files into RAW schema tables. dbt Transformation: stg_oracle_cost_transactions normalises Oracle column names; int_unified_cost_codes maps 6-digit Oracle cost codes to 4-level unified chart of accounts; fct_actual_expenditures and fct_cost_commitments are the reporting marts. Reconciliation Gate: dbt singular test sums actuals per project against Oracle control total row. Auditor Views: dim_project_hierarchy and cost mart views with masking policies applied. Historical Backfill: manually triggered DAG with start_date/end_date parameters.
target_output.system_components: EC2 m5.large running Airflow scheduler and two workers in ap-southeast-2 private subnet, IAM instance profile attached. S3 landing bucket with 90-day lifecycle rule on raw CSVs. Snowflake account in us-east-1 with three databases (DEV, STAGING, PROD) and schemas RAW, STAGING, MARTS per database. Oracle 19c on-premises accessed via Direct Connect over JDBC during extract window. Monte Carlo SaaS connected to Snowflake for freshness and volume anomaly detection. GitHub Actions for CI/CD of dbt models and DAG files.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Cost Load: Airflow runs oracle_extract_dag at 02:00 AEST; Airflow operator opens cx_Oracle connection, queries COST_TRANSACTIONS WHERE modified_date > last_watermark, writes partitioned CSVs to S3 key s3://landing/cost/{date}/; on success triggers snowflake_load_dag which executes COPY INTO raw.cost_transactions FROM @s3_stage/{date}/; on success triggers dbt_transform_dag which runs dbt run --select staging.* then intermediate.* then marts.*; reconciliation singular test runs and on pass updates Snowflake_load_metadata.last_successful_run; Tableau dashboards reflect updated data by 06:00 AEST business start. Auditor Access: External auditor authenticates via Okta SSO, receives AUDITOR Snowflake role; queries fct_actual_expenditures and sees masked approved_budget values (SHA2 token); can verify row counts and transaction IDs for SOX sample testing without reading raw financial amounts.
target_output.data_model: Snowflake MARTS schema: fct_actual_expenditures (transaction_id VARCHAR, project_code VARCHAR(20), cost_code VARCHAR(10), amount FLOAT masked for AUDITOR role, transaction_date DATE, gl_account VARCHAR(20), oracle_source_id BIGINT, loaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). fct_cost_commitments (commitment_id VARCHAR, project_code, cost_code, vendor VARCHAR(200), committed_amount FLOAT masked, commitment_date DATE, status VARCHAR(20)). dim_project_hierarchy (project_code PK, project_name, programme, portfolio, region, project_manager). dim_cost_codes (code PK, description, trade_category, account_class). raw.oracle_control_totals (project_code, extract_date, oracle_actuals_sum FLOAT, loaded_at). Reconciliation test: assert ABS(SUM(fct_actual_expenditures.amount) - oracle_actuals_sum) < 1 per project_code.
target_output.api_design: No external API. dbt models expose Snowflake views consumed by Tableau via Snowflake connector. Airflow exposes a REST API (internal VPC only) for triggering backfill DAGs: POST /api/v1/dags/oracle_backfill_dag/dagRuns with payload {conf:{start_date,end_date}}. Snowflake time-travel enables point-in-time queries: SELECT * FROM fct_actual_expenditures AT(timestamp => '2026-01-15 00:00:00') for auditor reproductions.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta SSO as Snowflake identity provider; Snowflake roles: DATA_ENGINEER (RAW+STAGING+MARTS read-write), FINANCE_ANALYST (MARTS read), AUDITOR (MARTS read with column masking on financial amounts). Dynamic masking policy 'mask_budget_amounts' applied to approved_budget and revised_budget columns: CASE WHEN CURRENT_ROLE() IN ('FINANCE_ANALYST','DATA_ENGINEER','SYSADMIN') THEN val ELSE SHA2(val::STRING) END. IAM instance profile on Airflow EC2 with scoped inline policy for S3 landing bucket and Secrets Manager ARN prefix; no static AWS credentials. Oracle credentials in Secrets Manager, rotated quarterly. Snowflake network policy restricts connections to corporate VPN CIDR. Snowflake QUERY_HISTORY and ACCESS_HISTORY retained 365 days for SOX audit trail. S3 server-side encryption (SSE-S3) on landing bucket.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Airflow on EC2 m5.large deployed via Terraform in ap-southeast-2 private subnet; no public IP; SSM Session Manager for operator access. GitHub Actions CI: sqlfluff lint on dbt SQL, dbt compile --target dev, dbt test --target dev on PR; on merge to main dbt run --target staging; production promotion via manual GitHub Actions workflow dispatch with required reviewer approval (SOX change management).
