Mitigation: Prefect retry=3 with exponential backoff; gap events logged in failed_ingest_log with auto-backfill flow on sensor recovery; documented procedure for manual gap notification to regulator. Risk: TimescaleDB connection exhaustion under burst ingest from simultaneous sensor type failures and retries. Mitigation: pgbouncer connection pool in front of TimescaleDB; max pool size set to 20 per worker. Tradeoff: SFTP delivery is more reliable than email for formal regulatory records but requires authority IT coordination to set up SSH key pair exchange before go-live.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose ReportLab over WeasyPrint for PDF generation for deterministic rendering without headless browser dependency. Confirmed trigger levels stored in database with effective dates rather than Prefect flow parameters to maintain audit trail. Selected Azure Communication Services for SMS over Twilio to stay within Azure stack and reduce vendor count.

metadata.notes: Tunnelling environmental compliance monitoring pipeline; ISO 14001 and EPA conditions of approval; moderate plan with slightly thin cost and API sections.

project_goal.value: Plant and heavy equipment fleet management web app tracking machine locations via telematics GPS, monitoring fuel consumption and utilisation hours against job allocations, and surfacing idle-time and deployment-efficiency reports to reduce fuel costs and over-fleet charges for civil earthworks contractors
project_goal.rationale: Fleet managers spend 3+ hours per day manually compiling utilisation data from multiple telematics portals; this system unifies them

target_users.value: Fleet managers, operations managers, and site supervisors at civil earthworks contractors operating 20-200 machines across multiple active sites
target_users.rationale: Primary user roles identified in discovery interviews

project_class.rationale: Browser-based live map and reporting; no mobile-first requirement; fleet managers use desktop workstations

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Live GPS map requires realtime; telematics API polling requires integrations; utilisation analytics is core value proposition

complexity_level.rationale: PostGIS spatial queries, WebSocket GPS streaming at 30-second intervals per machine, telematics API integration, fuel reconciliation logic

risk_level.rationale: Internal operational data; no financial transactions or personal health data; GPS location of machines is commercially sensitive

data_sensitivity.rationale: Machine location and fuel data is internal operational information, not personal or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Fleet management is an internal system; all access requires Cognito authentication

access_model.value: RBAC: fleet_manager (full access), site_supervisor (read-only live map and own site allocation), operations_manager (reports only), admin (user and machine configuration)
access_model.rationale: Site supervisors need live machine visibility but should not edit job allocations or fleet configuration

feature_scope.value: Live GPS map with machine status, job allocation management, fuel transaction logging and reconciliation, utilisation hours tracking, idle-event detection and alerting, weekly fleet efficiency reports, telematics API integration for major providers (Teletrac Navman, VisionTrack)
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope derived from fleet manager pain-point interviews

mvp_scope.value: Live GPS map with machine positions and status, job allocation assignment, fuel transaction manual entry, basic idle-time detection, weekly utilisation CSV export
mvp_scope.rationale: Core visibility use case that eliminates the primary manual work within 6-week MVP scope

security_baseline.value: Cognito with MFA for admin role, TLS 1.3 in transit, encryption at rest for PostGIS database, no public API endpoints
security_baseline.rationale: Internal system with sensitive machine location data; MFA for admin prevents unauthorised fleet reconfiguration

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript; Mapbox GL JS for live GPS map; Recharts for utilisation dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: Mapbox GL JS provides WebGL-accelerated machine marker rendering at 200+ concurrent machines; Recharts for time-series fuel and utilisation charts

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Echo; WebSocket collector for telematics pings; PostGIS spatial queries
backend_stack.rationale: Go's concurrency model handles 200+ concurrent WebSocket connections efficiently; Echo provides clean route grouping

data_platform.value: PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 for spatial machine location queries; Redis 7 for pub/sub GPS ping distribution and session cache
data_platform.rationale: PostGIS enables geofence containment queries for zone-based idle detection; Redis pub/sub decouples WebSocket collector from browser push

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in ap-southeast-2 (Sydney) for data residency
hosting_target.rationale: Australian civil contractors require data residency; Fargate scales collector tasks for GPS ingestion spikes

privacy_retention_policy.value: Machine location history retained 24 months for job costing; fuel transactions retained 7 years for financial records; idle event logs retained 12 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Job costing requires historical location data; fuel is a financial record subject to ATO retention requirements

future_scope.value: Predictive maintenance alerts from engine hour thresholds, driver behaviour scoring, mobile supervisor app, automated telematics API polling replacing manual entry
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap items dependent on telematics vendor API access agreements

constraints.value: Data must remain in ap-southeast-2; support 200 concurrent machines; Mapbox GL JS licence required; CircleCI for CI/CD per existing toolchain
constraints.rationale: Australian data residency is a contractual requirement with several clients; CircleCI is the team standard

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus on Fargate; machine-level GPS ingestion rate and idle detection lag as custom metrics; PagerDuty for GPS collector failures
observability_baseline.rationale: GPS ingestion failures could result in undetected idle machines incurring hire costs; PagerDuty escalation is required

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with live demo to fleet managers after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Fleet managers are the key stakeholders; demo-driven feedback loop catches scope misalignments early

project.goal: Unify telematics data and provide live machine visibility and fleet efficiency analytics

frontend.map: Mapbox GL JS for live GPS layer
frontend.state: TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for map filter state

backend.realtime: WebSocket for GPS pings, Redis pub/sub for distribution

open_questions.idle_threshold: Idle defined as zero movement for 10 min or configurable per machine type?

reasoner_reviews.product: The live GPS map is the primary daily-use feature -- fleet managers will leave this open on a second monitor all day. Map performance is critical: Mapbox GL JS with WebGL rendering is the right choice for 200 machine markers with status icons. The idle-time detection threshold should be configurable per machine type: excavators idle at 10 minutes, water trucks at 20 minutes. The weekly efficiency report is the key decision-support tool; it should rank machines by idle ratio and flag the top 10 candidates for redeployment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Echo is well-suited to handling 200 concurrent WebSocket connections for GPS ingestion. The Redis pub/sub architecture is correct: the Go WebSocket collector writes each GPS ping to a Redis channel keyed by machine_id, and a separate Go goroutine fan-out delivers updates to all browser sessions subscribed to that machine. PostGIS spatial queries for geofence containment (idle detection within job_site polygon) are more accurate than time-only idle detection and should be used for the idle_event detector. Partitioning the location_events table by week will be necessary within 12 months at 200 machines pinging every 30 seconds.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cognito MFA for admin role prevents unauthorised machine reassignment. Row-level security in PostGIS scoped to job_allocation ensures site supervisors only see machines allocated to their sites. TLS 1.3 is sufficient; no PII in GPS data. API keys for telematics vendor integrations stored in AWS Secrets Manager with rotation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 200 machines at 30-second ping intervals is 400 writes per minute to PostGIS -- easily handled by a t3.medium RDS instance. Location_events will reach approximately 600,000 rows per week; partition by week and enable TimescaleDB-style compression if costs escalate. Mapbox GL JS Standard plan required at this data volume.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The idle detection logic is the most business-critical algorithmic component: it directly drives the cost-reduction value proposition. Define idle_events precisely -- a machine is idle if its last GPS ping shows speed < 2 km/h AND engine_running=true for a configurable duration. Distinguish idle from parked (engine_running=false) in the database schema.

backend.service_design: Go Echo server with route groups: /api/v1/machines, /api/v1/jobs, /api/v1/fuel, /api/v1/reports. Separate goroutine-based WebSocket collector listens on /ws/gps for telematics ping ingestion. Redis pub/sub channels keyed by machine_id distribute GPS updates to connected browser clients via a goroutine fan-out dispatcher. Background goroutine runs idle_event_detector every 60 seconds using PostGIS ST_DWithin queries on recent location_events.
backend.api_patterns: REST for CRUD operations; WebSocket for live GPS push to browser clients. Cognito JWT validated on all routes via Echo middleware. Rate limiting on GPS ingestion endpoint: 10 pings/minute per machine_id.
backend.background_jobs: Idle event detector: runs every 60 seconds, queries machines with engine_running=true and no location_event movement in idle_threshold_minutes, creates idle_events record and sends Redis alert. Weekly report generator: cron job aggregates location_events and fuel_transactions by job_allocation for the trailing 7 days, computes idle_ratio and fuel_per_productive_hour per machine, stores fleet_cost_report, emails PDF to operations_manager via SES.
backend.backend_risks: Location_events table growth rate at 200 machines x 30-second pings: approximately 24 million rows per month. Requires range partitioning by week and index strategy review at 3-month mark. Redis pub/sub fan-out with 100 concurrent browser sessions per machine could create hot channels.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with route-based views: /map (live fleet map), /machines (fleet register), /jobs (job allocations), /fuel (fuel transactions), /reports (efficiency reports), /admin (configuration).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Live Map: Mapbox GL JS map with machine markers coloured by status (active/idle/parked/offline); click marker to open machine detail drawer with current speed, engine status, and current job_allocation. Machine markers subscribe to WebSocket GPS stream and update position in real time. Fleet Register: data table of plant_equipment with utilisation_hours, current_allocation, and idle_ratio for trailing 30 days. Reports: weekly fleet_cost_report view with ranked idle-time table and fuel-per-productive-hour chart.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for REST data (machine list, job allocations, reports). Zustand for map state (visible job sites filter, machine type filter, selected machine_id). WebSocket connection managed in a React context that distributes GPS update events to individual machine marker components via subscribed machine_id.
frontend.performance_notes: Mapbox GL JS renders 200 machine markers at WebGL 60 fps. GPS update events batched into 2-second animation frames to prevent marker jitter. Cluster markers at low zoom levels for dense site areas.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with MFA enforced for admin and fleet_manager roles; site_supervisor uses password-only. JWT validated by Go middleware on every request. WebSocket handshake validated with Cognito token before GPS stream subscription.
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security: site_supervisor role restricted to job_allocations where supervisor_id = current_user_id. fleet_manager and operations_manager roles have unrestricted read; only fleet_manager and admin can write machine assignments.
security.secrets_management: Telematics vendor API keys (Teletrac Navman, VisionTrack) stored in AWS Secrets Manager; Go backend fetches at startup with 6-hour cache refresh. Mapbox public token stored as environment variable; secret token stored in Secrets Manager.

data.entities: plant_equipment (id UUID PK, asset_number TEXT UNIQUE, machine_type ENUM excavator/bulldozer/water_truck/grader/compactor/tipper, make TEXT, model TEXT, year INT, idle_threshold_minutes INT, current_allocation_id FK, status ENUM active/idle/parked/offline). telematics_devices (id UUID PK, machine_id FK, device_serial TEXT, provider ENUM teletrac_navman/visiontrack/manual, last_ping_at TIMESTAMPTZ). job_allocations (id UUID PK, machine_id FK, job_site_id FK, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, allocated_by_id FK). location_events (id UUID PK, machine_id FK, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, lat DOUBLE PRECISION, lon DOUBLE PRECISION, speed_kmh REAL, engine_running BOOL, geom GEOMETRY(POINT, 4326)) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at). fuel_transactions (id UUID PK, machine_id FK, job_allocation_id FK, transaction_date DATE, litres REAL, odometer_hours REAL, recorded_by_id FK). idle_events (id UUID PK, machine_id FK, job_allocation_id FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_minutes INT, geom GEOMETRY(POINT, 4326)). fleet_cost_reports (id UUID PK, report_week DATE, machine_id FK, productive_hours REAL, idle_hours REAL, idle_ratio REAL, fuel_litres REAL, fuel_per_productive_hour REAL).
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS 3.4 extension in ap-southeast-2 Multi-AZ. location_events partitioned weekly by recorded_at with BRIN index on geom for spatial queries. Redis 7 for GPS pub/sub channels and Cognito session tokens.
data.analytics_events: Fleet efficiency: idle_ratio per machine per week, top-10 idle machines ranked by idle_hours. Fuel efficiency: fuel_per_productive_hour trend by machine_type over 12 weeks. GPS coverage: machines_without_ping_24h metric for telematics device health.

devops.deployment_topology: Two ECS Fargate task definitions: web-api (Go Echo, GPS WebSocket collector) and report-worker (weekly report generator cron). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in ap-southeast-2. ElastiCache Redis. ALB with WAF. CloudFront for static React assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: CircleCI: Go unit tests, golangci-lint, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with health check gate. React: vitest unit tests, Playwright E2E for live map GPS simulation. Deploy to dev on branch push, staging on main merge, production on tagged release with manual approval.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Go /metrics endpoint (GPS ingestion rate, WebSocket connection count, idle detector run duration, Redis pub/sub channel depth). Grafana dashboards: GPS collector health, idle detection lag, weekly report generation status. PagerDuty: GPS collector goroutine crash, RDS replication lag above 30 seconds.
devops.devops_risks: location_events partition management requires automated weekly partition creation; add partition-creation job to report-worker cron. PostGIS index bloat on the geom column as location_events grows; schedule VACUUM ANALYZE weekly.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a real-time fleet management platform for civil earthworks contractors. Key decisions: PostGIS with weekly range partitioning for location_events to manage 24M+ rows/month at 200 machines; Redis pub/sub with Go goroutine fan-out for WebSocket GPS distribution to browser clients; Mapbox GL JS for WebGL-accelerated 200-marker live map; idle detection using PostGIS ST_DWithin plus engine_running flag to distinguish idle from parked; CircleCI per existing toolchain.
target_output.title: Civil Earthworks Plant Fleet Management Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A real-time fleet management web application for civil earthworks contractors tracking 200+ machines via telematics GPS pings, built with React 18 and Mapbox GL JS on the frontend and a Go 1.22 Echo backend on AWS ECS Fargate in ap-southeast-2. PostGIS on PostgreSQL 16 stores machine location history with weekly range partitioning; Redis pub/sub distributes live GPS updates to browser clients via WebSocket. Idle-event detection uses PostGIS geofence queries and engine-running flags to distinguish genuine idle machines from parked equipment. Weekly fleet efficiency reports rank machines by idle ratio and fuel-per-productive-hour to surface redeployment opportunities.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to a Go Echo API on ECS Fargate. Live GPS tracking uses a dedicated WebSocket endpoint on the Go server: telematics devices ping /ws/gps every 30 seconds; the Go collector writes each ping to PostGIS location_events and publishes to a Redis pub/sub channel keyed by machine_id. A goroutine fan-out dispatcher consumes these channels and pushes GPS updates to all browser WebSocket sessions subscribed to that machine, enabling the Mapbox GL JS live map to update machine marker positions in real time without polling. A background Go goroutine runs idle_event_detector every 60 seconds using PostGIS ST_DWithin to identify machines with engine_running=true and no movement beyond 50 metres in the idle threshold window. Cognito authenticates users; row-level security in PostgreSQL scopes site supervisor visibility to their allocated job sites. Weekly report cron aggregates utilisation and fuel data into fleet_cost_reports and emails PDFs via SES.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Mapbox GL JS 3 (WebGL machine markers), Recharts (utilisation time-series), TanStack Query, Zustand, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Go 1.22, Echo 4 (REST + WebSocket), gorilla/websocket, go-redis/redis v9 for pub/sub, jackc/pgx v5 for PostGIS queries, aws-sdk-go-v2 for Cognito JWT validation and SES. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS 3.4, weekly range partitioning on location_events, Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for pub/sub and session. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (ap-southeast-2), ALB with WAF, CloudFront, RDS Multi-AZ, ECR, CircleCI, Grafana + Prometheus, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Live Fleet Map: Mapbox GL JS map showing real-time positions of all plant_equipment with status-coded markers (active/idle/parked/offline); click-to-open machine detail drawer with current speed, engine status, job_allocation, and trailing 2-hour track line. Job Allocation Manager: assign plant_equipment to job_site for a date range; fleet_manager can reassign mid-job. Fuel Transaction Log: manual entry of fuel_transactions per machine per day with litres and odometer hours; reconciliation view compares fuel logged vs telematics-estimated consumption. Idle Event Detection: background detector flags idle_events with duration, location, and job context; idle event list per machine with cost estimate based on hire rate. Fleet Efficiency Reports: weekly fleet_cost_report ranked by idle_ratio and fuel_per_productive_hour; highlight top-10 machines for redeployment; export to CSV or PDF.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN for React 18 SPA static assets, ALB with WAF for API and WebSocket traffic, ECS Fargate web-api task (Go Echo REST API and GPS WebSocket collector), ECS Fargate report-worker task (idle event detector cron, weekly report generator, partition management), RDS PostgreSQL 16 + PostGIS 3.4 Multi-AZ (location_events weekly partitioned), Redis 7 ElastiCache (GPS pub/sub channels and session tokens), AWS Cognito user pool (RBAC, MFA for admin/fleet_manager), AWS Secrets Manager (telematics vendor API keys), SES for weekly report email dispatch, Prometheus + Grafana for metrics, PagerDuty for GPS collector alerting.
target_output.workflows: Live GPS Tracking: Teletrac Navman or VisionTrack telematics device sends HTTP POST to /ws/gps with machine_id, lat, lon, speed_kmh, engine_running every 30 seconds; Go collector validates JWT, writes location_events row to PostGIS, computes GEOMETRY(POINT) for geom column, and publishes {machine_id, lat, lon, speed, status} to Redis channel machine:{machine_id}. Goroutine fan-out dispatcher reads from all active machine channels and pushes updates to subscribed browser WebSocket sessions; Mapbox GL JS map receives the event and smoothly animates the machine marker to the new position. Idle Event Detection: every 60 seconds the idle_event_detector queries machines WHERE engine_running=true AND last_movement_at < NOW() - idle_threshold_minutes; for each candidate, ST_DWithin confirms position has not moved beyond 50 metres; detector creates idle_events record with started_at, geom, and job_allocation_id, updates plant_equipment.status to 'idle', and publishes an idle_alert to the Redis channel consumed by the WebSocket dispatcher to update map marker colour. Weekly Fleet Report: every Monday at 06:00 AEST the report-worker cron aggregates location_events and fuel_transactions for the trailing 7 days per machine per job_allocation, computes idle_ratio = idle_hours / total_engine_hours and fuel_per_productive_hour, stores fleet_cost_report rows, renders a PDF via a Go HTML template, and dispatches via SES to the operations_manager email list.
target_output.data_model: plant_equipment (id UUID PK, asset_number TEXT UNIQUE, machine_type ENUM excavator/bulldozer/water_truck/grader/compactor/tipper, make TEXT, model TEXT, year INT, idle_threshold_minutes INT DEFAULT 10, current_allocation_id UUID FK, status ENUM active/idle/parked/offline, created_at). telematics_devices (id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, device_serial TEXT, provider ENUM teletrac_navman/visiontrack/manual, last_ping_at TIMESTAMPTZ). job_allocations (id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, job_site_id UUID FK, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, allocated_by_id UUID FK). location_events (id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, lat DOUBLE PRECISION, lon DOUBLE PRECISION, speed_kmh REAL, engine_running BOOL, geom GEOMETRY(POINT,4326)) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at). fuel_transactions (id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, job_allocation_id UUID FK, transaction_date DATE, litres REAL, odometer_hours REAL, recorded_by_id UUID FK). idle_events (id UUID PK, machine_id UUID FK, job_allocation_id UUID FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ, duration_minutes INT, geom GEOMETRY(POINT,4326)). fleet_cost_reports (id UUID PK, report_week DATE, machine_id UUID FK, productive_hours REAL, idle_hours REAL, idle_ratio REAL, fuel_litres REAL, fuel_per_productive_hour REAL).
target_output.api_design: REST (authenticated via Cognito JWT): GET /api/v1/machines (fleet list with current status), POST /api/v1/machines (register new asset), GET /api/v1/machines/:id/history?from=&to= (location_events paginated), POST /api/v1/jobs (create job_allocation), GET /api/v1/fuel?machine_id=&week= (fuel transactions), POST /api/v1/fuel (record fuel transaction), GET /api/v1/reports/weekly?week= (fleet_cost_report for week), GET /api/v1/reports/idle-events?machine_id= (idle event list). WebSocket: GET /ws/gps (telematics device ping ingestion, requires machine JWT). GET /ws/map (browser GPS update stream, requires user Cognito JWT, subscribes to requested machine_ids).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pool with MFA enforced for admin and fleet_manager roles. Cognito JWT validated in Go Echo middleware via JWKS endpoint with 10-minute key cache. RBAC: site_supervisor access scoped to their job_site via PostgreSQL row-level security policy on job_allocations and location_events. TLS 1.3 on ALB for all traffic including WebSocket upgrade. Telematics vendor API keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager; fetched at container startup with 6-hour refresh interval. WAF on ALB with rate limiting: 60 requests/minute per IP for REST endpoints; WebSocket GPS ingestion capped at 10 pings/minute per machine_id via Go rate limiter. Audit log: all job_allocation changes and machine status overrides written to audit_events table with user_id, action, and timestamp.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: CircleCI pipelines: Go tests (go test ./...), golangci-lint, Docker multi-stage build with Go binary, ECR push. React: vitest, Playwright E2E (tests GPS update flow with WebSocket mock), Docker build, ECR push. ECS deploy: rolling update with health check against /health. Environments: dev (single AZ Fargate tasks, RDS Single-AZ, auto-deploy on branch push), staging (Multi-AZ, auto-deploy on main merge, Playwright E2E suite runs post-deploy), production (Multi-AZ, manual approval gate, ECS auto-scaling 2-6 tasks on CPU 60% target). Weekly partition creation: report-worker cron creates next week's location_events partition every Sunday at 23:00. Database migrations via golang-migrate; run as ECS one-off task before rolling deploy.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics exported from Go /metrics: gps_pings_per_second per machine_type, websocket_connections_active (browser clients), idle_detector_run_duration_seconds, redis_pubsub_channel_depth per machine_id. Grafana dashboards: GPS Collector Health (ping rate vs expected rate per active machine), Fleet Status Overview (active/idle/parked/offline counts), Idle Detection Lag (time between idle onset and event creation). PagerDuty critical alerts: GPS collector goroutine crash (zero pings in 5 minutes during business hours), RDS replication lag above 30 seconds, idle_detector last run more than 5 minutes ago. Custom business metric: machines_with_idle_ratio_above_30pct_trailing_7d for weekly ops review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated AUD 2400/month for 200 machines: RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium (ap-southeast-2) AUD 280/month, ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.small AUD 60/month, ECS Fargate 2 tasks (web-api + report-worker) AUD 120/month, ALB + CloudFront AUD 80/month, Grafana Cloud free tier, SES AUD 10/month, Mapbox GL JS Standard plan AUD 800/month (dominant cost at high map tile load). Total under AUD 2500. Scaling: location_events partitioned weekly with BRIN spatial index; query performance reviewed at 6-month data volume. ECS auto-scaling handles GPS ingestion spikes during shift changeovers.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Go Echo server with GPS WebSocket collector and REST API, React 18 Mapbox GL JS live map with machine markers, job_allocation management, manual fuel_transactions entry, Cognito auth with RBAC, basic idle_event detection (time-based), weekly CSV export of utilisation data, CircleCI CI/CD pipeline, PostGIS location_events with weekly partitioning. Phase 2 (8 weeks): PostGIS ST_DWithin spatial idle detection replacing time-only, Redis pub/sub GPS fan-out for multi-browser updates, idle event alerting and map marker colour coding, fleet_cost_report PDF generation via SES, Teletrac Navman API integration for automated GPS ingestion replacing device WebSocket. Phase 3 (4 weeks): VisionTrack API integration, machine-type-specific idle thresholds, analytics dashboard with 12-week trend charts for fuel and idle metrics, site_supervisor role with RLS-scoped map view.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint enforced in CI with errcheck and staticcheck enabled. All PostGIS spatial queries use parameterised pgx queries; no string interpolation in SQL. Go unit tests for idle_event_detector with mock PostGIS responses. React: TypeScript strict mode, Vitest for component tests including WebSocket mock. Mapbox GL JS marker updates tested with Jest fake timers. Database migrations are forward-only; no down migrations. location_events BRIN index reviewed monthly during early scale-up; switch to GiST if spatial query plans degrade.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: location_events partition explosion at 200 machines x 30-second pings producing 700,000+ rows per week; each weekly partition requires explicit creation. Mitigation: report-worker cron creates next-2-weeks partitions automatically; alert if partition creation fails. Risk: Redis pub/sub hot channel if 100 browser sessions all subscribe to the same high-activity machine. Mitigation: fan-out goroutine uses buffered channels per session; slow consumers dropped after 5-second buffer overflow. Tradeoff: Go Echo monolith vs microservices -- monolith chosen for MVP simplicity; GPS collector and API are separate goroutines within one process, enabling future extraction. Risk: Mapbox GL JS billing spike if fleet managers leave the live map open on idle machines all day; Mapbox Standard plan pricing is tile-load-based -- cache static basemap tiles aggressively with CloudFront.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Idle threshold configurable per machine type (default 10 minutes for excavators, 20 minutes for water trucks) stored in plant_equipment.idle_threshold_minutes. Idle definition: engine_running=true AND no movement beyond 50 metres in threshold window (PostGIS ST_DWithin) -- distinct from parked (engine_running=false). Telematics ingestion via HTTP POST WebSocket in MVP; Teletrac Navman polling API added in Phase 2. Mapbox GL JS Standard plan selected for 200 machine marker volume.

metadata.notes: Strong: all sections are domain-specific with PostGIS spatial queries, Redis pub/sub GPS fan-out, and detailed idle_event detection logic for the earthworks fleet domain.

profile.domain: Construction Concrete Defect Inspection Mobile App

project_goal.value: Mobile application for construction site quality inspectors that uses an on-device computer vision model to detect visible concrete defects from smartphone photos, classifying severity, suggesting remediation, and logging inspections to the project quality management system
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual defect identification with AI-assisted classification to reduce missed defects and speed up inspection workflows

target_users.value: Site quality inspectors, site engineers, and project quality managers on commercial building construction projects
target_users.rationale: Primary field users performing concrete inspections and reviewing quality trends

project_class.rationale: Flutter cross-platform mobile app with on-device TFLite model and cloud backend sync

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend, FastAPI backend for sync, PostgreSQL for quality records, Firebase Auth, on-device TFLite model, analytics dashboard

complexity_level.rationale: On-device ML with offline capability, cloud sync conflict resolution, and analytics trend detection

risk_level.rationale: Internal project data; incorrect defect classifications could affect quality decisions but no safety-critical automation

data_sensitivity.rationale: Inspection records and defect photos are internal project documents; commercially sensitive but not personal data

external_exposure.rationale: All API and backend access requires Firebase Auth JWT

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with email/password; roles: inspector (create inspections), site_engineer (review and override), quality_manager (view analytics and export)
access_model.rationale: Role-based access for field users vs management review

feature_scope.value: On-device TFLite defect detection and classification, offline inspection form capture, cloud sync with conflict resolution, photo evidence with GCS storage, backend analytics for defect trends by pour and crew, model version management
feature_scope.rationale: Complete inspection-to-analytics workflow

mvp_scope.value: On-device defect classification for honeycombing and cracking, inspection form submission with photo, cloud sync when online
mvp_scope.rationale: Core field workflow for Q3 pilot with one structural contractor

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT on all API calls, TLS 1.3 for backend communication, photos stored in GCS with signed URLs, no raw photo URLs exposed in API responses
security_baseline.rationale: Internal project data protection standard

frontend_stack.value: Flutter with Dart, TFLite Flutter plugin for on-device inference
frontend_stack.rationale: Flutter provides cross-platform iOS and Android from single codebase; TFLite Flutter for on-device model execution

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI chosen for async handlers and automatic OpenAPI spec generation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for inspection records; GCS for photo evidence
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational inspection data; GCS for scalable photo storage with lifecycle policies

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run in australia-southeast1
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless scaling aligned to field inspection peaks; data residency in Australia

privacy_retention_policy.value: Inspection records retained for project duration plus 7 years; defect photos retained same period; model inference logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Construction defect liability period requires long-term retention

future_scope.value: Formwork and reinforcement defect classification, integration with head contractor QHSE platform, model retraining pipeline from confirmed field corrections
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 expansion beyond concrete surface defects

constraints.value: On-device model must classify defects within 3 seconds on a mid-range Android device; app must function fully offline for inspections in below-ground concrete pours
constraints.rationale: Site connectivity is unreliable; slow classification disrupts inspection workflow

observability_baseline.value: Firebase Crashlytics for app crashes, Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run metrics, custom analytics events for model inference outcomes
observability_baseline.rationale: Mobile crash reporting and backend observability standard

execution_preference.value: Feature-flagged rollout to pilot contractor before full deployment; model updates via Firebase Remote Config version flag
execution_preference.rationale: Staged rollout reduces risk of classifier errors reaching all inspectors simultaneously

llm_integration.value: On-device TFLite MobileNetV3 model fine-tuned on 12,000 labelled concrete defect images; model artefacts bundled in Flutter app via assets; model versions managed via Firebase Remote Config triggering in-app model download on version bump
llm_integration.rationale: On-device inference required for offline capability; TFLite for mobile inference efficiency

project.goal: On-device AI concrete defect inspection mobile app with cloud quality management backend
project.target_users: Site inspectors, site engineers, quality managers

frontend.inference: tflite_flutter plugin, model bundled in assets
frontend.offline: SQLite via drift package for local inspection storage

data.photos: GCS bucket with uniform bucket-level access

confirmed_decisions.inference: on-device TFLite only, no server-side inference in MVP
confirmed_decisions.sync: optimistic client-side with last-write-wins conflict resolution

reasoner_reviews.product: ISS-001 (model version drift) has been the core blocker - inspectors on older app versions were using a model trained on 8,000 images while new installs use the 12,000-image model, causing inconsistent classifications on the same defect type. The Remote Config-driven model download resolved this in round 2 but the rollout mechanism needs a fallback for inspectors in areas with no connectivity during the model update window.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The TFLite model bundling approach from round 2 is correct. The drift issue was that the app checked Firebase Remote Config only at startup. ISS-001 resolution requires the model version check to also run when the app returns to foreground after a background sync, ensuring inspectors who kept the app open through an update are prompted to download the new model.
reasoner_reviews.security: ISS-002 (GCS photo URLs exposed in sync response body) should now be fully addressed following round 2 fixes. Confirm that the photo_evidence endpoint returns only signed URLs with 15-minute expiry and the PostgreSQL inspection records store only the GCS object path, not the full signed URL.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 3-second inference latency SLO is being met on tested Samsung Galaxy A-series devices but not consistently on older iPhone SE models. If the pilot contractor has inspectors using iPhone SE generation 1 or 2, model quantisation (INT8 post-training quantisation) should reduce latency to under 2.5 seconds on those devices.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with feature-first directory structure: features/inspection (capture, form, sync), features/defect_viewer (history, photo), features/analytics (dashboard). Shared: core/tflite_service.dart, core/sync_service.dart, core/auth_service.dart
frontend.pages_and_flows: InspectionCapturePage: camera view with capture button, runs TFLite inference on capture, pre-fills DefectForm with classification and severity. DefectFormPage: inspector reviews/overrides classification, adds location, pour_id, notes, submits. InspectionHistoryPage: local SQLite list with sync status indicator. AnalyticsDashboardPage: quality_manager role only, shows defect trend charts via Cloud Run analytics API.
frontend.state_management: Riverpod for app-wide state including auth, sync status, and model version. Local SQLite via drift package stores inspections with sync_status (pending/synced/conflict) field.
frontend.performance_notes: TFLite inference runs on isolate to avoid UI thread blocking; inference output is top-3 classes with confidence scores; display only if top class confidence > 0.65 threshold

backend.service_design: FastAPI with routers: /inspections (create, list, get), /photos (generate upload URL, generate download URL), /analytics (defect trends by pour, crew), /model-version (current version metadata)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful; Firebase JWT verified in middleware; role claims extracted from Firebase custom claims; PATCH /inspections/{id} for engineer overrides with override_reason field
backend.failure_handling: Idempotent POST /inspections using client-generated UUID to handle duplicate submissions from retry after connectivity loss; Cloud Run min-instances=1 to eliminate cold start on first morning inspection

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth email/password; Firebase custom claims set by admin API for role assignment (inspector/site_engineer/quality_manager); FastAPI middleware verifies ID token and extracts claims
security.abuse_prevention: Cloud Run request rate limited by Cloud Armor policy: 60 req/min per Firebase UID; photo upload URLs restricted to authenticated users and expire after 15 minutes
security.privacy_controls: GCS bucket uniform bucket-level access; no public ACLs; photo access via POST /photos/{id}/download-url returning signed URL; raw GCS object paths never returned in API responses

data.entities: inspection_sessions (id UUID, inspector_id, concrete_pour_id FK, location_description, started_at, completed_at, sync_status). defect_observations (id UUID, session_id FK, defect_type ENUM honeycombing/cracking/surface_void/delamination, severity ENUM minor/moderate/severe, model_confidence FLOAT, inspector_override BOOLEAN, override_reason TEXT, photo_gcs_path, recorded_at). concrete_pours (id UUID, element_id, pour_date, formwork_type, crew_id, design_mix_code). remediation_recommendations (id UUID, defect_type, severity, recommendation_text, code_reference). quality_hold_points (id UUID, pour_id, defect_observation_id FK, status ENUM open/cleared/escalated, cleared_by, cleared_at).
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in australia-southeast1; GCS bucket for defect photos with Nearline storage class for items > 90 days; drift SQLite on device with defect_observations and sync_queue tables
data.analytics_events: Firebase Analytics events: defect_classified (defect_type, confidence, overridden), inspection_synced (defect_count, duration_seconds), model_version_updated

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service in australia-southeast1 with min-instances=1; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL private IP via Cloud SQL Auth proxy sidecar; GCS bucket in australia-southeast1
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Flutter test, flutter analyze, FastAPI pytest with pytest-asyncio and testcontainers for Cloud SQL, docker build, push Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy; Flutter iOS and Android builds via GitHub Actions with Fastlane
devops.observability_stack: Firebase Crashlytics for Flutter crash reporting; Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run request rates and latency; custom log-based metrics for defect classification outcomes and sync failure rates

ISS-001.title: TFLite model version drift between app installs
ISS-001.detail: Inspectors running older app versions used a model trained on 8,000 images while new installs used 12,000-image model, causing classification inconsistency for the same defect type across devices.

history.note: Identified from conflicting inspector classifications on same pour element
Firebase Remote Config model version flag with in-app download on app foreground restored version consistency

ISS-002.title: GCS photo object paths exposed in sync API response body
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 POST /inspections/sync response returned raw GCS object paths in photo_url fields, allowing any authenticated user to construct permanent GCS URLs bypassing signed URL expiry.

history.note: Security review found raw GCS paths in API response
photo_url field removed from sync response; photo access requires POST /photos/{id}/download-url returning 15-min signed URL

ISS-003.title: TFLite inference latency exceeds 3s on iPhone SE gen 1/2
ISS-003.detail: On iPhone SE first and second generation devices, TFLite MobileNetV3 inference takes 3.8-4.2 seconds, exceeding the 3-second SLO. Pilot contractor has 4 inspectors using these devices.

history.note: Identified during device compatibility testing with pilot contractor device inventory

focus_issues.title: TFLite inference latency exceeds 3s on iPhone SE gen 1/2
focus_issues.detail: iPhone SE gen 1/2 inference at 3.8-4.2s; INT8 post-training quantisation required to meet 3s SLO

latest_recommendations: Apply INT8 post-training quantisation to TFLite model to reduce inference latency on low-end devices to under 2.5 seconds
Add device-capability check at app launch to select between INT8 quantised and FP16 model variants based on available hardware accelerator

convergence_state.reason: One unresolved medium-severity latency issue on legacy devices in pilot contractor fleet

previous_audits.summary: Good mobile architecture with solid offline-first design but critical security issue with raw GCS paths in API and model version drift causing classification inconsistency.
strengths: Offline-first drift SQLite design handles tunnel connectivity correctly
Firebase Auth custom claims for role management is well-designed
concerns: GCS object paths exposed in sync API response
No model version enforcement mechanism across existing app installs
blocking_issues: GCS path exposure allows bypass of signed URL access controls
recommendations: Remove GCS paths from all API responses; enforce access via signed URL endpoint only
Implement Remote Config model version flag with in-app download prompt

issue_updates.title: Model version drift
issue_updates.detail: No mechanism to enforce model version across installed apps

issue_updates.title: GCS paths in API response
issue_updates.detail: Raw GCS object paths returned in sync response body

previous_audits.summary: ISS-001 and ISS-002 resolved. Architecture is strong. New ISS-003 latency issue on legacy devices is medium severity and does not block passing but should be addressed before full rollout.
strengths: Remote Config model versioning correctly triggers in-app download on foreground
Signed URL access pattern is now correctly enforced
concerns: TFLite inference 3.8-4.2s on iPhone SE gen 1/2 exceeds 3-second SLO
recommendations: Apply INT8 post-training quantisation to produce a quantised model variant; select between FP16 and INT8 models at runtime based on device benchmark score

issue_updates.title: Inference latency on iPhone SE
issue_updates.detail: Pilot contractor has 4 inspectors on SE gen 1/2; quantisation needed for SLO compliance

previous_plan.title: Concrete Defect Inspection App - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Flutter app with on-device TFLite MobileNetV3 classifier detecting honeycombing, cracking, and surface voids. Firebase Remote Config enforces model version with in-app download. GCS photo access restricted to signed URLs via dedicated endpoint.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Flutter app with drift SQLite for offline inspections, TFLite inference on capture, FastAPI Cloud Run backend for sync. Firebase Auth JWT gates all API calls. GCS for photo storage with 15-min signed URL access.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth with custom role claims. GCS signed URLs only for photo access (ISS-002 resolved). Remote Config model version flag (ISS-001 resolved). TLS 1.3 on Cloud Run.
previous_plan.llm_integration_notes: TFLite MobileNetV3 FP16 model bundled in app assets; Remote Config flag triggers download of new model version from Firebase Storage; model checked on app foreground return.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 focuses on ISS-003: TFLite inference latency on low-end iPhone SE devices. Solution is INT8 post-training quantisation producing a second model variant, with runtime device selection based on available hardware accelerator capability. Architecture from round 2 is solid; only tflite_service.dart and model management sections change materially.

fix_report.action_taken: Applied INT8 post-training quantisation to TFLite MobileNetV3 model using tf.lite.TFLiteConverter with DEFAULT optimisation and representative dataset calibration, producing model_v3_int8.tflite alongside existing model_v3_fp16.tflite. Flutter tflite_service.dart now runs a startup microbenchmark (10 inference passes on a blank 224x224 image) and selects INT8 model if median latency exceeds 2.0s, otherwise uses FP16. Both model variants bundled in assets and updated via Firebase Storage download on Remote Config version bump.
fix_report.expected_outcome: iPhone SE gen 1/2 inference latency reduces to 2.1-2.4s with INT8 model; FP16 model retained on capable devices for marginally higher classification accuracy; 3-second SLO met across all devices in pilot contractor fleet

target_output.title: Concrete Defect Inspection Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter mobile application for construction quality inspectors that captures smartphone photos of concrete surfaces and classifies defects (honeycombing, cracking, surface voids) using an on-device TFLite MobileNetV3 model within 3 seconds, even on low-end Android and legacy iPhone SE devices. Inspections are saved offline in SQLite and synced to a FastAPI backend on GCP Cloud Run when connectivity is available. PostgreSQL stores inspection records, defect observations, and quality hold points, while a nightly analytics job produces defect trend reports by concrete pour, formwork type, and crew.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter app with offline-first architecture using drift SQLite for local inspection storage and a background sync service that pushes pending inspections to FastAPI Cloud Run on connectivity restoration. On photo capture, tflite_service.dart runs inference on a device-selected model variant (FP16 for capable devices, INT8 quantised for legacy devices) and pre-populates the inspection form with top classification and confidence. Firebase Auth issues ID tokens verified by FastAPI middleware; Firebase custom claims carry inspector/site_engineer/quality_manager roles. GCS stores defect photos; all photo access is via FastAPI-generated signed URLs with 15-minute expiry. Firebase Remote Config signals model version updates, triggering background download of new model assets from Firebase Storage. Cloud Run backend is deployed in australia-southeast1 with min-instances=1 to eliminate cold start during morning inspection peaks.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart 3.4, tflite_flutter 0.10 plugin, drift 2.x for SQLite, firebase_auth and firebase_remote_config Flutter plugins. ML Models: TFLite MobileNetV3 FP16 (default) and INT8 quantised variants, fine-tuned on 12,000 labelled concrete defect images. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, firebase-admin 6, google-cloud-storage. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in australia-southeast1. Photo storage: GCS with Nearline class for photos > 90 days. Infrastructure: Cloud Run, Cloud SQL, GCS, Artifact Registry. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Fastlane for mobile builds.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Defect Capture: camera view with capture button; tflite_service selects FP16 or INT8 model based on startup benchmark; inference returns top-3 defect classes with confidence; DefectFormPage pre-filled if confidence > 0.65. Inspection Form: inspector reviews classification, selects concrete_pour, adds location description and notes; high-severity defects prompt quality_hold_point creation. Offline Storage: drift SQLite persists all inspections with sync_status ENUM pending/synced; sync_service retries pending queue on connectivity. Photo Management: upload via GCS signed upload URL; download via POST /photos/{id}/download-url returning 15-min signed URL. Analytics: quality_manager dashboard shows defect count heat-map by pour and severity trend by crew over configurable date range. Model Updates: Remote Config version flag triggers Firebase Storage download of new model variants with progress indicator.
target_output.system_components: Flutter mobile app (iOS and Android) with tflite_service.dart, sync_service.dart, drift SQLite database, Firebase SDK. Cloud Run FastAPI service in australia-southeast1 with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 instance with private IP. GCS bucket 'concrete-defect-photos-prod' in australia-southeast1 with Nearline lifecycle. Firebase project: Authentication, Remote Config, Crashlytics, Analytics. Artifact Registry for Cloud Run images. GitHub Actions CI with Fastlane lanes for TestFlight and Play Store internal track.
target_output.workflows: On-Device Defect Classification: inspector taps camera button on InspectionCapturePage; Flutter image_picker captures photo at 1024px width; tflite_service.dart runs inference on Isolate with selected model variant; top class and confidence returned in under 3 seconds; if confidence >= 0.65 DefectFormPage auto-populates defect_type and severity; inspector reviews, optionally overrides, adds pour_id and notes, taps Submit; drift SQLite saves inspection with sync_status=pending. Background Sync: sync_service.dart monitors connectivity via connectivity_plus; on network restore, queries drift for sync_status=pending; sends POST /inspections with idempotent client UUID; on 201 response updates sync_status=synced; photo uploaded separately via GCS signed upload URL returned in POST response. Nightly Analytics: Cloud Run scheduled job aggregates defect_observations by concrete_pour_id and crew_id for the prior 24 hours; updates defect_trend_summaries table; quality_manager dashboard queries this summary table rather than raw observations for performance.
target_output.data_model: inspection_sessions (id UUID PK, inspector_id VARCHAR, concrete_pour_id UUID FK, location_description TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, device_model VARCHAR, model_variant_used VARCHAR). defect_observations (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, defect_type VARCHAR(30), severity VARCHAR(20), model_confidence FLOAT, inspector_override BOOLEAN, override_reason TEXT, photo_gcs_path VARCHAR, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ). concrete_pours (id UUID PK, element_id VARCHAR(50), pour_date DATE, formwork_type VARCHAR(50), crew_id UUID FK, design_mix_code VARCHAR(20)). remediation_recommendations (id UUID PK, defect_type VARCHAR(30), severity VARCHAR(20), recommendation_text TEXT, code_reference VARCHAR(20)). quality_hold_points (id UUID PK, pour_id UUID FK, defect_observation_id UUID FK, status VARCHAR(20), created_by VARCHAR, cleared_by VARCHAR, cleared_at TIMESTAMPTZ). defect_trend_summaries (pour_id UUID FK, crew_id UUID, report_date DATE, defect_type VARCHAR(30), count INTEGER, avg_confidence FLOAT).
target_output.api_design: POST /inspections - create inspection session with defect_observations array; idempotent via client UUID in id field; returns 201 with session id and GCS upload URLs per photo. GET /inspections?pour_id={id}&date_from={date} - site_engineer and quality_manager roles; returns inspection list with defect counts. PATCH /inspections/{id}/defects/{defect_id} - site_engineer override of classification; requires override_reason. POST /photos/{defect_id}/download-url - returns 15-min signed GCS URL; any authenticated role. GET /analytics/defect-trends?from={date}&to={date}&by=pour|crew - quality_manager only; returns aggregated defect counts from defect_trend_summaries. GET /model-version - returns current TFLite model version and Firebase Storage download paths for FP16 and INT8 variants.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth ID token verification in FastAPI middleware; token expiry enforced (1-hour tokens with Firebase refresh). Firebase custom claims: role field set by admin Cloud Run job; claim propagated in all subsequent ID tokens. GCS photo bucket: uniform bucket-level access; no public ACLs; photos accessed only via FastAPI POST /photos/{id}/download-url returning a 15-minute signed URL; raw GCS paths never returned in any API response. Cloud Armor policy on Cloud Run: 60 req/min per Firebase UID to prevent sync storms from misconfigured clients. Cloud SQL private IP only; no public IP; Cloud Run connects via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy. TLS 1.3 on Cloud Run; enforced at load balancer.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run service in australia-southeast1 with min-instances=1, max-instances=20, concurrency=80. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 High Availability instance in australia-southeast1. GCS bucket in australia-southeast1 with Object Lifecycle Management: transition to Nearline after 90 days. GitHub Actions CI: flutter test, flutter analyze, FastAPI pytest with testcontainers for Cloud SQL, docker build, push Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy. Flutter builds: Fastlane iOS lane uploads to TestFlight for internal testing; Android lane uploads to Play Store internal track. Migration: Alembic applied as Cloud Run job before each service deployment. Feature flags via Firebase Remote Config for pilot contractor rollout.
target_output.observability: Firebase Crashlytics for Flutter crash reports with defect_type and model_variant custom keys. Google Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request latency p99 (SLO <500ms excluding TFLite inference), error rate, instance count. Custom log-based metrics in Cloud Logging: model_variant_selected (fp16 vs int8), inference_duration_ms per device_model, sync_retry_count. PagerDuty on Cloud Run error_rate > 3% and sync failure rate > 10% over 15-minute window.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $400-600/month for 50 daily active inspectors: Cloud Run (min 1 instance, ~$80), Cloud SQL db.g1-small HA (~$180), GCS (~$40 for photo storage growth), Firebase (Blaze plan, ~$50), GitHub Actions minutes (~$30). Scale: Cloud Run autoscales to 20 instances for project milestone inspection peaks; Cloud SQL read replica addable for analytics queries without impacting sync writes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (complete, rounds 1-2): Flutter app with FP16 TFLite model, offline drift SQLite, Firebase Auth, FastAPI sync API, GCS photo storage with signed URLs, Remote Config model versioning, ISS-001 and ISS-002 resolved. Phase 2 Current (round 3): INT8 quantised model variant for legacy device SLO compliance (ISS-003), device model variant selection benchmark at startup. Phase 3 Future: formwork and reinforcement defect classification, QHSE platform integration, model retraining pipeline from inspector override feedback.
target_output.development_guidelines: tflite_service.dart must log device_model, selected model_variant, and inference_duration_ms to Firebase Analytics for every inference. INT8 model generated via tf.lite.TFLiteConverter with representative dataset of 500 calibration images; accuracy delta vs FP16 validated on held-out test set (acceptable threshold: <1% mAP drop). New defect types require: updated TFLite model training, new remediation_recommendations rows, updated DefectFormPage picklist. All GCS interactions via FastAPI only; Flutter communicates with GCS exclusively via signed URLs obtained from FastAPI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: INT8 quantisation accuracy drops below acceptable threshold for surface_void class which has smaller feature footprint than honeycombing. Mitigation: quantisation-aware training rather than post-training quantisation if accuracy drop exceeds 1% mAP on surface_void test subset. Risk: model download during active tunnel inspection session exhausts device storage. Mitigation: Remote Config update prompts at app launch only; download blocked if available storage < 50MB. Tradeoff: dual model variant adds 18MB to app bundle size (FP16 + INT8 assets); acceptable given inspection accuracy and latency requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected INT8 post-training quantisation over model pruning for faster iteration; pruning would require full retraining cycle. Confirmed device benchmark at startup over compile-time device list to handle new device models without app update. Retained FP16 model for capable devices to avoid accuracy regression for inspectors with current hardware.

metadata.notes: Flutter concrete defect inspection app; round 3 addresses TFLite latency on legacy devices with INT8 quantisation; on-device inference with offline-first sync.

project_goal.value: A Go library implementing CPM and PERT scheduling algorithms for construction project networks with thousands of activities and multiple calendars, returning typed results for float values, critical paths, and resource-levelled start/finish dates for embedding in project management platforms
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates redundant CPM implementations across construction PM platform vendors; typed Go API enables safe embedding in scheduling tools

target_users.value: Go developers building construction project management platforms, scheduling engines, and ERP integrations that require CPM and PERT computation
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are software developers integrating the library into host scheduling applications

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a Go module via pkg.go.dev; no hosted service, no web frontend

capabilities.rationale: Pure computation library: scheduling algorithms and typed result structures; no frontend, auth, or persistence

complexity_level.rationale: CPM forward/backward pass with multiple calendar types, PERT three-point estimates, resource-levelling heuristics, and topological sort on activity networks with cycles detection add significant algorithmic complexity

risk_level.rationale: No hosted service, no user data, no network calls; library operates on caller-supplied data structures

data_sensitivity.rationale: Library stores no data; schedule data is the calling application's responsibility

external_exposure.rationale: Published as open-source Go module on pkg.go.dev and GitHub

access_model.value: No authentication; public Go module importable by any Go project
access_model.rationale: Open-source library with no access restrictions

feature_scope.value: CPM forward and backward pass with finish-to-start, start-to-start, finish-to-finish, and start-to-finish relationship types and lag values; PERT three-point duration estimates with statistical distribution outputs; multiple named working calendars with holiday exceptions; resource levelling heuristic (minimum float delay); typed ScheduleResult with early-start, late-finish, total-float, free-float per activity; critical path extraction; cycle detection returning typed CycleError
feature_scope.rationale: Full industry-standard CPM feature set required by commercial scheduling tools; PERT for risk analysis; calendar support for multi-region projects

mvp_scope.value: CPM forward/backward pass with finish-to-start and finish-to-finish relationships, single calendar, typed ScheduleResult, critical path extraction, cycle detection
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the dominant scheduling pattern; additional relationship types and multi-calendar deferred to post-MVP

security_baseline.value: No network calls from library; no unsafe Go pointers in algorithm implementations; go vet and staticcheck in CI; fuzz testing for malformed activity network inputs
security_baseline.rationale: Library accepts caller-supplied data; input validation and memory safety are the primary security concerns

frontend_stack.value: Not applicable - library_sdk with no UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Pure Go library; no frontend component

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 standard library only; no external runtime dependencies
backend_stack.rationale: Zero external dependencies keeps the library lightweight and avoids dependency conflicts in host applications

data_platform.value: No persistent storage; in-memory graph data structures only
data_platform.rationale: Scheduling computation is stateless; host application manages persistence

hosting_target.value: Go module published on pkg.go.dev via GitHub repository with semantic versioning
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Go module distribution; no deployment infrastructure required

privacy_retention_policy.value: Library retains no data; no privacy obligations apply
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No hosted service, no data storage

future_scope.value: Start-to-start and start-to-finish relationship types, multi-calendar support, PERT Monte Carlo simulation, resource-loaded schedule with cost aggregation, JSON/XML serialisation of ScheduleResult
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP expansion to full P6-compatible feature set

constraints.value: Zero external Go module dependencies in library itself; forward pass on 10,000-activity network must complete in under 100 ms on modern hardware; public API must be stable across minor versions
constraints.rationale: Zero-dependency constraint prevents version conflicts in host scheduling applications; performance constraint from real-time scheduling tools

observability_baseline.value: Benchmark suite using Go testing.B for CPM forward pass, backward pass, and resource levelling at 100, 1000, and 10000 activity scales; coverage via go test -cover
observability_baseline.rationale: Library quality measured by benchmark regression detection and test coverage

execution_preference.value: Go module semver release cycle; GitHub Actions CI; CHANGELOG per release; example programs in /examples directory
execution_preference.rationale: Standard Go open-source library practices

project.goal: Go CPM/PERT scheduling algorithms library for construction PM platform embedding
project.target_users: Go developers building project management and scheduling tools

backend.testing: go test, go test -bench, go vet, staticcheck, govulncheck

security.input_validation: cycle detection before CPM pass; activity count limit guard
security.fuzz: go test -fuzz for malformed network inputs

data.types: typed Go structs for all public API inputs and outputs

devops.publishing: Go module tag release, pkg.go.dev auto-indexing

constraints.zero_deps: no external Go module imports in library
constraints.api_stability: stable public API across minor versions

open_questions.monte_carlo: PERT Monte Carlo simulation deferred to v0.3.0

reasoner_reviews.product: The CPM forward/backward pass with all four relationship types is the critical feature; start-to-start relationships are common in civil infrastructure schedules with parallel concrete pours and are needed for real-world use. The resource-levelling heuristic must describe its specific algorithm choice (minimum float delay, also called least float first) to differentiate from other heuristics that produce different levelled schedules.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The internal graph representation should be an adjacency list using activity IDs as map keys for O(1) successor lookup during the forward pass. Topological sort using Kahn's algorithm detects cycles before the CPM pass begins, returning a typed CycleError listing the activity IDs forming the cycle. The resource levelling heuristic should operate on a time-indexed resource loading array rather than recalculating the full schedule on each iteration.
reasoner_reviews.security: go vet and staticcheck catch nil pointer dereferences and integer overflow in duration calculations. govulncheck should scan transitive test dependencies even though the library has zero runtime dependencies. Input guard: reject networks with more than 50,000 activities to prevent runaway computation from adversarial inputs.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Three previous rounds have not resolved the performance benchmark specification gap: the plan states benchmarks exist but does not show the benchmark function signatures, the network topology used for benchmarks (chain vs parallel vs dense), or the acceptance criteria for regression. This makes the benchmark claims unverifiable. Additionally, the resource levelling section has been vague in all three prior rounds about what 'resource limit' means in the API: is it a per-resource daily unit limit or a per-activity resource requirement vector?
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero external dependencies is a hard constraint — the library must be importable in any Go project without introducing transitive dependency conflicts. The API surface must remain stable across patch versions: no changes to Network, Activity, or ScheduleResult struct field names without a major version bump. go test -fuzz must complete a 60-second fuzzing session without panics before any release tag.

backend.service_design: Single Go module (github.com/example/cpm-go) with packages: network (ProjectNetwork, Activity, Relationship structs and validation), scheduler (CPM and PERT algorithm implementations), leveller (resource levelling heuristic), calendar (WorkCalendar with working days and holiday exceptions). No main package; library only. All packages expose only typed public functions and structs.
backend.api_patterns: Public API: func Compute(net *network.ProjectNetwork, cal *calendar.WorkCalendar) (*ScheduleResult, error). func ComputePERT(net *network.ProjectNetwork, cal *calendar.WorkCalendar) (*PERTResult, error). func LevelResources(result *ScheduleResult, constraints []ResourceConstraint) (*LevelledSchedule, error). All return typed errors: *CycleError (activity_ids []string), *ValidationError (field, message). ScheduleResult contains per-activity ActivitySchedule{early_start, early_finish, late_start, late_finish, total_float, free_float, is_critical}.
backend.failure_handling: CycleError returned by Compute if topological sort detects a cycle; lists activity IDs in the cycle. ValidationError returned for: zero-activity network, activity with duration < 0, relationship referencing non-existent activity IDs, activity count > 50000. LevelResources returns ErrInfeasible if no feasible levelled schedule exists within resource limits and available float.
backend.backend_risks: Float calculation precision with large lag values and long-duration activities. Mitigation: use int64 minutes internally; avoid float64 duration arithmetic. Resource levelling may produce suboptimal results for dense networks; document that the minimum-float-first heuristic does not guarantee globally optimal levelling.

security.auth_design: No authentication; caller-supplied data only
security.secrets_management: No secrets; GitHub Actions uses GITHUB_TOKEN for Go module release tag
security.abuse_prevention: Input guard rejects networks with >50,000 activities. go test -fuzz harness exercises Compute() with randomly mutated ProjectNetwork inputs to surface panics on malformed activity ID strings and relationship arrays.
security.security_risks: Integer overflow in duration + lag addition for very long projects. Mitigation: validate that individual activity duration and lag values do not exceed int32 max before promoting to int64 for calculation.

data.entities: ProjectNetwork (project_id STRING, activities []Activity, data_date DATE, default_calendar_id STRING). Activity (id STRING, name STRING, duration INT minutes, duration_type ENUM fixed_duration/fixed_units, calendar_id STRING, resource_requirements []ResourceRequirement). ActivityRelationship (predecessor_id STRING, successor_id STRING, relationship_type ENUM fs/ff/ss/sf, lag INT minutes). WorkCalendar (id STRING, name STRING, working_days []Weekday, exceptions []CalendarException). ScheduleResult (project_id STRING, computed_at TIME, activities []ActivitySchedule, critical_path []STRING activity IDs, project_duration INT minutes). ActivitySchedule (activity_id STRING, early_start TIME, early_finish TIME, late_start TIME, late_finish TIME, total_float INT minutes, free_float INT minutes, is_critical BOOL). PERTResult (activities []PERTActivity where each has expected_duration, variance, std_deviation). ResourceConstraint (resource_id STRING, daily_units INT). LevelledSchedule (activities []LevelledActivity with levelled_start, levelled_finish, delay INT minutes).
data.schema_notes: All durations represented as int64 minutes internally to avoid float precision issues. Activity IDs are caller-assigned strings; the library validates uniqueness. Calendar exceptions are date ranges (start_date, end_date, description) stored as slices; overlap detection is performed during calendar validation.
data.data_risks: Caller-supplied activity ID strings with Unicode or special characters could cause map key collisions if the host application uses case-insensitive comparison. Document that library uses exact string equality for activity ID matching.

devops.deployment_topology: No deployment infrastructure. Go module hosted on GitHub; pkg.go.dev indexes automatically on version tag creation.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on every PR and main push: go vet ./..., staticcheck ./..., govulncheck ./..., go test -race ./... with -count=1, go test -cover with 85% gate, go test -bench=. -benchtime=5s -benchmem with regression check using benchstat against main branch baseline (fail if p50 forward-pass time regresses >15%), go test -fuzz=FuzzCompute -fuzztime=30s. Release on version tag: gh release create, Go module available on pkg.go.dev within minutes via proxy cache refresh.
devops.devops_risks: Benchmark regression detection requires storing baseline benchstat results as GitHub Actions artifact; artifact retention default of 90 days must be set to avoid losing baseline after inactivity.

ISS-001.title: Benchmark specification gap: topology and acceptance criteria not defined
ISS-001.detail: Across rounds 1-3 the plan asserts that benchmarks exist but has never specified the network topology (chain vs parallel vs sparse vs dense) used for the 10K-activity benchmark, the benchmark function signature, or whether the 100 ms acceptance criterion is measured on p50, p95, or worst-case. This makes the performance claim unverifiable and leaves CI benchmark regression detection underspecified.

This creates ambiguity for platform developers embedding the library.

The flawed quality is achieved by producing a plan that still fails to fully resolve the benchmark specification (ISS-001) and resource levelling semantics (ISS-002) despite the fix_report claiming to address them - the fix_report mentions adding topology specification but the observability section still describes the benchmark vaguely without the network generation code snippet, and the api_design section resolves daily_units but then contradicts itself in the data_model by using the old ambiguous description. This represents a convergence failure pattern where the architect partially addresses issues without fully closing them.
