target_output.title: Go CPM/PERT Scheduling Library - Round 4
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-dependency Go module (github.com/example/cpm-go) implementing CPM critical path scheduling and PERT three-point estimation for construction activity-on-node networks with up to 50,000 activities. The forward and backward pass compute early-start, late-finish, total-float, and free-float per activity using Kahn topological sort for cycle detection. A minimum-float-first resource levelling heuristic delays non-critical activities within their float to resolve daily resource peaks. Typed errors CycleError and ValidationError provide structured failure information for host application error handling.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Go module with four internal packages. network package defines ProjectNetwork, Activity, ActivityRelationship, and WorkCalendar structs and performs input validation including cycle detection via Kahn topological sort. scheduler package implements the CPM forward pass (earliest early-start and early-finish propagation through the topological order) and backward pass (latest late-start and late-finish back-propagation from project end). leveller package implements the minimum-float-first heuristic on a time-indexed int[] resource loading array. calendar package implements WorkCalendar with working-day lookup and holiday exception handling. All packages use only Go standard library; no external module imports. Zero-dependency constraint verified by go mod graph in CI.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 standard library only. No external dependencies. Development tooling: go vet, staticcheck, govulncheck, go test -race, go test -fuzz, benchstat for benchmark regression comparison. Distribution: Go module on GitHub, auto-indexed to pkg.go.dev on semantic version tag. go test -fuzz targets the critical-path calculator with random activity networks to detect panics on malformed input. go generate invokes stringer for ActivityType and RelationshipType enum string methods. Module tagged as v1 to signal stable API.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CPM Scheduler: forward pass computes early_start and early_finish for each activity in topological order accounting for all predecessor relationships with lag; backward pass computes late_start and late_finish from project end date backward. Float Computation: total_float = late_finish - early_finish; free_float = min(successor early_start) - early_finish. Critical Path Extraction: returns ordered slice of activity IDs where total_float == 0. PERT Analysis: for each activity with optimistic/most_likely/pessimistic durations, computes expected = (o+4m+p)/6 and variance = ((p-o)/6)^2; PERTResult.ProjectCompletionProbability computes z-score for target date using sum of critical path variances. Resource Levelling: minimum-float-first heuristic delays non-critical activities within their float to resolve daily resource peaks below ResourceConstraint.daily_units (peak concurrent units) limits. Cycle Detection: Kahn topological sort returns CycleError listing cycle activity IDs before any schedule computation begins.
target_output.system_components: network package (input validation, topological sort, cycle detection). scheduler package (CPM forward and backward pass). leveller package (resource levelling heuristic with time-indexed loading array). calendar package (working day calculation with holiday exceptions). examples/ directory with annotated programs demonstrating Compute, ComputePERT, and LevelResources usage. benchmarks/ directory with BenchmarkForwardPass100, BenchmarkForwardPass1K, BenchmarkForwardPass10K benchmark functions.
target_output.workflows: CPM Computation: host application constructs ProjectNetwork with Activity structs (id, duration in minutes, resource_requirements) and ActivityRelationship structs (predecessor_id, successor_id, relationship_type=fs, lag=0). Calls network.Validate(net) which runs Kahn topological sort; returns *CycleError if cycle detected. Calls scheduler.Compute(net, cal) which performs forward pass then backward pass; returns *ScheduleResult with ActivitySchedule slice. Host application iterates result.Activities to render Gantt bars with early_start and late_finish dates. Resource Levelling: host application constructs []ResourceConstraint where each constraint specifies resource_id and daily_units (peak concurrent units; e.g., daily_units=4 means at most 4 units of that resource may be in-progress simultaneously on any calendar day). Calls leveller.LevelResources(result, constraints). Library iterates time periods in project duration, for each period sums resource units of in-progress activities, delays the non-critical activity with least float when sum exceeds daily_units. Returns *LevelledSchedule with per-activity levelled_start, levelled_finish, and delay in minutes.
target_output.data_model: ProjectNetwork: project_id STRING, activities []Activity, data_date time.Time, default_calendar_id STRING. Activity: id STRING (unique within network), name STRING, duration int64 (minutes), duration_type ENUM fixed_duration/fixed_units, calendar_id STRING, resource_requirements []ResourceRequirement where ResourceRequirement is {resource_id STRING, units int (number of concurrent units required while activity is in progress)}. ActivityRelationship: predecessor_id STRING, successor_id STRING, relationship_type ENUM fs/ff/ss/sf, lag int64 (minutes). ScheduleResult: project_id, computed_at time.Time, activities []ActivitySchedule, critical_path []string, project_duration int64. ActivitySchedule: activity_id, early_start time.Time, early_finish time.Time, late_start time.Time, late_finish time.Time, total_float int64, free_float int64, is_critical bool. ResourceConstraint: resource_id STRING, daily_units int. NOTE: daily_units means maximum concurrent resource units assigned to in-progress activities; this is not a daily unit-hour budget. PERTResult: activities []PERTActivity, critical_path_variance float64, project_completion_probability func(target time.Time) float64. ResourceConstraint semantics are clarified in the api section but the struct tag comment in this section still says 'total daily resource budget' which contradicts the corrected api_design definition - this is the intentional flaw for training.
target_output.api_design: Public package API. network.Validate(net *ProjectNetwork) error returns *CycleError or *ValidationError. scheduler.Compute(net *ProjectNetwork, cal *calendar.WorkCalendar) (*ScheduleResult, error). scheduler.ComputePERT(net *ProjectNetwork, cal *calendar.WorkCalendar) (*PERTResult, error). leveller.LevelResources(result *ScheduleResult, constraints []ResourceConstraint) (*LevelledSchedule, error). ResourceConstraint.daily_units: maximum concurrent units of the specified resource that may be assigned to in-progress activities simultaneously on any calendar day. Example: ResourceConstraint{resource_id: 'tower_crane', daily_units: 2} means at most 2 tower cranes may be active on the same calendar day. PERTResult.ProjectCompletionProbability(target time.Time) float64 returns probability (0.0-1.0) that the project finishes by target using normal distribution approximation over critical path variance sum.
target_output.security_and_compliance: go vet ./... and staticcheck ./... run on every PR; both tools catch nil dereferences and integer overflow risks in duration arithmetic. govulncheck ./... checks transitive test dependencies for known CVEs. Input validation in network.Validate rejects: networks with >50,000 activities (returns ValidationError), activities with duration < 0, relationships referencing non-existent activity IDs, duplicate activity IDs. go test -fuzz=FuzzCompute exercises Compute with mutated ProjectNetwork including nil activity slices, empty ID strings, and negative lag values. No unsafe package used anywhere in the module.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on every PR: go vet ./..., staticcheck ./..., govulncheck ./..., go test -race ./... -count=1, go test -cover ./... with 85% gate, go test -bench=. -benchtime=5s -benchmem outputs benchmark results stored as artifact, benchstat comparison against main branch baseline artifact fails build if BenchmarkForwardPass10K p50 regresses >15%. Benchmark network generation for BenchmarkForwardPass10K: uses math/rand with fixed seed 42 to generate a random precedence network with N=10000 activities and edge density 0.05 (approximately 500,000 finish-to-start relationships), duration uniformly distributed 1-30 days. The 100 ms acceptance criterion is defined as p99 wall-clock time measured across 5-second benchtime runs on a Linux x86_64 GitHub Actions runner. go test -fuzz=FuzzCompute -fuzztime=30s on PR to main. Release: gh release create vX.Y.Z triggers pkg.go.dev proxy cache refresh automatically.
target_output.observability: go test -cover report published as GitHub Actions artifact on each run. benchstat comparison between PR benchmark results and main branch baseline detects regressions in BenchmarkForwardPass100, BenchmarkForwardPass1K, BenchmarkForwardPass10K. Benchmark acceptance: BenchmarkForwardPass10K mean wall-clock must remain under 100 ms; this threshold is verified by a post-bench script parsing benchstat output. No runtime observability for embedded library; host application is responsible for tracing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI minutes for a pure Go library. pkg.go.dev and GitHub storage are free for open-source. At 10,000-activity networks, memory allocation is approximately 80 MB for the full ScheduleResult; document this in README for host applications with constrained heap.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): CPM forward/backward pass with finish-to-start and finish-to-finish relationships, single WorkCalendar, typed ScheduleResult, CycleError and ValidationError, critical path extraction, go test coverage 85%, benchmark suite with RPG topology, pkg.go.dev publish. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Start-to-start and start-to-finish relationship types, multi-calendar support, resource levelling heuristic with ResourceConstraint. Phase 3 (6 weeks): PERT three-point estimation with PERTResult and ProjectCompletionProbability, PERT Monte Carlo simulation option, JSON serialisation of ScheduleResult.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public types must have godoc comments with example usage. New relationship type support requires: updated topological sort tests, forward pass unit test with at least one positive-lag and one negative-lag case, backward pass unit test, and integration test with a 50-activity network of mixed relationship types. Duration arithmetic must use int64 minutes throughout; no float64 duration types in internal packages. Benchmark functions must use the RPG generator from internal/testdata/rpg.go with fixed seed for reproducibility. API breaking changes require major version bump per Go module compatibility rules.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: minimum-float-first resource levelling produces suboptimal levelled schedules for dense networks with many critical activities leaving little float for delay. Mitigation: document that the heuristic does not guarantee globally optimal levelling; recommend using LevelResources output as a starting point for manual adjustment in the host application. Risk: activity ID string map performance degrades with very long ID strings. Mitigation: document recommended practice of using short numeric-string IDs (e.g., 'A001') rather than UUIDs. Tradeoff: zero external dependencies over using a graph library like gonum increases implementation burden but eliminates version conflict risks in host scheduling platforms that may already depend on gonum with a different version.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved benchmark topology: RPG with N=10000, density=0.05, fixed seed 42 using math/rand. Resolved 100 ms criterion: p99 wall-clock on Linux x86_64 GitHub Actions runner. Resolved daily_units semantics: peak concurrent units not unit-hours (though data_model section comment contains residual contradiction as noted above - intentional flaw). PERT Monte Carlo deferred to Phase 3. Start-to-start relationships deferred to Phase 2.

metadata.notes: Round 4 CPM/PERT library plan with intentional flaw: fix_report claims to resolve ISS-002 resource levelling semantics but data_model section retains contradictory comment calling daily_units a total daily budget; represents convergence failure pattern for ArchitectAgent training.

project_goal.value: AI-powered construction daily report generation system replacing manual site diary writing by processing voice memos and structured field inputs from site supervisors, using an LLM to draft formal daily site reports compliant with AS 4000 contract requirements, including progress summaries, weather impacts, delays, and workforce counts
project_goal.rationale: Site supervisors spend 45-60 minutes per day writing site diaries; LLM drafting reduces this to 5-10 minute review-and-approve

target_users.value: Site supervisors and project managers on civil infrastructure and commercial construction projects under AS 4000 general conditions of contract
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in client discovery; AS 4000 compliance is a contractual obligation on all target projects

project_class.rationale: LLM-powered report drafting is the core value; AI orchestration pipeline with human review is the defining architecture pattern

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Voice transcription API, LLM drafting, structured field inputs, report approval workflow, and analytics on delay patterns

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-step AI pipeline: speech-to-text, structured data extraction, LLM report drafting with AS 4000 context injection, human approval workflow

risk_level.rationale: Confidential project data; AI-generated reports require human approval before formal submission; no financial transactions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Daily site reports contain commercially sensitive progress data, delay events, and workforce information

external_exposure.rationale: Internal project team platform; all access requires Auth0 authentication

access_model.value: RBAC: site_supervisor (submit field inputs, record voice memos, review and approve AI drafts), project_manager (view all reports, approve final submissions, access analytics), admin (project and user configuration)
access_model.rationale: Site supervisors are the primary data creators; project managers have oversight and formal submission authority

feature_scope.value: Voice memo recording and transcription, structured field input form (weather, headcount, activities, delays), LLM report drafting with AS 4000 clause alignment, supervisor review and edit interface, project manager approval workflow, monthly analytics on delay events and weather impact days, PDF export of approved reports
feature_scope.rationale: Full daily report lifecycle from voice input to formal approved document

mvp_scope.value: Structured field input form, LLM report drafting from field data, supervisor review and approve flow, PostgreSQL storage, PDF export, Auth0 SSO
mvp_scope.rationale: Voice transcription requires separate vendor evaluation; LLM drafting from structured data delivers core value without voice dependency

security_baseline.value: Auth0 with MFA, TLS 1.3, encrypted S3 for audio files, no raw site data sent to public LLM APIs without project data masking policy
security_baseline.rationale: Site diary content includes contractor names, progress details, and delay claims -- confidential data requiring masking before external LLM submission

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript; mobile-responsive layout for tablet use on site
frontend_stack.rationale: Site supervisors use iPads on site; mobile-responsive React avoids a separate mobile app build

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI; LangChain for LLM orchestration; Whisper API for speech-to-text transcription
backend_stack.rationale: Python LangChain ecosystem provides structured output parsing for LLM report drafting; FastAPI async handles concurrent transcription jobs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for structured report data; S3 for audio recordings and approved PDF reports; Redis 7 for LLM job queuing
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for report approval workflow; S3 lifecycle management for audio file retention policy

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: Containerised FastAPI with auto-scaling for LLM inference jobs during morning site diary submission peaks

privacy_retention_policy.value: Daily reports retained 7 years per AS 4000 record-keeping requirements; voice_memos retained 90 days then purged from S3; LLM prompts with site data not logged by provider (enterprise API contract required)
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: AS 4000 clause 47 requires contractor records retention; voice recordings are operationally transient and purged to limit confidential data exposure

future_scope.value: Integration with project management platforms (Aconex, Procore), mobile voice recording app, LLM-generated delay variation draft letters, predictive delay pattern detection
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap items requiring additional procurement and security review

constraints.value: LLM provider must offer enterprise data privacy agreement (no training on customer data); audio transcription within 60 seconds; report draft must be reviewable within 2 minutes of field input submission
constraints.rationale: Site supervisors need to complete the diary process before the morning tool-box meeting ends

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on Fargate tasks; custom metrics for LLM draft generation latency p95, transcription success rate, and daily report approval rate
observability_baseline.rationale: LLM pipeline latency is the primary user-facing performance concern; approval rate tracks adoption quality

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints with site supervisor feedback sessions after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: LLM prompt quality requires iterative refinement based on real site supervisor feedback on draft accuracy

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o via LangChain with structured output parsing (Pydantic models); AS 4000 clause context injected into system prompt; project-specific entity masking applied before prompt construction
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o provides sufficient instruction-following for AS 4000 report structure; LangChain structured output ensures parseable JSON report sections

compliance_context.value: AS 4000 general conditions of contract record-keeping requirements for daily site diaries; enterprise OpenAI API data privacy agreement prohibiting training on customer data
compliance_context.rationale: Risk medium with confidential data triggers compliance context; AS 4000 is the primary contractual compliance driver

project.goal: Replace manual AS 4000 daily site diary writing with AI-drafted reports reviewed by supervisors

frontend.layout: Mobile-responsive for iPad use on site
frontend.state: TanStack Query, Zustand for draft editing state

backend.ai: LangChain + OpenAI GPT-4o for report drafting, Whisper API for transcription

security.data_privacy: Entity masking before LLM prompt, enterprise OpenAI API agreement

open_questions.masking_approach: Entity masking via NER or simple regex substitution?

reasoner_reviews.product: The 2-minute report draft SLA is the key product promise: if drafts take longer, supervisors will abandon the tool and revert to manual writing. The LangChain structured output with Pydantic parsing is the right approach to ensure the LLM produces parseable sections rather than free-form prose that the review UI cannot render. The AS 4000 compliance framing in the system prompt is a significant differentiator: the prompt must include the specific contractual clause requirements for daily diary content (clause 47 activities, weather impacts, delay notices). Plan must specify the prompt engineering strategy, not just mention GPT-4o.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with async LangChain calls is the right pattern: each report generation job should be processed as a background task using FastAPI BackgroundTasks or a Celery/Redis queue, returning a job_id immediately so the browser can poll for completion. The entity masking layer must run before prompt construction: replace project names, contractor names, and ABNs with generic tokens (PROJECT_A, SUBCONTRACTOR_1) before sending to OpenAI. LangChain's output parser with a Pydantic DailySiteReportDraft model should enforce all required AS 4000 sections as non-optional fields.
reasoner_reviews.security: Enterprise OpenAI API agreement prohibiting training on customer data must be in place before any production data reaches the LLM. Entity masking via spaCy NER is more reliable than regex for organisation names. Whisper audio files must be transmitted over TLS and not retained by OpenAI beyond the API request. S3 audio files encrypted at rest with KMS; 90-day lifecycle rule purges voice_memos. Audit trail must record which LLM model version generated each draft.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 2-minute draft SLA is achievable with GPT-4o: structured field inputs produce prompts of approximately 800 tokens; GPT-4o responds in under 30 seconds for this payload. Whisper transcription of a 3-minute voice memo takes approximately 10-15 seconds. Total pipeline under 60 seconds. Voice transcription must be async so the UI can accept structured field inputs while transcription runs in parallel.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan must specify the exact LangChain chain structure: a system prompt with AS 4000 context, a human message with the masked field inputs and transcription, and a structured output parser targeting DailySiteReportDraft Pydantic model. Without this, the LLM integration spec is underspecified. Also ensure the human review interface shows a diff between the original field inputs and the LLM draft so supervisors can identify any hallucinated content.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: /reports (daily report CRUD, draft generation trigger, approval workflow), /audio (voice memo upload, transcription trigger), /projects (project and user management), /analytics (delay and weather trend queries). LLM orchestration module uses LangChain LCEL chain: masking_transform | prompt_template | ChatOpenAI(model=gpt-4o) | PydanticOutputParser(DailySiteReportDraft). Report generation runs as a FastAPI BackgroundTask; job_id returned immediately; browser polls /reports/{id}/status.
backend.llm_integration_design: LangChain LCEL pipeline: 1) Entity masking via spaCy NER replaces PROJECT, ORG, and PERSON entities with generic tokens stored in a per-report masking_map. 2) Prompt construction: system prompt includes AS 4000 clause 47 daily diary requirements and the DailySiteReportDraft JSON schema. Human message contains masked field data (weather, headcount, activities, delay_events) plus Whisper transcription if available. 3) ChatOpenAI call with temperature=0.3. 4) PydanticOutputParser parses JSON into DailySiteReportDraft with sections: work_completed, resources_deployed, weather_impact, delays_and_causes, safety_observations, outstanding_items. 5) Token substitution restores original entity names before storage.
backend.background_jobs: Voice transcription: S3 event triggers transcription Lambda that calls Whisper API, stores transcript in voice_memos.transcript_text, and marks transcription_status complete. Report generation: FastAPI BackgroundTask runs the LangChain chain, stores draft in daily_reports.draft_json, updates status to draft_ready. Monthly analytics: first-day-of-month cron aggregates delay_events by cause category and weather_records by impact type for the trailing 30 days, stores analytics_snapshots.
backend.backend_risks: GPT-4o hallucination risk: LLM may invent progress quantities not present in field inputs. Mitigation: reviewer diff view highlights LLM-added content vs field input sources. Masking token collision: if entity masking produces duplicate tokens across different entities, unmasking corrupts names. Mitigation: masking_map keyed by entity span with unique integer suffixes.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with routes: /projects (project list), /reports/new (field input form + voice recorder), /reports/:id/review (LLM draft review and edit), /reports/:id/pdf (approved report PDF preview), /analytics (delay and weather trend charts).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Field Input Form: weather dropdown, headcount counter by trade, activities checklist with free-text notes per activity, delay event entry with cause category and duration, voice memo recorder with upload progress. After submit: spinner with estimated draft time; redirect to review page when status is draft_ready. Review Interface: side-by-side layout with submitted field inputs on left and LLM draft sections on right; supervisor edits each section inline; diff highlights LLM-added text not in field inputs. Approve button submits for project manager review.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for report status polling (/reports/:id/status every 5 seconds while generating). Zustand for draft edit state (unsaved section edits). React Hook Form for field input form with Zod validation.
frontend.frontend_risks: Draft review UI must handle LLM output that occasionally returns malformed JSON; display raw text fallback if Pydantic parsing fails at the backend. Voice recorder requires getUserMedia permission on iPad Safari; test permission flow explicitly.

security.auth_design: Auth0 OIDC with PKCE in React; JWT validated by FastAPI middleware via Auth0 JWKS. MFA enforced via Auth0 policy for site_supervisor and project_manager roles.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: site_supervisor can create and review own daily_reports; project_manager can view and approve all reports for their project; admin has full access. FastAPI dependency injection enforces per-route role checks.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Each daily_report record stores llm_model_version, llm_prompt_hash, and masking_map_id for audit trail. Report approvals logged with approver_id, action ENUM (approved/returned_for_revision), and timestamp. LLM API call duration and token counts logged to Datadog without prompt content.

data.entities: projects (id UUID PK, name TEXT, contract_type ENUM as4000/as2124/other, project_manager_id FK, start_date DATE). voice_memos (id UUID PK, report_id FK, s3_key TEXT, duration_seconds INT, transcription_status ENUM pending/complete/failed, transcript_text TEXT, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). weather_records (id UUID PK, report_id FK, conditions ENUM clear/overcast/light_rain/heavy_rain/wind/extreme, temperature_c REAL, impact_level ENUM none/minor/significant/work_stopped). workforce_counts (id UUID PK, report_id FK, trade TEXT, headcount INT). delay_events (id UUID PK, report_id FK, cause_category ENUM weather/materials/access/design_query/subcontractor/other, description TEXT, duration_hours REAL, notified_superintendent BOOL). daily_reports (id UUID PK, project_id FK, report_date DATE, submitted_by_id FK, status ENUM draft_pending/draft_ready/supervisor_reviewed/approved/submitted, draft_json JSONB, final_content TEXT, llm_model_version TEXT, masking_map_id UUID FK, approved_by_id FK, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_s3_key TEXT). masking_maps (id UUID PK, report_id FK, entity_map JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for structured report data and approval workflow. S3 with KMS encryption for voice_memos (90-day lifecycle rule) and approved PDF reports (7-year retention). Redis 7 for report generation job queue.
data.retention_and_deletion: daily_reports and approved PDFs retained 7 years per AS 4000. voice_memos S3 lifecycle deletes after 90 days; transcript_text retained with report. masking_maps retained with report for audit trail.

devops.deployment_topology: Two ECS Fargate task definitions: api-task (FastAPI application, LangChain report generation BackgroundTasks) and worker-task (Celery worker for analytics cron, future-scale LLM job queue). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis. ALB with WAF.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest (LangChain chain unit tests with OpenAI mocked via pytest-mock), mypy type checking, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Separate test suite for LLM output validation: golden-file tests compare DailySiteReportDraft parsed output against expected section structure for 20 representative field input fixtures.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on Fargate tasks. Custom metrics: report_generation_duration_seconds p50/p95 (target p95 under 90 seconds), transcription_success_rate, draft_approval_rate (% of drafts approved without supervisor edit), llm_api_errors_per_hour.
devops.devops_risks: LLM API rate limits during morning submission peak (all supervisors submit within 30-minute window). Mitigation: Redis queue with 10 concurrent LLM workers; queue depth alert at 50 jobs. OpenAI API outage plan: cache last-used model response templates as fallback; notify supervisors to use manual entry mode.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an AI system for AS 4000 daily site report generation. Key decisions: LangChain LCEL with PydanticOutputParser for structured GPT-4o output ensuring all AS 4000 diary sections are present; spaCy NER-based entity masking before prompt construction to protect confidential project data; FastAPI BackgroundTask pattern with Redis job queue and polling endpoint for the 2-minute draft SLA; Auth0 OIDC with MFA; S3 KMS + 90-day lifecycle for voice memos. Strong quality with detailed LLM orchestration spec.
target_output.title: AS 4000 Daily Site Report AI Generation Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An AI-powered daily site report platform for civil and commercial construction projects that replaces manual AS 4000 site diary writing. Site supervisors submit structured field inputs (weather, workforce counts, activities, delays) and optional voice memos via a mobile-responsive React 18 frontend; a Python 3.12 FastAPI backend processes inputs through a LangChain LCEL pipeline that applies spaCy NER entity masking, injects AS 4000 clause 47 requirements as system context, and calls GPT-4o to draft a structured DailySiteReportDraft Pydantic model. Supervisors review and edit the draft in under 2 minutes; project managers approve final reports which are stored in PostgreSQL and exported to PDF. Monthly analytics surface delay cause distributions and weather impact day trends.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 mobile-responsive SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to a FastAPI application on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. Auth0 OIDC secures all endpoints. Report generation is async: the supervisor submits field inputs via POST /reports, FastAPI stores the draft_pending record, enqueues a report generation BackgroundTask, and returns report_id immediately. The React frontend polls /reports/:id/status every 5 seconds; when status transitions to draft_ready the supervisor is redirected to the review interface. The LangChain LCEL chain runs in the BackgroundTask: spaCy NER masks confidential entities, a structured prompt template injects AS 4000 clause 47 requirements, ChatOpenAI GPT-4o generates the draft, PydanticOutputParser enforces DailySiteReportDraft schema, and entity names are restored from the masking_map before storing draft_json to PostgreSQL. Voice memo transcription runs in parallel via an S3-triggered Lambda calling Whisper API, completing within 60 seconds. Approved reports are rendered to PDF by a WeasyPrint worker and stored in S3.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query (status polling), Zustand (draft edit state), React Hook Form + Zod (field input validation). Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, LangChain LCEL (LangChain 0.2), ChatOpenAI GPT-4o, PydanticOutputParser, spaCy 3.7 (NER entity masking), OpenAI Whisper API (transcription), WeasyPrint (PDF generation), SQLAlchemy 2.0 + asyncpg, redis-py. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ), Redis 7 (ElastiCache). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, Lambda (transcription trigger), ALB with WAF, CloudFront, S3 + KMS, Auth0, GitHub Actions, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Field Input Form: weather conditions, temperature, headcount by trade, activity items with notes, delay_events with AS 4000 cause categories (weather/materials/access/design_query/subcontractor), voice memo recorder with S3 upload. LLM Report Drafting: LangChain pipeline produces DailySiteReportDraft with sections work_completed, resources_deployed, weather_impact, delays_and_causes, safety_observations, outstanding_items -- each section aligned to AS 4000 clause 47 requirements. Review Interface: side-by-side field inputs vs LLM draft with diff highlighting of LLM-added content; inline section editing. Approval Workflow: supervisor approves or returns for revision; project manager performs final approval; approved report locked and PDF generated. Analytics: monthly breakdown of delay_events by cause category, weather impact days by month, average time-to-approval per project.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN for React SPA, ALB with WAF, ECS Fargate api-task (FastAPI + LangChain + spaCy NER), ECS Fargate worker-task (analytics cron, PDF generation queue), Lambda function (S3 voice memo upload trigger, Whisper API transcription), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache (job queue + session cache), S3 with KMS (voice_memos 90-day lifecycle + approved PDF 7-year retention), Auth0 tenant (OIDC with MFA), OpenAI GPT-4o API (enterprise data privacy agreement), Datadog APM.
target_output.workflows: Daily Report Submission and AI Drafting: Site supervisor opens the New Report page on iPad, fills the structured field input form (weather conditions, 3 trade headcount rows, 5 activity items with notes, 1 delay event with AS 4000 cause category), optionally records a 2-minute voice memo which uploads to S3 triggering the transcription Lambda. Supervisor submits the form; FastAPI stores daily_report with status draft_pending, enqueues a BackgroundTask, returns report_id. BackgroundTask runs the LangChain chain: spaCy NER identifies ORG and PERSON entities in the text, replaces them with tokens (PROJECT_A, SUBCONTRACTOR_1), stores the masking_map. Prompt is constructed with the AS 4000 clause 47 system context and masked field data plus Whisper transcript if available; ChatOpenAI GPT-4o call returns a JSON payload; PydanticOutputParser validates against DailySiteReportDraft schema; original entity names restored from masking_map; draft_json saved to PostgreSQL; status updated to draft_ready. Browser poll detects draft_ready status and redirects supervisor to review page within 90 seconds of submission. Report Approval: Supervisor reviews side-by-side diff, edits the weather_impact section to correct a hallucinated rainfall figure, clicks Approve; record transitions to supervisor_reviewed. Notification dispatched via SES to project manager. Project manager opens report, reviews, clicks Final Approve; status set to approved; WeasyPrint renders PDF from final_content, uploads to S3; pdf_s3_key stored; report locked from further edits.
target_output.data_model: projects (id UUID PK, name TEXT, contract_type ENUM as4000/as2124/other, project_manager_id UUID FK, start_date DATE). voice_memos (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, s3_key TEXT, duration_seconds INT, transcription_status ENUM pending/complete/failed, transcript_text TEXT, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). weather_records (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, conditions ENUM clear/overcast/light_rain/heavy_rain/wind/extreme, temperature_c REAL, impact_level ENUM none/minor/significant/work_stopped). workforce_counts (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, trade TEXT, headcount INT). delay_events (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, cause_category ENUM weather/materials/access/design_query/subcontractor/other, description TEXT, duration_hours REAL, notified_superintendent BOOL). daily_reports (id UUID PK, project_id UUID FK, report_date DATE, submitted_by_id UUID FK, status ENUM draft_pending/draft_ready/supervisor_reviewed/approved/submitted, draft_json JSONB, final_content TEXT, llm_model_version TEXT, masking_map_id UUID FK, approved_by_id UUID FK, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_s3_key TEXT). masking_maps (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, entity_map JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). report_templates (id UUID PK, contract_type ENUM as4000/as2124/other, system_prompt_text TEXT, required_sections TEXT[], version INT, active BOOL).
target_output.api_design: REST (Auth0 JWT required): POST /reports (submit field inputs, returns {report_id, status}); GET /reports/:id/status (poll for draft_ready); GET /reports/:id/draft (retrieve DailySiteReportDraft JSON for review UI); PATCH /reports/:id/sections/:sectionKey (supervisor edit of a draft section); POST /reports/:id/approve (supervisor approval, transitions to supervisor_reviewed); POST /reports/:id/final-approve (project manager final approval, triggers PDF generation); GET /reports/:id/pdf (pre-signed S3 URL for approved PDF). POST /audio/upload-url (returns pre-signed S3 upload URL for voice memo); GET /analytics/delays?project_id=&months=3 (delay cause breakdown); GET /analytics/weather?project_id=&months=3 (weather impact days trend).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OIDC with PKCE; MFA enforced for all roles via Auth0 policy. JWT validated by FastAPI dependency via Auth0 JWKS with 15-minute token cache. Entity masking using spaCy NER en_core_web_trf model applied before any text reaches the OpenAI API; masking_map stored per report for audit and unmasking. Enterprise OpenAI API agreement prohibits training on customer prompts; verified via OpenAI enterprise data processing addendum. Whisper audio files transmitted over TLS; not retained by OpenAI beyond the synchronous request. S3 KMS encryption for voice memos and PDFs. RBAC: site_supervisor can only access reports for their assigned project; enforced in FastAPI route dependencies. Audit log in daily_reports table: llm_model_version and masking_map_id recorded per draft for future forensic review.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest with OpenAI mocked via respx, mypy type checks, Docker multi-stage build (api-task image includes spaCy model download), ECR push. LLM golden-file tests: 20 field input fixtures with expected DailySiteReportDraft section structure validated at model-load time in CI. ECS rolling deploy with health check against /health. Environments: dev (single Fargate task, RDS Single-AZ), staging (Multi-AZ, auto-deploy on main merge, Playwright E2E with mocked OpenAI responses), production (Multi-AZ, manual approval gate, ECS auto-scaling 2-8 tasks on CPU 60% target, ALB target group scaling for morning submission peak). Secrets via AWS Secrets Manager: OpenAI API key, Auth0 client secret, DB credentials with 30-day rotation. WeasyPrint PDF generation runs in worker-task to isolate heavy rendering from API latency.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces each FastAPI request with child spans for: spaCy NER masking duration, LangChain chain execution time, OpenAI API call duration and token counts (input/output separately), PydanticOutputParser success/failure. Custom Datadog metrics: report_generation_duration_seconds (histogram, p50/p95 target under 90 seconds), transcription_success_rate (Whisper API success vs timeout), draft_approval_rate (drafts approved without supervisor section edit vs total), llm_parse_failure_rate (PydanticOutputParser failures per day). PagerDuty alerts: p95 generation latency above 120 seconds, Whisper transcription failure rate above 5%, OpenAI API error rate above 2%, Redis queue depth above 50 pending jobs during business hours.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated USD 2800/month for 50 supervisors submitting daily reports: OpenAI GPT-4o at 1000 tokens per report x 50 reports/day x 30 days x USD 0.005/1K tokens USD 7.50/day = USD 225/month. Whisper API at 3 minutes audio x 50 x 30 x USD 0.006/min = USD 27/month. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium USD 180/month. ECS Fargate 3 tasks USD 150/month. Datadog Pro USD 600/month. S3 + CloudFront USD 30/month. Total USD 1212/month plus Datadog. Scaling: ECS auto-scaling handles 8am submission peaks; Redis queue with 10 concurrent LLM workers prevents OpenAI rate limit breaches.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): structured field input form (no voice), LangChain GPT-4o report drafting pipeline with spaCy masking, DailySiteReportDraft Pydantic schema with AS 4000 sections, supervisor review and edit interface with diff highlight, project manager approval workflow, PostgreSQL + S3 storage, Auth0 OIDC with MFA, basic PDF export, Datadog APM. Phase 2 (6 weeks): voice memo recording and Whisper API transcription pipeline, draft incorporation of transcript into LangChain prompt, transcription Lambda on S3 event, 90-day S3 lifecycle for voice_memos, report template versioning for AS 4000 vs AS 2124. Phase 3 (4 weeks): monthly analytics on delay events and weather impact days, Aconex document register integration for approved report upload, LLM prompt A/B testing framework for supervisor-measured draft quality improvement.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: ruff linting and mypy strict mode enforced in CI. LangChain chain isolated in report_generator.py module with dependency-injected LLM client to enable pytest mocking. spaCy model pinned to en_core_web_trf version in requirements.txt; model downloaded during Docker build. All OpenAI API calls logged to structured JSON with token counts but without prompt content for cost monitoring. React: TypeScript strict mode; React Hook Form + Zod for field input validation matching backend Pydantic models. TanStack Query polling interval set to 5 seconds during draft generation; stops polling on draft_ready or error status.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPT-4o hallucination inventing progress quantities not in field inputs. Mitigation: diff view in review UI highlights LLM-added content not sourced from field inputs; supervisor review is mandatory before approval. Risk: OpenAI API outage during morning submission peak. Mitigation: Redis-queued jobs retry with exponential backoff for 10 minutes; supervisors notified via in-app banner to use manual text entry fallback mode which bypasses LLM. Risk: spaCy NER misses an organisation name causing unmasked confidential data to reach OpenAI. Mitigation: additional regex pass for known project names stored in the projects table. Tradeoff: FastAPI BackgroundTask vs Celery -- BackgroundTask chosen for MVP simplicity; Celery added in Phase 2 if queue depth grows beyond single-process capacity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Entity masking uses spaCy NER en_core_web_trf with regex supplement for known project names rather than simple regex alone. LLM temperature set to 0.3 for deterministic AS 4000 format compliance. DailySiteReportDraft Pydantic model defines all 6 AS 4000 clause 47 sections as required fields -- LangChain retries once on parse failure before falling back to raw text storage. Voice transcription MVP deferred to Phase 2; Phase 1 uses structured text inputs only.

metadata.notes: Strong: detailed LLM orchestration with LangChain LCEL, spaCy NER masking, AS 4000 compliance context, DailySiteReportDraft Pydantic schema, and domain-specific workflows throughout.

project_goal.value: CLI tool for site engineers to validate and transform field survey data from total stations and GPS receivers before uploading to the project surveying database, checking coordinate system mismatches, duplicate point IDs, and statistical outliers
project_goal.rationale: Reduces upload errors that corrupt the project survey database

target_users.value: Site engineers and surveyors on civil infrastructure construction sites
target_users.rationale: Primary users running point data validation in the field

project_class.rationale: Command-line workflow; no browser UI needed

capabilities.rationale: Local processing with optional API upload integration

complexity_level.rationale: Single-user local tool with deterministic validation rules

risk_level.rationale: Local tool with no external authentication surface

data_sensitivity.rationale: Survey coordinates are commercially sensitive project data but not personal or financial

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on engineer laptop; upload is optional and authenticated via stored API token

access_model.value: No authentication; API token stored in ~/.surveycheck/config.toml for upload subcommand
access_model.rationale: CLI tools run as the logged-in OS user; API token protects upload endpoint

feature_scope.value: Validate CSV point files for coordinate system, duplicate IDs, and outliers; transform to target CRS; upload to surveying API; local SQLite manifest of upload history
feature_scope.rationale: Complete field workflow from validation to upload

mvp_scope.value: validate subcommand with stdout report and JSON summary; upload subcommand with API token auth
mvp_scope.rationale: Core field workflow for pilot deployment

security_baseline.value: API token stored with file permission 0600; TLS enforced for all API upload calls; no survey data cached beyond local SQLite manifest
security_baseline.rationale: Token protection and encrypted transit for upload

frontend_stack.value: None - CLI only (stdout, stderr, exit codes)
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; CLI output is the interface

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 standalone; pyproj for CRS transformations; scipy for statistical outlier detection
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem has best geospatial and statistics libraries

data_platform.value: Local SQLite via stdlib sqlite3 for upload manifest; no server-side database
data_platform.rationale: Lightweight local manifest without external dependencies

hosting_target.value: pipx-installable CLI package distributed on PyPI
hosting_target.rationale: Engineers install via pipx for isolated environment

privacy_retention_policy.value: No retention policy; tool processes files transiently and writes only upload manifests to local SQLite
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No server-side data storage

future_scope.value: Transform subcommand for CRS reprojection; support for Leica GSI and Trimble JXL formats in addition to CSV
future_scope.rationale: Wider instrument support post-MVP

constraints.value: Must run offline on site laptop with no internet; no third-party cloud dependencies; Python 3.12 only
constraints.rationale: Remote site conditions with unreliable connectivity

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON log file at ~/.surveycheck/logs/surveycheck.log; exit code 1 on validation failure
observability_baseline.rationale: Engineers and CI scripts check exit codes; log file for troubleshooting

execution_preference.value: Single-developer project; trunk-based development; release on every merged PR that bumps version
execution_preference.rationale: Small team, fast iteration

project.goal: Validate field survey CSV files and upload to project surveying API

security.transport: TLS enforced for upload API calls

open_questions.gsi_format: Leica GSI support deferred to post-MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: The validate and upload subcommands cover the complete field workflow. The offline constraint is firm and correctly excludes any cloud lookup during validation. The local SQLite manifest for upload history is a lightweight but sufficient audit trail for the field context.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Using Click for argument parsing and Rich for output formatting is the right choice for a Python CLI at this complexity level. pyproj wraps PROJ and handles the CRS transformation and mismatch detection correctly. scipy IQR-based outlier detection is well-suited for the statistical outlier check on point clouds. SQLite via stdlib is appropriate for the local manifest with no ORM overhead.
reasoner_reviews.security: API token stored at 0600 is the correct minimal control. The config file must warn if permissions are too open. TLS must be enforced for the upload subcommand with no cert verification bypass option. Survey point data must not be written to any temp file that persists after the process exits.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Offline operation during field surveys is a hard requirement — no network calls during validate subcommand execution. The coordinate transformation library (pyproj) must reference a locally bundled PROJ datum grid, not the CDN-backed network fetch. File size limit of 50MB per survey file is enforced at CLI input validation to prevent memory exhaustion on field laptops with 8GB RAM.

backend.service_design: Single Python package with two Click command groups: surveycheck validate and surveycheck upload; shared lib modules for CRS validation, duplicate detection, outlier analysis, and manifest writes
backend.api_patterns: No server-side API; upload subcommand is an HTTP client posting multipart form-data to the project surveying API endpoint
backend.business_modules: validate.py (CRS check via pyproj CRS.equals(), duplicate ID set check, scipy IQR outlier detection), upload.py (HTTP client with retry, manifest write), config.py (TOML config reader with permission check), report.py (Rich console output and JSON summary writer)
backend.failure_handling: ValidationError raised on CRS mismatch or duplicate IDs; OutlierWarning for statistical outliers (warning not error); upload retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts

security.auth_design: API token read from config.toml at upload time; config file permission check warns if chmod is not 0600
security.secrets_management: Token never logged; masked in Rich output; not included in JSON summary file
security.abuse_prevention: No rate limiting needed for local tool; upload subcommand validates file size less than 50MB before posting

data.entities: upload_manifest table in local SQLite: (id INTEGER PK, project_code TEXT, file_path TEXT, file_hash TEXT, api_revision TEXT, uploaded_at TEXT, status TEXT). validation_reports: transient in-memory; written to JSON summary file on disk if --output flag provided
data.storage_design: SQLite at ~/.surveycheck/manifests.db; JSON summary at user-specified path or stdout
data.data_risks: Duplicate uploads detected by file_hash check against manifest before posting to avoid duplicate point ingestion

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI package; no server infrastructure

previous_plan.title: Survey Data Validation CLI Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 CLI tool distributed via PyPI that validates field survey CSV files for coordinate system consistency, duplicate point IDs, and IQR-based outlier detection before optionally uploading to the project surveying API with a local SQLite upload manifest.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI using Click for argument parsing and Rich for colour-coded terminal output. pyproj handles CRS validation by comparing EPSG codes as strings. scipy IQR method detects statistical outliers. Local SQLite manifest records upload history.
previous_plan.system_components: Click CLI entry point, validate module (CRS string check, duplicate ID set check, scipy IQR outlier), upload module (requests HTTP client, manifest write), config module (TOML reader), SQLite manifest database.
previous_plan.workflows: validate: read CSV, check EPSG string match, detect duplicate IDs, flag IQR outliers, write report to stdout and optionally to --output JSON file (overwrites if exists). upload: hash file, check manifest, POST to API with token.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision focused on two unresolved issues: replacing string-based CRS comparison with pyproj CRS.equals() and adding file existence guard on --output. This is a flawed quality target so the plan intentionally retains a minor inconsistency: the executive summary references 'field interface' ambiguously despite the tool being CLI-only, and the phased implementation phase descriptions are too generic for the domain.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced EPSG string comparison with pyproj CRS.from_user_input() and CRS.equals() logic in validate module; updated system_components and workflows to reflect authority-aware CRS matching
fix_report.expected_outcome: CRS validation will correctly handle equivalent CRS representations and detect transformation parameter mismatches

fix_report.action_taken: Added file existence check to --output flag handling; raises CLIError if file exists unless --force flag is passed; documented in api_design and development_guidelines
fix_report.expected_outcome: Engineers will not silently overwrite previous validation reports

target_output.title: Survey Data Validation CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 CLI tool distributed via PyPI that validates field survey CSV files using pyproj authority-aware CRS comparison, duplicate point ID detection, and IQR outlier analysis, then optionally uploads cleaned point files to the project surveying API with idempotency guarded by a local SQLite manifest.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI using Click for subcommand routing and Rich for colour-coded stdout output. The validate subcommand loads the survey CSV, reads the declared CRS using pyproj CRS.from_user_input(), and compares it to the project CRS from config using CRS.equals() which performs an authority-aware spatial reference comparison rather than a string match. scipy IQR method detects coordinate outliers per axis. The upload subcommand hashes the file, checks the local SQLite manifest for a prior upload with the same hash, and posts the file to the API endpoint over TLS with exponential backoff retry.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Click (CLI framework), Rich (terminal output), pyproj 3.6 (CRS transformation and equality checks), scipy 1.12 (IQR outlier detection), requests (HTTP client for upload), tomllib stdlib (config parsing), sqlite3 stdlib (manifest database). Distributed as a wheel on PyPI, installed via pipx.
target_output.functional_feature_map: surveycheck validate: reads CSV, performs pyproj CRS.equals() authority comparison against project config, detects duplicate point IDs via set membership, applies IQR outlier detection per coordinate axis, writes colour-coded Rich report to stdout, writes machine-readable JSON summary to --output path (with --force required to overwrite existing file). surveycheck upload: SHA-256 hash check against SQLite manifest, multipart POST to project surveying API endpoint, writes upload manifest record on success. surveycheck config: initialise config file, set API token and project CRS.
target_output.system_components: Click entry point with validate, upload, and config command groups. validate.py: CRS check via pyproj CRS.from_user_input() plus CRS.equals(), duplicate ID check using Python set, scipy IQR outlier detector per axis. upload.py: SHA-256 file hash, SQLite manifest lookup, requests multipart POST with retry. config.py: tomllib TOML reader, file permission 0600 check on config file. report.py: Rich Console for stdout output; JSON summary writer with file existence guard. manifests.db: SQLite at ~/.surveycheck/manifests.db.
target_output.workflows: Validate Workflow: Engineer runs 'surveycheck validate survey_run_042.csv --project PROJ001 --output report.json'; tool reads declared EPSG from CSV header, calls pyproj CRS.from_user_input(declared_epsg) and CRS.from_user_input(project_config_epsg), asserts CRS.equals() returns True; on mismatch raises ValidationError with authority context; duplicate ID check via set(); IQR outlier detection on E, N, Z columns independently; Rich prints pass/fail per check with point counts; if --output report.json exists and --force not set, raises CLIError 'report.json already exists, use --force to overwrite'; exits 0 on clean, 1 on error. Upload Workflow: Engineer runs 'surveycheck upload survey_run_042.csv --project PROJ001'; tool computes SHA-256, queries manifests.db for prior upload with same file_hash and project_code; if found prints 'Already uploaded (revision X)' and exits 0; else POSTs multipart form-data to API endpoint from config with Bearer token; on HTTP 200 writes manifest record with server revision number; on HTTP 4xx/5xx retries with 1s/2s/4s backoff.
target_output.data_model: upload_manifest table (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, project_code TEXT NOT NULL, file_name TEXT, file_hash TEXT NOT NULL, file_size_bytes INTEGER, api_revision TEXT, uploaded_at TEXT, status TEXT). In-memory during validate: point_records list of dicts with point_id, E, N, Z, crs_epsg; ValidationResult dataclass with crs_match BOOL, duplicate_ids LIST, outlier_points LIST, point_count INT.
target_output.api_design: CLI interface: surveycheck validate FILE --project CODE [--output PATH] [--force] [--tolerance SIGMA]; surveycheck upload FILE --project CODE; surveycheck config init; surveycheck config set-token TOKEN; surveycheck config set-crs PROJECT EPSG. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 validation failure, 2 configuration error, 3 upload failure. Upload subcommand issues POST to base_url/api/v1/projects/PROJECT_CODE/surveys with multipart Content-Type and Authorization Bearer token header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API token stored in ~/.surveycheck/config.toml; config.py checks os.stat().st_mode at startup and emits warning if permissions are not 0600. Token masked in all Rich output and excluded from JSON summary. TLS enforced on upload API calls; requests session configured with verify=True and no cert bypass option. Survey point data processed in memory only; no temp files written during validate. SQLite manifest stores only metadata (hash, revision) not coordinate data.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Package released to PyPI as surveycheck wheel. GitHub Actions pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with 90% coverage gate on Python 3.11 and 3.12 matrix, build sdist and wheel with build module, upload to PyPI on version tag push via Twine with trusted publisher OIDC. Defective releases yanked on PyPI within 1 hour of report; users instructed to pin version in pipx install invocation. No server infrastructure.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON log at ~/.surveycheck/logs/surveycheck.log with fields: timestamp, command, project_code, file_path, outcome (success/validation_error/upload_error), error_detail. Exit code 0/1/2/3 for shell scripting integration. No remote telemetry; all observability is local. Log rotation at 10MB with three retained files prevents unbounded disk growth on field laptops. The --debug flag enables verbose per-coordinate transformation tracing to aid field support.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No ongoing infrastructure cost beyond GitHub Actions free tier for CI. PyPI hosting is free. pyproj and scipy are compiled wheels; cold import time on first run is approximately 1.5s acceptable for field use. Validation of 10,000-point CSV runs in under 2 seconds on a standard site laptop.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (3 weeks): validate subcommand with pyproj CRS.equals() check, duplicate ID detection, IQR outlier detection, Rich stdout output, JSON summary with --force guard, PyPI package structure, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (1 week): upload subcommand with SHA-256 manifest, API token auth, TLS upload, retry logic. Phase 3 post-MVP: transform subcommand for CRS reprojection, Leica GSI and Trimble JXL format parsers.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public functions type-annotated and checked with mypy strict. ruff for linting and import sorting. pytest fixtures use temp directories for all file I/O tests. CLI commands tested with Click CliRunner. 90% branch coverage enforced in CI. Semantic versioning; CHANGELOG updated on each release. No external runtime dependencies beyond declared requirements.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: pyproj CRS.equals() returns false for CRS definitions that are functionally equivalent but have different axis ordering. Mitigation: document expected canonical EPSG codes in surveycheck config docs; provide surveycheck config check-crs command for pre-flight CRS verification. Risk: scipy IQR outlier threshold too aggressive for certain survey methodologies. Mitigation: --tolerance flag adjusts IQR multiplier, default 3.0. Tradeoff: SQLite stdlib over a proper ORM keeps zero-dependency install footprint small at the cost of manual SQL queries.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed pyproj CRS.equals() replaces string EPSG comparison from rounds 1 and 2. --output file existence guard uses --force escape hatch rather than interactive prompt for CI script compatibility. IQR outlier detection applied per axis independently to avoid false positives from legitimate Z-value ranges differing from E/N ranges.
