backend.service_design: Dagster software-defined assets: raw_submission_files (Cloud Storage), stg_contractor_submissions (BigQuery, dbt staging), wbs_validation_asset (checks all WBS codes against wbs_baseline table before proceeding), evm_metrics_asset (dbt incremental model), evm_variance_flags_asset (flags >10% SPI/CPI deviation), dashboard_refresh_asset (triggers Looker Studio data source cache invalidation)
backend.business_modules: dbt project: staging (stg_contractor_submissions, stg_planned_value_baselines), intermediate (int_wbs_aligned_submissions normalising contractor WBS codes to programme standard), marts (fct_earned_value_snapshots, fct_evm_metrics, dim_wbs_hierarchy, dim_contractor_packages)
backend.background_jobs: Monday 04:00 UTC Dagster schedule triggers full asset materialisation chain; late submission reprocessing via manual Dagster UI asset rematerialisation with specific programme_period partition
backend.failure_handling: Dagster asset failure on wbs_validation blocks downstream EVM computation; PagerDuty alert on asset failure; Datadog monitors Dagster Cloud Run health; if dashboard not refreshed by 07:30 UTC on Monday, PagerDuty critical alert

security.auth_design: Google Workspace SSO as BigQuery identity provider; IAM roles: dataEditor on raw/staging datasets for Dagster service account, dataViewer on marts for Looker Studio service account, programme staff get bigquery.dataViewer on marts via group membership
security.secrets_management: Secret Manager stores per-contractor API keys and BigQuery service account JSON; Dagster Cloud Run uses Workload Identity Federation, no key file on container
security.audit_and_logging_controls: BigQuery DATA_ACCESS audit logs enabled on marts dataset; Dagster asset materialisation logs stored in Cloud Storage for 90 days; all WBS validation failures logged to BigQuery validation_audit table with submission_id, contractor_id, unrecognised_wbs_code, and timestamp

data.entities: wbs_elements (wbs_code VARCHAR, wbs_description, level INT, parent_wbs_code, contract_package_id, is_active). planned_value_baselines (id, wbs_code FK, programme_period DATE, planned_value NUMERIC(18,4), baseline_version INT). earned_value_snapshots (id, wbs_code FK, programme_period DATE, earned_value NUMERIC(18,4), actual_cost NUMERIC(18,4), contractor_id, submission_id, loaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). evm_metrics (wbs_code FK, programme_period DATE, spi FLOAT, cpi FLOAT, eac NUMERIC(18,4), bac NUMERIC(18,4), variance_at_completion NUMERIC(18,4), partitioned by programme_period). contractor_submissions (id UUID, contractor_id, submission_date DATE, status VARCHAR, raw_file_gcs_path, validation_status VARCHAR, loaded_at). programme_periods (period_date DATE PK, reporting_label VARCHAR, is_baseline_frozen BOOLEAN).
data.storage_design: BigQuery dataset hierarchy: raw (EXTERNAL tables over Cloud Storage JSON), staging (dbt materialised tables), marts (dbt incremental partitioned tables). evm_metrics table partitioned by programme_period DATE and clustered by contract_package_id for Looker Studio query performance.
data.consistency_notes: dbt singular test: SUM(earned_value_snapshots.earned_value) per wbs_code for current programme_period must be <= corresponding planned_value_baselines.planned_value * 1.05 (5% tolerance for rounding); test failure pauses evm_metrics materialisation

devops.deployment_topology: Dagster webserver and agent on Cloud Run in GCP project us-central1; BigQuery datasets in us-central1 multi-region; Cloud Storage for raw submissions in us-central1
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: dbt compile, dbt test --target dev, sqlfluff lint, Dagster asset materialisation smoke test on dev BigQuery dataset, docker build, push Artifact Registry, Cloud Run rolling deploy; staging BigQuery promotion on PR merge, prod promotion via manual workflow dispatch requiring data engineering lead approval
devops.rollback_strategy: BigQuery table snapshots taken before each Monday materialisation; Dagster asset backfill for specific programme_period partitions allows targeted reprocessing of a single period without affecting other partitions

ISS-001.title: WBS mismatch silently drops earned value data
ISS-001.detail: Contractor WBS codes not matching the approved baseline were silently filtered out during staging transformation, causing understated EV figures in the dashboard without any alert.

history.note: Discovered when programme director noticed EV total 12% lower than expected; traced to WBS filter dropping unrecognised codes
wbs_validation_asset added as hard dependency blocking evm_metrics materialisation on any unrecognised WBS code; Dagster alerts on failure

ISS-002.title: Contractor API keys shared via environment variable accessible to all contractors
ISS-002.detail: All contractor API keys were stored in a single Dagster environment variable; a contractor with Cloud Run logs access could read other contractors' API keys.

history.note: Security review found all keys in single env var
Per-contractor keys migrated to individual Secret Manager secrets; Dagster job reads only the relevant secret per contractor submission

ISS-003.title: evm_metrics dbt model full-refresh taking 18 minutes, threatening Monday 08:00 dashboard deadline
ISS-003.detail: The evm_metrics dbt model uses full-refresh materialisation, rescanning all historical earned_value_snapshots and planned_value_baselines on every Monday run. With 30 weeks of history, this takes 18 minutes on a 100-slot BigQuery job, leaving only 22 minutes of slack before the 08:00 deadline.

history.note: Timing analysis of Monday 04:00 run showed evm_metrics materialisation at 18 min; other assets complete in 8 min total

focus_issues.title: evm_metrics dbt full-refresh 18 min threatens Monday dashboard deadline
focus_issues.detail: Full-refresh of evm_metrics over 30 weeks of history takes 18 min; incremental materialisation keyed by programme_period required

latest_recommendations: Convert evm_metrics dbt model from full-refresh to incremental materialisation using unique_key=(wbs_code, programme_period) and partition pruning on programme_period
Add BigQuery partition on evm_metrics.programme_period to enable efficient incremental merge
Add late-submission reprocessing procedure: manual Dagster rematerialisation scoped to specific programme_period partition

convergence_state.reason: One high-severity performance issue threatening the Monday dashboard SLO

previous_audits.summary: Critical WBS silent drop issue and contractor key exposure are serious blockers. Pipeline architecture is fundamentally sound but data integrity and security gaps must be resolved before programme director can rely on EVM figures.
strengths: Dagster software-defined asset chain correctly models data dependencies
dbt incremental intention is correct though not yet implemented
concerns: WBS mismatch silently drops EV data without alert
All contractor API keys in single env var accessible to Cloud Run logs
blocking_issues: Programme director cannot trust EVM figures until WBS silent drop is fixed
recommendations: Add wbs_validation_asset as hard upstream dependency of evm_metrics_asset
Migrate contractor keys to individual Secret Manager secrets

issue_updates.title: WBS mismatch silent drop
issue_updates.detail: EV figures understated 12% due to silent WBS filter

issue_updates.title: Contractor API keys in single env var
issue_updates.detail: All keys readable from Cloud Run environment

previous_audits.summary: ISS-001 and ISS-002 resolved. Architecture and security now solid. Performance issue ISS-003 with evm_metrics full-refresh is a high-severity operational risk for the Monday deadline.
strengths: wbs_validation_asset correctly blocks EVM computation on any WBS mismatch
Per-contractor Secret Manager keys eliminate cross-contractor credential exposure
concerns: evm_metrics full-refresh at 18 minutes leaves insufficient buffer for Monday 08:00 deadline
recommendations: Convert evm_metrics to incremental dbt model with unique_key (wbs_code, programme_period) and BigQuery MERGE
Add programme_period partition to evm_metrics BigQuery table

issue_updates.title: evm_metrics full-refresh latency
issue_updates.detail: 18-minute materialisation leaves only 22-minute buffer before Monday 08:00 SLO

previous_plan.title: Infrastructure Programme EVM Pipeline - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Dagster-orchestrated weekly EVM pipeline ingesting contractor JSON submissions, validating WBS codes against approved baseline, and computing SPI/CPI/EAC via dbt models in BigQuery. WBS validation asset blocks EVM computation on mismatch (ISS-001 resolved). Per-contractor API keys in Secret Manager (ISS-002 resolved). evm_metrics using full-refresh materialisation (ISS-003 unresolved).
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Cloud Run Dagster service orchestrates Monday 04:00 UTC asset chain: raw JSON to Cloud Storage, BigQuery EXTERNAL table, dbt stg_contractor_submissions, wbs_validation_asset, dbt evm_metrics (full-refresh), variance flags, Looker Studio cache invalidation.
previous_plan.data_model: BigQuery marts: fct_earned_value_snapshots, fct_evm_metrics (full-refresh), dim_wbs_hierarchy, dim_contractor_packages. wbs_validation_audit log in BigQuery.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Google Workspace SSO for BigQuery. Per-contractor Secret Manager keys. BigQuery column-level policy tags on eac_forecast restricting to programme_director role.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves ISS-003 by converting evm_metrics from full-refresh to incremental dbt materialisation keyed on (wbs_code, programme_period) with BigQuery MERGE and partition pruning. The fix reduces Monday run from 18 to ~2-3 minutes. Late-submission reprocessing is handled via Dagster partition backfill on specific programme_period. All prior security and data integrity fixes from rounds 1-2 are preserved.

fix_report.action_taken: Converted evm_metrics dbt model from full-refresh to incremental materialisation: added {{config(materialized='incremental', unique_key=['wbs_code','programme_period'], partition_by={'field':'programme_period','data_type':'date','granularity':'week'}, incremental_strategy='merge')}} in dbt model config. Added programme_period DATE partition and (wbs_code, contract_package_id) cluster to evm_metrics BigQuery table. Incremental model processes only earned_value_snapshots rows WHERE programme_period >= (SELECT MAX(programme_period) FROM {{this}}) - 7 on regular runs, and accepts full backfill via dbt run --full-refresh --select evm_metrics --vars '{period_from: 2025-01-01, period_to: 2025-03-01}' for late submission reprocessing of specific periods.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Monday evm_metrics materialisation reduces from 18 minutes to 2-3 minutes (processing only current-week partition); Monday 08:00 dashboard deadline met with 55+ minutes of buffer; late contractor submissions replayed via scoped partition backfill without affecting historical EVM data

target_output.title: Infrastructure Programme Earned Value Management Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A Dagster-orchestrated weekly ELT pipeline that ingests earned value progress submissions from multiple delivery contractors via a REST API, validates WBS structure against the approved programme baseline, computes SPI, CPI, and EAC metrics in BigQuery using dbt incremental models partitioned by programme_period, and refreshes a Looker Studio executive dashboard by 08:00 every Monday. Per-contractor Secret Manager API keys, WBS validation gates, BigQuery column-level policy tags on EAC forecasts, and comprehensive audit logging meet government capital works reporting standards.
target_output.architecture_overview: Cloud Run hosts the Dagster webserver and agent in us-central1. Every Monday at 04:00 UTC, a Dagster schedule triggers the weekly asset materialisation chain. Contractors submit JSON progress reports via an authenticated Cloud Run endpoint to Cloud Storage. The Dagster chain proceeds: raw_submission_files asset reads Cloud Storage JSON to BigQuery EXTERNAL table, stg_contractor_submissions asset runs dbt staging model normalising contractor schemas, wbs_validation_asset checks all submitted WBS codes against wbs_elements table and fails hard on any unrecognised code, evm_metrics_asset triggers dbt incremental model merging current-week earned_value_snapshots with planned_value_baselines to compute EV, PV, AC, SPI, CPI, and EAC per WBS element, evm_variance_flags_asset identifies >10% SPI or CPI deviations, and dashboard_refresh_asset invalidates the Looker Studio data source cache. Google Workspace SSO gates all BigQuery access; BigQuery column-level policy tags restrict EAC and CPI to programme_director IAM binding. Datadog monitors Cloud Run and PagerDuty alerts on asset failure or late dashboard refresh.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Dagster 1.7 (software-defined assets) on Cloud Run Python 3.12 container. Transformation: dbt-core 1.8 with dbt-bigquery adapter, sqlfluff for SQL linting. Data warehouse: BigQuery in us-central1 with datasets raw, staging, marts. Staging: Cloud Storage for raw contractor JSON submissions. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager with per-contractor API key secrets. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with BigQuery dev dataset integration tests. Dashboards: Google Looker Studio connected to BigQuery marts via service account. Monitoring: Datadog APM on Cloud Run, PagerDuty for operational alerts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Contractor Submission API: POST /submissions endpoint on Cloud Run validates per-contractor API key, stores JSON to Cloud Storage, records contractor_submissions row. WBS Validation: Dagster asset queries all submitted WBS codes against wbs_elements WHERE is_active=TRUE; any unrecognised code fails the asset and blocks EVM computation. EVM Computation: dbt incremental model fct_evm_metrics merges current programme_period earned_value_snapshots with planned_value_baselines; computes SPI=EV/PV, CPI=EV/AC, EAC=BAC/CPI per WBS element. Variance Flagging: downstream dbt model flags any WBS element with ABS(SPI-1)>0.1 or ABS(CPI-1)>0.1 with a variance_flag and deviation_percentage. Looker Studio Dashboard: Looker report shows cumulative and periodic SPI/CPI trend per contract_package, drill-down to WBS level 3, EAC vs BAC comparison. Late Submission Reprocessing: Dagster partition backfill UI allows data engineer to rematerialise evm_metrics for a specific programme_period without disturbing other partitions.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run service (Dagster webserver min 1 instance, agent auto-scales 0-3). BigQuery datasets: raw (EXTERNAL tables), staging (dbt materialised), marts (dbt incremental partitioned). Cloud Storage bucket 'evm-raw-submissions-prod' for contractor JSON files with 7-year lifecycle. Secret Manager secrets named 'contractor-api-key-{contractor_id}' per contractor. Looker Studio report connected to BigQuery marts via dedicated service account. Artifact Registry for Dagster container images. Datadog APM integration on Cloud Run via DD_AGENT_HOST sidecar.
target_output.workflows: Weekly Monday EVM Cycle: Monday 04:00 UTC Dagster schedule triggers; raw_submission_files asset scans Cloud Storage for submissions with submission_date = current_monday; stg_contractor_submissions dbt model runs, normalising contractor_a WBS code format (X.Y.Z) and contractor_b format (XX-YY-ZZ) to programme standard (PPPP.LL.EE); wbs_validation_asset queries wbs_elements for all submitted codes - any code NOT IN wbs_elements raises Dagster AssetCheckSeverity.ERROR, fails asset, blocks chain, sends PagerDuty alert to programme director and data engineering lead; on wbs validation pass, evm_metrics_asset runs dbt incremental model processing only current programme_period partition via BigQuery MERGE with unique_key (wbs_code, programme_period); evm_variance_flags_asset queries evm_metrics for >10% deviations and inserts to evm_variance_flags; dashboard_refresh_asset calls Looker Studio Data Source API to invalidate cache; full chain completes by ~06:15 UTC, dashboard refreshed by 06:30, well before 08:00 local deadline. Late Submission Reprocessing: data engineer opens Dagster UI, navigates to evm_metrics asset partition view, selects specific programme_period (e.g., 2025-W10), clicks 'Materialize selected partition'; dbt runs with only that partition's data using MERGE; other partitions unchanged; validation_audit row recorded.
target_output.data_model: BigQuery marts dataset: fct_earned_value_snapshots (wbs_code STRING, programme_period DATE, earned_value NUMERIC, actual_cost NUMERIC, contractor_id STRING, submission_id STRING, loaded_at TIMESTAMP, PARTITION BY programme_period, CLUSTER BY wbs_code). fct_evm_metrics (wbs_code STRING, programme_period DATE, spi FLOAT64, cpi FLOAT64, eac NUMERIC, bac NUMERIC, variance_at_completion NUMERIC, spi_trend_3wk FLOAT64, PARTITION BY programme_period, CLUSTER BY contract_package_id) - INCREMENTAL with unique_key (wbs_code, programme_period). evm_variance_flags (wbs_code, programme_period, flag_type STRING, deviation_pct FLOAT64, flagged_at TIMESTAMP). dim_wbs_hierarchy (wbs_code STRING PK, wbs_description, level INT64, parent_wbs_code, contract_package_id, is_active BOOL). dim_contractor_packages (package_id STRING PK, package_name, contractor_id, bac NUMERIC, start_date DATE, end_date DATE). contractor_submissions (id STRING, contractor_id, submission_date DATE, validation_status STRING, raw_file_gcs_path, loaded_at TIMESTAMP). wbs_validation_audit (id STRING, submission_id, contractor_id, unrecognised_wbs_code STRING, detected_at TIMESTAMP). Programme financial entities: budget_accounts (id UUID PK, wbs_code VARCHAR, programme_id VARCHAR, account_type VARCHAR, approved_budget_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), fiscal_year INT), payment_transactions (id UUID PK, wbs_code VARCHAR, invoice_id UUID, invoice_number VARCHAR, supplier_id VARCHAR, invoice_amount_cents BIGINT, payment_amount_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), payment_date DATE, status ENUM pending/paid/disputed), ledger_variance_log (id UUID PK, wbs_code VARCHAR, period DATE, planned_value_cents BIGINT, earned_value_cents BIGINT, actual_cost_cents BIGINT, currency CHAR(3), cost_variance_cents BIGINT, schedule_variance_cents BIGINT).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/submissions - contractor submits weekly progress JSON; Authorization: ApiKey {contractor_key} header; validates key against Secret Manager secret 'contractor-api-key-{contractor_id}'; stores JSON to Cloud Storage with path submissions/{contractor_id}/{submission_date}/progress.json; inserts contractor_submissions record with status=received; returns 202 with submission_id. GET /api/v1/submissions/{id}/status - contractor checks processing status; returns validation_status and any WBS validation error codes. Internal Dagster REST API (VPC only): POST /runs for manual asset rematerialisation. No external-facing analytics API; Looker Studio queries BigQuery directly via service account.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Google Workspace SSO for all BigQuery access; IAM bindings: Dagster service account gets bigquery.dataEditor on raw and staging, bigquery.dataViewer on marts; Looker Studio service account gets bigquery.dataViewer on marts; programme staff groups get bigquery.dataViewer on marts; programme_director Google group additionally gets access to eac_forecast and cpi columns via BigQuery column-level policy tag binding. Contractor API keys stored as individual Secret Manager secrets named contractor-api-key-{contractor_id}; Dagster Cloud Run uses Workload Identity Federation, no service account key file. Submission endpoint validates API key before writing to Cloud Storage; contractor_a key cannot trigger contractor_b submission path. BigQuery DATA_ACCESS audit logging enabled on marts dataset for government audit. wbs_validation_audit table is append-only; no UPDATE or DELETE permitted. Cloud Run not publicly accessible; submission endpoint exposed via Cloud Load Balancing with Cloud Armor enforcing HTTPS-only and rate limiting 10 req/min per API key.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Run Dagster webserver in us-central1, min 1 max 1 instance (always-on for Looker Studio and manual triggers). Cloud Run Dagster agent pool, min 0 max 3 instances (scales on Monday run). GitHub Actions CI: sqlfluff lint, dbt compile --target dev, dbt test --target dev on BigQuery dev dataset, pytest for submission API handlers, docker build, push Artifact Registry, Cloud Run rolling deploy. Staging BigQuery dataset promotion on PR merge to main; production promotion via manual workflow_dispatch with required approval from data engineering lead. BigQuery table snapshots taken as SNAPSHOT TABLE before each Monday materialisation for rollback capability. Dagster asset materialisation logs archived to Cloud Storage for 90 days. WBS baseline changes require approved change request before wbs_elements UPDATE; enforced by dbt test that counts wbs_elements rows and alerts on unexpected decrease.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces Dagster asset materialisation durations: target evm_metrics asset <5 minutes, full chain <3 hours. PagerDuty P1 alert: any Dagster asset failure during Monday run, dashboard not refreshed by 07:30 UTC. Custom Dagster asset checks: wbs_submission_completeness (all expected contractors have submitted by 03:30 UTC), evm_metrics_row_count (must be >= prior week count). dbt test results stored as JSON artefacts in Cloud Storage and surfaced in Datadog as custom metrics. Looker Studio usage statistics via Google Analytics 360 to track which programme staff and auditors are actively using the dashboard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,200-1,600/month: Cloud Run Dagster webserver (always-on ~$80), Cloud Run Dagster agents (Monday burst only ~$20), BigQuery on-demand processing $5/TB (incremental evm_metrics processes ~20GB/week ~$5/week = $20/month), BigQuery storage for marts and snapshots (~$200/month growing), Cloud Storage for submissions and logs (~$30), Secret Manager (~$10), Datadog APM (~$400), PagerDuty (~$50). Incremental materialisation reduces BigQuery on-demand costs from ~$80/week (full-refresh over 30-week history) to ~$5/week.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Complete (rounds 1-2): Dagster asset chain, contractor submission REST API, Cloud Storage staging, BigQuery raw and staging layers, dbt EVM metric computation (full-refresh), wbs_validation_asset blocking bad WBS (ISS-001 resolved), per-contractor Secret Manager API keys (ISS-002 resolved), Looker Studio dashboard, BigQuery policy tags for EAC column. Phase 2 Current (round 3): Convert evm_metrics to incremental dbt materialisation (ISS-003 resolved), BigQuery partition on programme_period, late submission reprocessing via Dagster partition backfill, BigQuery table snapshots before each Monday run. Phase 3 Future: real-time contractor progress feed via streaming API, LLM-assisted EAC variance commentary, contractor self-service EVM view.
target_output.development_guidelines: dbt model config for all incremental models must specify unique_key, partition_by, and incremental_strategy='merge' explicitly; no implicit full-refresh in production. BigQuery MERGE statements in dbt incremental models tested in CI against dev dataset with representative 3-contractor, 10-period fixture data. WBS element changes must update wbs_elements with is_active flag change rather than DELETE; dbt test enforces row count non-decrease. All Dagster asset materialisation events emit structured logs with asset_key, programme_period, row_count_processed, duration_seconds for Datadog ingestion. sqlfluff Snowflake dialect replaced with BigQuery dialect in CI config.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Late contractor submission after Monday window causes stale EVM for that contractor's packages in dashboard. Mitigation: wbs_submission_completeness Dagster asset check alerts at 03:30 UTC if any expected contractor has not submitted; programme team can chase contractor before 04:00 run. Risk: BigQuery MERGE on evm_metrics fails mid-run leaving partial week partition. Mitigation: Dagster retry=2 on evm_metrics_asset; BigQuery MERGE is atomic so partial run leaves previous partition intact. Tradeoff: incremental materialisation requires disciplined unique_key management; late corrections to historical periods must use scoped --full-refresh rather than direct BigQuery row updates to maintain dbt lineage integrity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Dagster partition backfill for late submission reprocessing over a separate reconciliation DAG to leverage existing incremental model without additional code path. Confirmed BigQuery on-demand billing over flat-rate slots given weekly batch cadence makes on-demand more cost-effective below 5TB/day average. Chose Looker Studio over custom React dashboard to reduce frontend development scope and leverage native BigQuery integration.

metadata.notes: Infrastructure programme EVM pipeline; round 3 converts evm_metrics dbt model to incremental materialisation resolving Monday deadline performance issue; government audit compliance.

project_goal.value: Web application enabling head contractors to collect, verify, and store financial capacity documents, insurance certificates, safety management plans, and licence registrations for subcontractors on their approved vendor register, with automated expiry reminders and compliance scoring
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual spreadsheet tracking of subcontractor compliance documents with an automated system

target_users.value: Head contractor procurement teams (internal users) and subcontractors (external partner users) submitting their compliance documents
target_users.rationale: Dual user base with different access levels and workflows

project_class.rationale: Browser-based application with external partner-facing subcontractor portal and internal admin

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full web app with partner portal, document storage, expiry automation, and admin reporting

complexity_level.rationale: Document management, expiry scheduling, and compliance scoring are well-understood patterns

risk_level.rationale: Contains confidential financial and insurance documents; partner-facing portal

data_sensitivity.rationale: Financial capacity statements, insurance certificates, and safety plans are commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Subcontractors access via invitation-authenticated partner portal

access_model.value: RBAC: head_contractor_admin, procurement_officer, and subcontractor roles; subcontractors access only their own documents; Cognito user pools per tenant
access_model.rationale: Strict data isolation between subcontractor tenants is essential

feature_scope.value: Subcontractor invitation and onboarding, document upload and categorisation, expiry tracking and automated reminders, procurement review and approval workflow, compliance scoring dashboard, admin panel for vendor register management, trade category filtering
feature_scope.rationale: Complete subcontractor pre-qualification lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Subcontractor invitation, document upload for 5 document categories, expiry date tracking, 60/30/7-day email reminders, procurement review and approval workflow, basic compliance score display
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot with 50 subcontractors before full rollout

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, S3 server-side encryption for documents, Cognito MFA for procurement team, row-level security enforcing subcontractor data isolation
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential financial documents require strict access controls

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript and Vite
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Vue; Vite for fast local dev

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django REST Framework; Celery for async tasks
backend_stack.rationale: Django ORM and admin panel reduce boilerplate; Celery for expiry job scheduling

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security; S3 for document file storage
data_platform.rationale: Row-level security enforces subcontractor tenant isolation at DB layer

hosting_target.value: AWS Elastic Beanstalk for API; S3 and CloudFront for Vue SPA; Celery workers on Elastic Beanstalk worker environment
hosting_target.rationale: Team familiar with Elastic Beanstalk; managed platform simplifies ops for moderate-scale deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Subcontractor documents retained while active on vendor register plus 3 years after removal; audit logs retained 5 years; documents deleted on written request if legally permissible
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Privacy Act and contractual record-keeping obligations

future_scope.value: Integration with ASIC and state licensing authority APIs for automated licence verification; mobile app for on-site subcontractor document check; multi-head-contractor tenancy
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP expansion roadmap

constraints.value: AWS cloud only; must support 500 active subcontractors at MVP; email reminders via SES; no per-subcontractor fee model at MVP
constraints.rationale: AWS commitment and pricing model constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for API tracing; structured JSON logging with subcontractor_id and document_id context; CloudWatch for Elastic Beanstalk health
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard

execution_preference.value: 2-week Agile sprints; UAT with procurement team before release to subcontractors
execution_preference.rationale: Procurement team must validate workflows before partner access

compliance_context.value: Privacy Act obligations for collecting subcontractor financial data; Work Health and Safety Act prequalification requirements; GDPR consideration for any subcontractors operating in EU jurisdictions
compliance_context.rationale: Financial documents trigger Privacy Act; WHS Act mandates safety plan prequalification; EU operations may involve GDPR

project.goal: Automate subcontractor pre-qualification document collection and expiry tracking

security.auth: Cognito with MFA for procurement team

devops.hosting: Elastic Beanstalk web and worker environments

open_questions.api_verification: ASIC licence verification deferred to post-MVP

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope covering 5 document categories with expiry reminders and review workflow is well-defined for a 50-subcontractor pilot. The compliance score display is valuable for procurement teams but scoring criteria need to be confirmed with the head contractor before implementation. Deferring ASIC API integration is correct as it requires separate regulatory engagement.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django REST Framework with Celery is the right choice for this document-management-heavy workload. PostgreSQL row-level security at the DB layer provides defence-in-depth for subcontractor data isolation beyond application-level RBAC. S3 pre-signed URL pattern for document downloads avoids proxying large files through the API.
reasoner_reviews.security: Subcontractor tenant isolation is the critical security requirement. Row-level security policies must be enforced with a Cognito sub as the row owner. S3 pre-signed URLs must use 15-minute expiry for document downloads. MFA must be enforced for procurement officers who can view all subcontractor documents. Invitation tokens must be time-limited (48 hours) and single-use.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SES is sufficient for expiry reminder volumes at 500 subcontractors. Elastic Beanstalk auto-scaling covers moderate traffic spikes during deadline periods when many subcontractors upload simultaneously.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with route-based lazy loading; separate route groups for subcontractor portal and procurement admin
frontend.pages_and_flows: Subcontractor portal: Accept Invitation, Document Upload Dashboard, Document Status, Profile. Procurement portal: Vendor Register, Subcontractor Detail, Document Review, Compliance Reports, Admin Panel.
frontend.state_management: Pinia for auth state and user profile; Vue Query for document lists and compliance scores with 5-minute cache TTL
frontend.ui_modules: DocumentUploadCard (drag-drop with progress), ComplianceScoreBadge (colour-coded), ExpiryCalendar (upcoming expiries view), ReviewDecisionPanel

backend.service_design: Django app with domain apps: vendors, documents, compliance, notifications, admin; DRF ViewSets per resource
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI via drf-spectacular; versioned at /api/v1/; bulk endpoints for document list and expiry queries
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat task runs daily at 06:00 AEST to query expiring documents and dispatch SES reminder emails; Celery task for async S3 pre-signed URL generation
backend.failure_handling: Celery task retry with 3 attempts and exponential backoff for SES send failures; dead-letter queue for failed reminders

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool for procurement team with MFA; separate Cognito pool for subcontractors with email verification; invitation token UUID 48-hour TTL single-use stored in PostgreSQL
security.authorization_model: Django REST permission classes check Cognito JWT sub matches subcontractor row owner; procurement officers have cross-subcontractor read access; only admin role can delete records
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Django signal on every document status change writes to document_audit_log table; Datadog APM traces all API requests with subcontractor_id tag

data.entities: subcontractors (id UUID PK, company_name, abn, trade_categories ARRAY, cognito_sub, status ENUM invited/active/suspended/removed, compliance_score NUMERIC, created_at), compliance_documents (id UUID PK, subcontractor_id FK, document_type ENUM insurance_certificate/safety_plan/financial_statement/licence_registration/other, s3_key, expiry_date, review_status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/expired, reviewed_by_user_id FK, reviewed_at), expiry_schedules (id UUID PK, document_id FK, reminder_day INT, sent_at, status ENUM pending/sent/failed), document_audit_log (id UUID PK, document_id FK, user_id, action, prior_status, new_status, timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policy enforcing subcontractor_id row ownership; S3 bucket with private ACL; pre-signed URLs generated per download request with 15-minute TTL
data.retention_and_deletion: Celery task archives documents for removed subcontractors to cold S3 storage class after 3 years; physical deletion only on verified legal request with audit log entry

devops.deployment_topology: Elastic Beanstalk web environment (API, 2-4 instances, auto-scaling on CPU 60%); Elastic Beanstalk worker environment (Celery, 1-2 instances); RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ; ElastiCache Redis; S3 bucket; CloudFront for Vue SPA assets
devops.ci_cd_design: CircleCI pipeline: ruff, pytest 80% coverage, Django check --deploy, Docker build, push to ECR, deploy to Elastic Beanstalk via eb deploy, staging smoke test, manual gate, production deploy
devops.rollback_strategy: Elastic Beanstalk environment version rollback to previous; RDS point-in-time restore for data incidents

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a subcontractor pre-qualification system on AWS with Vue 3, Django REST Framework, and PostgreSQL with row-level security. The weak quality target means some sections are serviceable but thinner than ideal. Key decisions: RLS for subcontractor tenant isolation, Celery beat for daily expiry job, S3 pre-signed URLs for document downloads, invitation token pattern for subcontractor onboarding.
target_output.title: Subcontractor Pre-Qualification and Compliance Portal Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A partner-facing web application for head contractor subcontractor pre-qualification built on Vue 3, Django REST Framework, and PostgreSQL with row-level security on AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Subcontractors are invited via time-limited tokens, upload compliance documents in five categories, and receive automated SES expiry reminders. Procurement teams review documents, manage the vendor register, and monitor compliance scores via an admin dashboard.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier architecture with a Vue 3 SPA hosted on S3 and CloudFront communicating over HTTPS to a Django REST Framework API on Elastic Beanstalk. PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security enforces subcontractor tenant isolation at the database layer. Celery workers running on an Elastic Beanstalk worker environment handle daily expiry scanning and SES email dispatch. S3 stores document files with private ACL; the API generates 15-minute pre-signed URLs for each download. Cognito manages two user pools: one for procurement team with MFA, one for subcontractors with email verification. Datadog APM provides API tracing with subcontractor_id tags.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Vite, Vue Router 4, Pinia, Vue Query. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework, drf-spectacular for OpenAPI, Celery 5 with Redis broker. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security. Cache and broker: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Document storage: S3 with SSE-S3. Auth: AWS Cognito two pools. Email: AWS SES. CI/CD: CircleCI. Monitoring: Datadog APM, CloudWatch.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Subcontractor Onboarding: procurement admin creates vendor record and sends invitation with 48-hour single-use token; subcontractor clicks link, creates Cognito account, and is bound to the vendor row. Document Upload: subcontractor uploads files in 5 categories with expiry date; each file stored in S3 and compliance_documents record created with status pending. Expiry Reminders: Celery beat job daily queries expiry_schedules for due reminders and dispatches SES emails. Review Workflow: procurement officer opens pending document, reviews, approves or rejects with note, compliance score recalculated. Admin Panel: vendor register list with compliance scores, trade category filters, overdue document alerts.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution serving Vue 3 SPA from S3. Elastic Beanstalk web environment with Django REST Framework API and ALB. Elastic Beanstalk worker environment with Celery beat and worker. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with row-level security. ElastiCache Redis 7 as Celery broker and result backend. S3 bucket with private ACL and SSE-S3. AWS Cognito procurement user pool with MFA and subcontractor user pool. AWS SES for reminder emails. Datadog Agent on Elastic Beanstalk instances.
target_output.workflows: Subcontractor Document Upload: Subcontractor logs into partner portal, selects document category (e.g., insurance_certificate), uploads PDF via drag-drop; Vue calls POST /api/v1/documents with multipart form; Django saves file to S3 and creates compliance_documents record with status pending_review; Celery task creates expiry_schedules rows for 60-, 30-, and 7-day reminders based on expiry_date field. Expiry Reminder Dispatch: Celery beat triggers daily at 06:00 AEST; task queries expiry_schedules WHERE reminder_day IN (60,30,7) AND sent_at IS NULL AND expiry_date equals CURRENT_DATE plus reminder_day; dispatches SES email to subcontractor and assigned procurement contact; updates sent_at on success; on SES failure retries 3 times with backoff and writes status failed.
target_output.data_model: subcontractors (id UUID PK, company_name TEXT, abn TEXT, trade_categories TEXT[], cognito_sub TEXT, status TEXT ENUM invited/active/suspended/removed, compliance_score NUMERIC, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). compliance_documents (id UUID PK, subcontractor_id UUID FK, document_type TEXT ENUM insurance_certificate/safety_management_plan/financial_statement/licence_registration/other, s3_key TEXT, file_name TEXT, expiry_date DATE, review_status TEXT ENUM pending/approved/rejected/expired, reviewed_by UUID FK users, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). expiry_schedules (id UUID PK, document_id UUID FK, reminder_day INT, sent_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT). document_audit_log (id UUID PK, document_id UUID FK, user_id UUID, action TEXT, prior_status TEXT, new_status TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/invitations (create subcontractor invitation with token). POST /api/v1/auth/accept-invitation (validate token, bind Cognito sub to vendor record). POST /api/v1/documents (upload document with expiry date). GET /api/v1/documents/ID/download-url (return 15-minute pre-signed S3 URL). PATCH /api/v1/documents/ID/review (procurement review: status and review note). GET /api/v1/vendors (procurement vendor register list with compliance scores and filters). GET /api/v1/vendors/ID/compliance-summary (aggregate compliance score and document status). GET /api/v1/reports/expiring (documents expiring within N days). All endpoints require Cognito JWT; RLS enforced via current_setting per request.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA enforced for all procurement officer accounts. Subcontractor accounts require email verification. Invitation tokens are single-use UUIDs with 48-hour TTL stored in PostgreSQL; invalidated on first use. S3 documents have private ACL; download via 15-minute pre-signed URLs only. PostgreSQL RLS policy subcontractor_isolation on compliance_documents enforces that subcontractors can only read their own rows. Django REST permission classes enforce procurement-level cross-subcontractor access. Document audit log captures every status change. Privacy Act compliance: minimum necessary data collection; subcontractor consent captured on invitation acceptance. WHS Act: safety management plan is a mandatory document category.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: CircleCI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy, pytest 80% coverage with Django test client, Django check --deploy validation, Docker build and push to ECR, eb deploy to staging Elastic Beanstalk environment, smoke test (invitation creation, document upload, reminder schedule), manual approval gate, eb deploy to production. RDS Multi-AZ for automatic failover. Elastic Beanstalk rolling deploy with health check on /health/ endpoint. Django migrations run via EB deployment hook. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager; EB environment variables reference secrets ARNs.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM for Django request traces with subcontractor_id and document_type tags. Custom metrics: documents_uploaded_daily, expiry_reminders_sent, review_completion_time_hours, compliance_score_distribution. CloudWatch alarms: Elastic Beanstalk unhealthy instances, RDS CPU exceeds 80%, Redis memory exceeds 70%. Datadog alert: Celery expiry job failure, SES bounce rate exceeds 2%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $350/month for 500 active subcontractors: RDS db.t3.small Multi-AZ approximately $80, Elastic Beanstalk t3.small instances approximately $60, ElastiCache cache.t3.micro approximately $20, S3 storage for 500 subcontractors at 5 documents each approximately $5, SES for reminder emails at low volume approximately $5. Auto-scaling on CPU 60% handles document upload peaks.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Cognito auth for both user pools, subcontractor invitation and onboarding, 5 document categories with upload, expiry tracking, SES 60/30/7-day reminders, procurement review workflow, basic compliance score, admin vendor register list, CI/CD pipeline to staging. Phase 2 (4 weeks): compliance scoring algorithm with configurable weights, analytics dashboard with trend charts, bulk document request to subcontractors, trade category filter and reporting. Phase 3 post-MVP: ASIC and state licensing authority API integration for automated licence verification, mobile-responsive enhancements, multi-head-contractor tenancy.
target_output.development_guidelines: Django REST Framework ViewSets with explicit permission classes; no function-based views. PostgreSQL RLS policies tested in integration tests by switching app.current_subcontractor_id. All S3 URL generation via helper function to enforce 15-minute expiry. Celery tasks must be idempotent; check sent_at IS NULL before dispatching SES. 80% test coverage enforced in CircleCI. Vue components use TypeScript with strict null checks. PR requires one senior review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Subcontractor data isolation breach if RLS policy is bypassed by a misconfigured DB connection. Mitigation: RLS tested in integration suite; SET SESSION app.current_subcontractor_id enforced as middleware in all API requests. Risk: SES rate limiting during peak reminder periods when many documents expire simultaneously. Mitigation: Celery rate limit of 20 SES sends per second; spread reminder dispatch over 2-hour window. Tradeoff: Elastic Beanstalk managed platform over ECS for simpler ops at cost of less fine-grained scaling control. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL RLS over application-level row filtering for defence-in-depth at cost of slightly more complex migration management.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Cognito over Auth0 for AWS-native integration and simpler IAM role mapping. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS for tenant isolation over application-level filtering. Compliance score algorithm: weighted sum of approved documents divided by required documents by category, with expired documents reducing score to 0 for that category. ASIC licence verification deferred to Phase 3.

metadata.notes: Subcontractor pre-qualification portal; weak quality with thin workflow descriptions and sparse phase criteria.

project_goal.value: CLI tool for structural engineers that reads reinforced concrete section definitions from a TOML config file and computes bending and shear capacity to AS 3600 or Eurocode 2, printing tabular capacity tables and M-N interaction diagrams as SVG files
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual spreadsheet calculations with a reproducible, version-controlled command-line tool

target_users.value: Structural engineers performing RC section design and checking to Australian or European codes
target_users.rationale: Primary users running design checks in a local engineering office environment

project_class.rationale: Deterministic calculation tool with no browser UI; output is stdout tables and SVG files

capabilities.rationale: Local computation and file I/O only; no auth or networking required

complexity_level.rationale: Deterministic numerical algorithms with TOML input and stdout and SVG output

risk_level.rationale: Local-only tool with no network exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Processes geometric and material input data with no personal or confidential content

external_exposure.rationale: Runs entirely on the engineer workstation; no server or API involved

access_model.value: No authentication; single-user local tool
access_model.rationale: CLI tools run as the local OS user with no access control requirements

feature_scope.value: compute subcommand for bending and shear capacity tables, diagram subcommand for M-N interaction SVG, TOML section definition input, AS 3600 and Eurocode 2 code selection flag, Markdown table output to stdout
feature_scope.rationale: Complete section design check workflow in two subcommands

mvp_scope.value: compute subcommand with AS 3600 bending capacity, tabular Markdown output to stdout; diagram subcommand producing SVG M-N interaction diagram
mvp_scope.rationale: Core design check outputs needed for pilot with engineering team

security_baseline.value: No network calls; no external API dependencies; stdlib-only runtime; TOML input validated against schema before computation
security_baseline.rationale: No security surface beyond input validation

frontend_stack.value: None - CLI only; output is stdout Markdown tables and SVG files
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; engineer output is captured to terminal or redirected to file

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 using stdlib only: tomllib, math, dataclasses, pathlib, csv; svgwrite for SVG generation
backend_stack.rationale: Minimal dependencies for portability; svgwrite is the only third-party package required

data_platform.value: No database; section definitions in TOML files; output to stdout and SVG files on disk
data_platform.rationale: File-based I/O appropriate for a deterministic calculation tool

hosting_target.value: pipx-installable CLI package distributed on PyPI
hosting_target.rationale: Engineers install via pipx for isolated Python environment

privacy_retention_policy.value: No retention policy; tool processes TOML files locally without any server-side storage
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No data leaves the local workstation

future_scope.value: Eurocode 2 shear capacity, deflection checks to AS 3600 clause 8.5, PDF report generation combining capacity table and SVG diagram
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP extensions to cover full section design check suite

constraints.value: Python 3.12 only; no compiled extensions (no numpy, scipy, pandas); must run offline; svgwrite is the only permitted third-party package
constraints.rationale: Simple install footprint for firm-wide deployment via pipx on managed workstations

observability_baseline.value: Exit code 0 on success, 1 on validation error, 2 on computation error; stderr for error messages; --verbose flag for intermediate calculation steps
observability_baseline.rationale: Shell script integration and engineer debugging via verbose output

execution_preference.value: Single-developer project; semantic versioning; release to PyPI on version tag; CHANGELOG maintained
execution_preference.rationale: Small project; direct PyPI release on tag

project.goal: Compute RC section bending and shear capacity and generate M-N interaction diagrams

backend.libraries: tomllib stdlib, svgwrite, math stdlib, dataclasses stdlib

data.output: Markdown tables to stdout, SVG files to disk

devops.hosting: PyPI via Twine with trusted publisher OIDC

reasoner_reviews.product: The compute and diagram subcommands cover the core engineering workflow. AS 3600 first is correct given the target market of Australian structural engineers. The constraint to stdlib-only except svgwrite keeps the install footprint minimal, which is critical for firm-wide pipx deployment on locked-down workstations.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Python dataclasses for SectionDefinition and CapacityResult are the right approach for a typed, test-friendly calculation pipeline. The neutral-axis depth iteration for the M-N diagram is a standard numerical approach that works without numpy. Click subcommands with shared context for code standard selection are clean. The --verbose flag routing intermediate calc steps to stderr avoids polluting the stdout Markdown table output.
reasoner_reviews.security: No security surface beyond input validation. TOML schema validation must reject negative dimensions and invalid material grades before calculation to prevent nonsense results being used in design. Exit codes are critical for integration with engineering CI scripts that check calculation results automatically.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: AS 3600-2018 is the mandatory code for all capacity calculations; the CLI must embed the relevant code coefficients as constants, not user-configurable parameters, to prevent accidental non-compliant calculations. TOML config files are the only supported input format to ensure version-controllable section definitions. The CLI must run without internet access for use in air-gapped project environments.

backend.service_design: Single Python package with Click entry point; separate modules: parser.py (TOML loading and validation), sections.py (SectionDefinition dataclass), as3600.py (bending and shear capacity functions), ec2.py (stub for post-MVP), diagram.py (M-N interaction curve generation and SVG output), report.py (Markdown table formatter)
backend.api_patterns: CLI interface only: rccap compute section.toml --code AS3600 or EC2 with optional --verbose flag; rccap diagram section.toml --code AS3600 or EC2 --output path.svg
backend.business_modules: as3600.py: ultimate_bending_capacity(section, axial_force) returning MomentCapacity; ultimate_shear_capacity(section) returning ShearCapacity; neutral_axis_depth(section, axial_force) using iterative bisection on equilibrium equation
backend.failure_handling: InputValidationError raised on invalid TOML (negative dimensions, unrecognised steel grade, f_c below 20 MPa); ComputationError raised if bisection fails to converge within 100 iterations; both routed to stderr with informative message and exit code

security.auth_design: No authentication required
security.secrets_management: No secrets; no API keys or credentials
security.abuse_prevention: TOML input validation rejects physically implausible section dimensions (width exceeds 3000mm or depth exceeds 5000mm flagged as likely data entry error with warning)

data.entities: SectionDefinition dataclass (b_mm FLOAT, d_mm FLOAT, d_prime_mm FLOAT, f_c_MPa FLOAT, f_sy_MPa FLOAT, reinforcement_bars LIST of BarGroup, code_standard ENUM as3600/ec2). BarGroup dataclass (count INT, diameter_mm FLOAT, layer_depth_mm FLOAT). CapacityResult dataclass (M_uo_kNm FLOAT, V_uc_kN FLOAT, phi_M_uo_kNm FLOAT, phi_V_uc_kN FLOAT, neutral_axis_depth_mm FLOAT). InteractionPoint dataclass (N_kN FLOAT, M_kNm FLOAT).
data.storage_design: No persistent storage; all data in-memory during execution; TOML files are the persistent section definition format
data.migration_notes: TOML schema versioned via schema_version field; future schema changes handled by backward-compatible additions; breaking changes require major version bump

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI package; no server infrastructure
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy strict type check, pytest 90% coverage on Python 3.11 and 3.12 matrix, test AS 3600 capacity results against hand-verified reference values, build sdist and wheel, publish to PyPI on version tag via Twine OIDC trusted publisher
devops.rollback_strategy: PyPI yank for defective versions; engineers pin version in pipx install

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a greenfield RC section capacity CLI tool with Python 3.12 stdlib-only except svgwrite. Key decisions: Click for subcommand routing, dataclasses for typed section definitions and results, neutral-axis bisection iteration for M-N diagram without numpy, Markdown table output to stdout so engineers can pipe to files. Strong quality means all sections need real AS 3600 clause references, named dataclasses, and specific numerical details.
target_output.title: Reinforced Concrete Section Capacity CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A greenfield Python 3.12 command-line tool distributed via PyPI that computes reinforced concrete bending and shear capacity to AS 3600 or Eurocode 2, printing tabular Markdown results to stdout and exporting M-N interaction diagrams as SVG files. Uses only Python stdlib plus svgwrite, runs fully offline, and is designed for pipx installation on engineering firm workstations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI with two Click subcommands: rccap compute for bending and shear capacity tables and rccap diagram for M-N interaction SVG generation. Section geometry, reinforcement layout, and material properties are defined in a TOML config file parsed by the stdlib tomllib module. The parser module validates the TOML schema and constructs a typed SectionDefinition dataclass before any calculation is performed. AS 3600 capacity calculations use iterative bisection to solve the neutral-axis depth equilibrium equation without external numerical libraries. SVG generation via svgwrite produces vector interaction diagrams suitable for inclusion in design reports. All error conditions write to stderr with informative messages and return distinct exit codes for shell script integration.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12. Click 8 for CLI framework and subcommand routing. tomllib stdlib for TOML config parsing. math stdlib for trigonometric and iterative calculations. dataclasses stdlib for SectionDefinition, BarGroup, CapacityResult, InteractionPoint. svgwrite 1.4 for SVG M-N diagram generation. setuptools for PyPI packaging. Distributed via PyPI and installed via pipx. CI: GitHub Actions with ruff, mypy strict, pytest 90% coverage matrix on Python 3.11 and 3.12.
target_output.functional_feature_map: rccap compute: reads section.toml, validates schema (dimensions, f_c range 20-100 MPa, f_sy in set 250/500 MPa), computes phi*M_uo per AS 3600 clause 8.1.3 at zero axial force, computes phi*V_uc per AS 3600 clause 8.2.1, prints a Markdown table with M_uo, phi*M_uo, V_uc, phi*V_uc, neutral_axis_depth_mm, k_uo; with --verbose flag prints intermediate steps (alpha_2, gamma, d_n iterations) to stderr. rccap diagram: iterates neutral-axis depth from pure compression (N_umax) to pure tension (N_umin) in 50 steps, computing (N, M) capacity point at each step, exports SVG with axes labelled in kN and kN.m, marks the squash load, balanced point, and pure bending point.
target_output.system_components: Click entry point (rccap command with compute and diagram subgroups). parser.py (tomllib TOML load, schema validation, SectionDefinition construction). sections.py (SectionDefinition dataclass: b_mm, d_mm, d_prime_mm, cover_mm, f_c_MPa, f_sy_MPa, reinforcement_bars as list of BarGroup, code_standard ENUM). as3600.py (ultimate_bending_capacity, ultimate_shear_capacity, neutral_axis_depth bisection solver, alpha_2 and gamma factor functions per AS 3600 clause 8.1.3). diagram.py (InteractionCurve: iterate 50 (N, M) points, SVG canvas generation via svgwrite). report.py (Markdown table formatter for stdout, --verbose debug lines to stderr). ec2.py (stub module raising NotImplementedError for post-MVP Eurocode 2 implementation).
target_output.workflows: Bending Capacity Compute: Engineer runs 'rccap compute rect_600x300.toml --code AS3600'; parser.py loads TOML, validates b_mm=300, d_mm=550, f_c_MPa=32, f_sy_MPa=500, reinforcement_bars=[{count:4,diameter_mm:28,layer_depth_mm:530}]; as3600.py computes alpha_2=0.85-0.0015*32=0.802, gamma=0.97-0.0025*32=0.890, iterates bisection on force equilibrium 0=0.85*f_c*alpha_2*b_mm*d_n minus A_st*f_sy to find d_n=78.4mm, computes M_uo=A_st*f_sy*(d-d_n*gamma/2), applies phi=0.85, writes Markdown table to stdout; exits 0. Interaction Diagram Export: Engineer runs 'rccap diagram rect_600x300.toml --code AS3600 --output diagram.svg'; diagram.py iterates axial force from N_uo (pure compression, alpha_2*f_c*A_g) to negative A_st*f_sy (pure tension) in 50 steps, computes M capacity at each N via bisection, constructs svgwrite Drawing with polyline through (N, M) points, marks balanced point at k_uo=0.545/(0.545+0.003), exports diagram.svg.
target_output.data_model: SectionDefinition dataclass: b_mm FLOAT, d_mm FLOAT, d_prime_mm FLOAT, cover_mm FLOAT, f_c_MPa FLOAT (20-100), f_sy_MPa FLOAT (250 or 500), reinforcement_bars LIST of BarGroup, code_standard ENUM as3600/ec2. BarGroup dataclass: count INT, diameter_mm FLOAT (allowable: 12,16,20,24,28,32,36,40), layer_depth_mm FLOAT. CapacityResult dataclass: M_uo_kNm FLOAT, phi_M_uo_kNm FLOAT, V_uc_kN FLOAT, phi_V_uc_kN FLOAT, neutral_axis_depth_mm FLOAT, k_uo FLOAT, alpha_2 FLOAT, gamma FLOAT. InteractionPoint dataclass: N_kN FLOAT, M_kNm FLOAT. All types validated in parser.py before instantiation.
target_output.api_design: CLI interface: rccap compute SECTION_TOML --code AS3600_or_EC2 [--verbose] [--output-format markdown_or_csv]; rccap diagram SECTION_TOML --code AS3600_or_EC2 --output SVG_PATH [--steps 50] [--verbose]; rccap validate SECTION_TOML (dry-run schema check only, no computation). Exit codes: 0 success, 1 input validation error (malformed TOML or out-of-range values), 2 computation convergence error, 3 unsupported code standard. No HTTP API; no server.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input validation in parser.py rejects: b_mm or d_mm at or below 0, f_c_MPa outside 20-100, f_sy_MPa not in set 250/500/600, bar diameter not in standard series, layer_depth_mm at or above d_mm (reinforcement outside section depth). Warnings emitted to stderr for physically implausible but technically valid inputs (b_mm exceeds 2000mm, d_mm exceeds 4000mm). No network calls; no API keys; no temp files written. svgwrite output path validated to prevent directory traversal using pathlib.resolve() check. stdlib tomllib safely parses TOML without code execution risk.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI package published via Twine with OIDC trusted publisher on GitHub Actions version tag push. Package includes rccap entry point script plus all calculation modules. GitHub Actions pipeline: ruff lint and import check, mypy --strict, pytest 90% branch coverage on Python 3.11 and 3.12 matrix, reference value tests asserting phi*M_uo within 0.1% of hand-verified AS 3600 example calculations, build sdist and wheel, publish on tag. No server infrastructure.
target_output.observability: Exit codes 0/1/2/3 for shell and CI script integration. Stderr error messages include: failing TOML key name, out-of-range value, and AS 3600 clause reference for context. --verbose flag writes intermediate calculation steps to stderr: alpha_2 and gamma factor values, each bisection iteration d_n and residual force, final equilibrium convergence result. No remote telemetry; local only.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero ongoing infrastructure cost. PyPI and GitHub Actions free tier. pyproject.toml declares Python 3.11 or higher with svgwrite as the only runtime dependency. Cold start on a standard workstation under 0.5 seconds for compute subcommand on typical 4-6 bar section. Diagram with 50 iteration steps completes in under 1 second. Scales to batch use via shell for-loop over multiple TOML files with exit code checking.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): TOML parser with schema validation, SectionDefinition and BarGroup dataclasses, AS 3600 bending capacity per clause 8.1.3 (neutral-axis bisection, alpha_2, gamma, phi=0.85), AS 3600 shear capacity per clause 8.2.1 (k_v and theta_v simplified method), Markdown table output to stdout, M-N interaction diagram via svgwrite with 50 steps, GitHub Actions CI with reference value tests, PyPI package structure. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Eurocode 2 bending capacity implementation in ec2.py, --output-format csv option for compute subcommand, rccap validate dry-run subcommand. Phase 3 post-MVP: AS 3600 clause 8.5 deflection check, PDF report generation combining Markdown table and SVG, batch mode reading multiple sections from a single TOML array.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public functions type-annotated; mypy --strict enforced in CI. ruff for linting and import sorting. pytest reference value tests assert phi*M_uo and phi*V_uc within 0.1% tolerance of worked examples from AS 3600-2018 commentary. No floating point comparisons without tolerance. bisection solver tested for convergence with at most 50 iterations on all valid input ranges. ec2.py stub raises NotImplementedError with message 'EC2 support planned for v2.0' to prevent silent incorrect output. CHANGELOG updated on each PyPI release.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: bisection solver fails to converge for pathological section geometries with very high reinforcement ratio. Mitigation: convergence check after 100 iterations raises ComputationError with diagnostic message and d_n bounds at failure; documented in README with typical valid ranges. Risk: svgwrite SVG output not rendered correctly in older PDF converters used by some engineering firms. Mitigation: svgwrite generates SVG 1.1 with explicit viewBox and no CSS transforms; tested against Inkscape and Chrome SVG rendering. Tradeoff: stdlib math over numpy for portability at cost of slower iteration speed for large interaction curve step counts; acceptable given sub-1-second performance for 50-step diagram. Tradeoff: Click over argparse for cleaner subcommand API at cost of one additional dependency acceptable given svgwrite precedent.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed AS 3600 simplified shear model (clause 8.2.1 k_v and theta_v method) over detailed method for MVP due to simpler implementation. M-N diagram uses 50 neutral-axis depth steps as default with --steps flag allowing override. Eurocode 2 shear and deflection deferred to Phase 2 and 3. Output format defaults to Markdown for easy inclusion in engineering calculation sheets; CSV option added in Phase 2 for spreadsheet import.

metadata.notes: Greenfield RC section capacity CLI with AS 3600 clause specifics, dataclass schemas, bisection solver detail, and reference value testing requirements.

access_model.value: Fully public; no authentication required; free-trial form collects name and work email only, handled by Cloudflare Worker relay to product API
access_model.rationale: Open marketing site; trial credentials managed by product onboarding API

feature_scope.value: Interactive ROI calculator, anonymised case study outcome cards, pricing tier comparison table, free-trial sign-up form, testimonial carousel, product feature breakdown sections
feature_scope.rationale: Full conversion-optimised page feature set

mvp_scope.value: Hero section with CTA, ROI calculator, three case study cards, free-trial sign-up form with Cloudflare Worker integration
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for initial GTM launch

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare; CSP header restricting scripts to self and plausible.io; no cookies except GDPR-consent preference in localStorage; honeypot field on sign-up form to block bot submissions
security_baseline.rationale: Low-risk static site; basic headers and bot protection sufficient

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS for styling; Astro islands for ROI calculator only
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro partial hydration minimises JS shipped to browser; ideal for performance-sensitive marketing pages

backend_stack.value: No backend runtime; one Cloudflare Worker Pages Function handles free-trial form submission forwarding to product onboarding API
backend_stack.rationale: Landing page has no server-side business logic; Worker is sufficient for form relay

data_platform.value: No database; content managed in Astro MDX files and JSON fixture files committed to the repository
data_platform.rationale: Static content model eliminates database dependency

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages with global CDN; Hetzner Object Storage for WebP image assets
hosting_target.rationale: Cloudflare Pages free tier covers expected traffic; co-located Worker for form handling

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data stored on the site; GDPR cookie consent banner gates Plausible analytics script loading; consent preference stored in localStorage only
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Cookieless Plausible and no server-side storage minimise GDPR obligations

future_scope.value: Blog with construction industry content, A/B testing via Cloudflare Zaraz, localised pages for DACH and Nordics markets
future_scope.rationale: Post-launch growth roadmap items

constraints.value: Page must achieve Lighthouse performance score >= 95 on mobile; total JS bundle under 50 KB gzipped; no third-party cookies
constraints.rationale: Performance and privacy constraints from marketing team requirements

observability_baseline.value: Plausible Analytics for privacy-first pageview and conversion tracking; Cloudflare Worker logs for form submission error rates; Cloudflare Web Analytics for CDN-level metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a static marketing site

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment from main branch; preview deployments for every PR via Cloudflare Pages preview URLs
execution_preference.rationale: Fast iteration cycle for marketing content changes

project.goal: High-converting landing page driving free-trial sign-ups for construction cost overrun prediction SaaS
project.timeline: MVP launch in 3 weeks; full feature set in 6 weeks

frontend.hydration: Astro islands for ROI calculator only; rest is static HTML

backend.worker: Cloudflare Worker Pages Function for trial form relay to product onboarding API

security.headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy via Cloudflare Transform Rules
security.bot_protection: Honeypot hidden field on sign-up form

data.content: MDX files and JSON fixtures in repository

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint, type-check, and Astro build

reasoner_reviews.product: The ROI calculator is the highest-conversion element on the page and must be credible to CFO and commercial manager audiences -- inputs should mirror real construction procurement variables (contract value in GBP/EUR, contract type ENUM fixed-price/target-cost/cost-reimbursable, number of active projects) and output should reference published industry benchmark overrun rates. Generic percentage sliders will not convert this audience.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The round-1 plan introduced a Netlify Forms integration for the trial sign-up, which contradicts the locked contract specifying a Cloudflare Worker relay to the product onboarding API. This must be corrected. Astro 4 island architecture is the right call for keeping JS under the 50 KB gzipped budget -- the ROI calculator island should be the only hydrated component.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP must explicitly allowlist the Plausible Analytics script domain (plausible.io) and block all other external script sources. The Cloudflare Worker should validate the trial sign-up payload (work email domain check, honeypot empty assertion) and apply rate limiting via Cloudflare KV to prevent spam flooding the product onboarding pipeline.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse >= 95 on mobile is achievable with Astro zero-JS-by-default output, Cloudflare CDN edge caching, and Hetzner Object Storage for WebP/AVIF images with correct Cache-Control headers. The round-1 plan used unoptimised PNG screenshots; these must be replaced with WebP assets under 100 KB each.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with src/pages/index.astro as the single page entry point. src/components/ holds section components (Hero, RoiCalculator, CaseStudies, Pricing, Testimonials, SignUpForm, CookieBanner). src/content/ holds MDX files for case studies. src/data/ holds pricing-tiers.json and roi-benchmarks.json. Public assets in public/images/ as WebP.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single index page with anchor-linked sections: #hero (headline, CTA), #roi-calculator (Astro island with TypeScript logic), #case-studies (three anonymised project outcome cards from MDX), #pricing (tier comparison table), #testimonials (static carousel), #sign-up (free-trial form). Navigation is a sticky header with anchor links. Cookie consent banner gates Plausible script loading on first visit.
frontend.performance_notes: ROI calculator is the only Astro island (client:load directive). All other components render to static HTML at build time. Images served as WebP from Hetzner Object Storage via Cloudflare CDN with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000. Tailwind CSS purged at build time. Total JS budget: ROI calculator island ~18 KB gzipped, Plausible snippet ~1 KB, cookie consent ~2 KB -- well under 50 KB target.
frontend.frontend_risks: Risk: ROI calculator benchmark figures become stale; mitigate by sourcing from roi-benchmarks.json fixture file updatable without a code change. Risk: Cookie consent banner blocking Plausible script reduces analytics coverage on first visit; legally required and acceptable.

security.auth_design: No authentication on the static site. Cloudflare Worker validates trial sign-up payloads: work email regex, company name non-empty, honeypot field empty, rate limit 5 submissions per IP per hour via Cloudflare KV counter.
security.abuse_prevention: Honeypot hidden input named 'website' with CSS display:none; Worker rejects submissions where this field is non-empty. Cloudflare Bot Fight Mode enabled on the Pages domain to block known bot ASNs before reaching the Worker.
security.privacy_controls: Plausible Analytics script loaded only after explicit GDPR consent; consent stored in localStorage key 'plausible_consent'. No cookies set by the site. Trial form collects name and work email only; data forwarded to product API and not retained in Worker or Pages.

data.entities: case_study_entries (MDX frontmatter: id, sector ENUM infrastructure/commercial/residential, contract_value_range, overrun_reduced_pct, project_duration_months, anonymised_description). roi_scenarios (roi-benchmarks.json: contract_type, avg_overrun_pct_without_tool, avg_overrun_pct_with_tool, data_source_label). pricing_tiers (pricing-tiers.json: tier_name, monthly_price_gbp, project_limit, user_limit, features_list). trial_signups (no storage; forwarded to product API via Worker). testimonials (testimonials.json: name, title, company_size_band, quote).
data.analytics_events: Plausible custom events: roi_calculator_used (props: contract_type, project_count_band), cta_clicked (props: section, variant), trial_signup_submitted, trial_signup_success, case_study_expanded (props: sector).

devops.deployment_topology: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub. Main branch auto-deploys to production. Every PR generates a preview deployment. Cloudflare Worker deployed as a Pages Function in functions/api/trial-signup.ts alongside the static output.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: checkout, bun install, bun run type-check (tsc --noEmit), bun run lint (ESLint with astro plugin), bun run build:images (Squoosh CLI converts PNG sources to WebP), bun run build (astro build). Cloudflare Pages GitHub integration triggers deployment on merge to main.
devops.cost_controls: Cloudflare Pages free tier: 500 builds/month, unlimited bandwidth. Cloudflare Worker free tier: 100K requests/day. Hetzner Object Storage: ~$3/month for image assets. Plausible Analytics: $9/month. Total under $15/month.
