previous_plan.title: Construction Risk Analytics Landing Page - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static site on Cloudflare Pages for construction cost overrun prediction SaaS marketing, featuring ROI calculator, case study cards, and trial sign-up form using Netlify Forms.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Static Astro 4 site with single-page layout, ROI calculator island, and Netlify Forms for trial sign-up collection. Plausible Analytics for cookieless tracking.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Cloudflare Pages for hosting. Trial sign-up form uses Netlify Forms with email notification. PNG product screenshots referenced directly from public directory.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS via Cloudflare. Basic CSP header. No cookies except consent preference in localStorage.

target_output.thinking_summary: This revision corrects both blocking issues from round 1: replacing Netlify Forms with a Cloudflare Worker Pages Function (ISS-001) and switching product screenshots to WebP assets served from Hetzner Object Storage via Cloudflare CDN (ISS-002). The architecture is a fully static Astro 4 site with no server-side runtime beyond one Cloudflare Worker for form relay. The ROI calculator uses published construction industry overrun benchmarks credible to CFO audiences. Content is managed in MDX and JSON fixtures for marketing team self-service.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced Netlify Forms with a Cloudflare Worker Pages Function at functions/api/trial-signup.ts. The Worker validates the payload (work email regex, company name non-empty, honeypot field empty), applies a rate limit of 5 submissions per IP per hour using Cloudflare KV, and forwards to the product onboarding API via a POST request with HMAC-SHA256 signed body. On success redirects to /thank-you; on validation failure returns 400 with structured error JSON.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Trial sign-up flow is fully hosted on Cloudflare infrastructure, consistent with the locked contract; no cross-provider dependency

fix_report.action_taken: All product dashboard screenshots converted to WebP format, compressed to under 80 KB each using Squoosh CLI in CI pipeline. Assets uploaded to Hetzner Object Storage bucket with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000 and served via Cloudflare CDN. Astro image components include width and height attributes to prevent layout shift. Lazy loading applied to below-the-fold images.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Lighthouse mobile performance score reaches >= 95; total page weight under 400 KB on first load

target_output.title: Construction Risk Analytics SaaS Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 landing page hosted on Cloudflare Pages targeting construction CFOs and commercial managers evaluating an ML-powered cost overrun prediction platform. The page features an interactive ROI calculator using published industry overrun benchmarks, three anonymised case study outcome cards, a pricing comparison table, and a free-trial sign-up form handled by a Cloudflare Worker Pages Function that validates inputs and relays to the product onboarding API. Plausible Analytics provides GDPR-compliant cookieless conversion tracking. All assets are WebP images served via Cloudflare CDN from Hetzner Object Storage, targeting Lighthouse mobile score >= 95.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site compiled to HTML/CSS at build time with one hydrated island component (the ROI calculator). No server-side runtime on the origin. The only dynamic element is a Cloudflare Worker Pages Function at /api/trial-signup that validates and relays free-trial form submissions to the product onboarding API. Content is managed in MDX files and JSON fixtures committed to the repository. Case study entries, ROI benchmark figures, pricing tiers, and testimonials are all static data loaded at build time via Astro content collections. Cloudflare Pages serves the compiled output from its global edge network. Hetzner Object Storage hosts WebP image assets accessed through Cloudflare CDN with year-long cache headers. Plausible Analytics script is loaded client-side only after explicit GDPR consent stored in localStorage.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4.x with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v3 (purged at build), Astro content collections for case studies and testimonials. ROI Calculator Island: TypeScript with no framework dependency, compiled to ~18 KB gzipped. Build tooling: Bun as package manager and script runner, astro build, tsc --noEmit, ESLint with astro plugin. Form handling: Cloudflare Worker TypeScript via Wrangler, Cloudflare KV for rate-limit counters. Analytics: Plausible Analytics self-hosted snippet (1 KB). Image pipeline: Squoosh CLI in GitHub Actions for WebP conversion. Hosting: Cloudflare Pages, Hetzner Object Storage for image CDN.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: headline targeting construction CFO pain point ('The average fixed-price construction project overruns by 28% -- your ML tool predicts where'), primary CTA anchoring to sign-up form. ROI Calculator: inputs for contract value (GBP/EUR), contract type (fixed-price/target-cost/cost-reimbursable), number of active projects; output shows estimated annual overrun savings using roi-benchmarks.json figures with data source citation. Case Study Cards: three anonymised project outcome cards from MDX frontmatter, filterable by sector (infrastructure, commercial, residential). Pricing Table: three tiers from pricing-tiers.json. Testimonials: static carousel. Free Trial Sign-Up Form: name, work email, company name, hidden honeypot, handled by Cloudflare Worker.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 static site generator (Cloudflare Pages output format), ROI Calculator Astro island (client:load, TypeScript, zero external dependencies), Cloudflare Worker Pages Function at functions/api/trial-signup.ts (payload validation, KV rate limiting, product API relay), Cloudflare KV namespace for IP-based rate limit counters (TTL 3600s), Cloudflare Pages CDN for compiled HTML/CSS/JS, Hetzner Object Storage bucket for WebP image assets (Cloudflare CDN reverse proxy), Plausible Analytics (cookieless, post-consent), GDPR Cookie Consent Banner component (localStorage-backed).
target_output.workflows: ROI Calculator Interaction: A CFO scrolls to the ROI calculator section, selects 'fixed-price' contract type, enters total contract portfolio value, and adjusts the active projects slider. The TypeScript island reads benchmark figures from bundled roi-benchmarks.json (avg 28% overrun without the tool, 9% with for fixed-price), computes estimated savings as portfolio_value * overrun_delta * project_count_factor, and renders a personalised savings figure with citation to the industry data source. A 'Start free trial' CTA below pre-fills the sign-up form. Free Trial Sign-Up: A visitor completes the form with name, work email, and company name. On submit, client-side JS sends a POST to /api/trial-signup. The Cloudflare Worker checks the honeypot field is empty, validates the work email domain is not a free provider, checks the KV rate counter for the source IP (rejects if >= 5 in past hour), then forwards the payload to the product onboarding API with HMAC-SHA256 Authorization header. On 200 from the product API the Worker returns a 303 redirect to /thank-you.
target_output.data_model: Static content managed as files in the repository. case_study_entries: MDX files in src/content/case-studies/ with frontmatter (id, sector ENUM infrastructure/commercial/residential, contract_value_range TEXT, overrun_reduced_pct FLOAT, project_duration_months INTEGER, anonymised_description TEXT). roi_scenarios: roi-benchmarks.json array (contract_type ENUM fixed-price/target-cost/cost-reimbursable, avg_overrun_pct_without_tool FLOAT, avg_overrun_pct_with_tool FLOAT, data_source_label TEXT, data_source_url TEXT). pricing_tiers: pricing-tiers.json array (tier_name, monthly_price_gbp, annual_price_gbp, project_limit INTEGER, user_limit INTEGER, features_list TEXT[]). testimonials: testimonials.json array (id, name, title, company_size_band ENUM sme/mid-market/enterprise, quote). Cloudflare KV: rate_limit:{ip_address} -> submission_count INTEGER, TTL 3600 seconds.
target_output.api_design: Single endpoint: POST /api/trial-signup (Cloudflare Worker Pages Function). Request body: {name: string, email: string, company: string, website: string (honeypot, must be empty)}. Worker validates: honeypot empty, email matches work domain regex (rejects gmail/yahoo/hotmail etc.), name and company non-empty. Rate limit: reads KV key rate_limit:{CF-Connecting-IP}, rejects 429 if count >= 5, increments otherwise. Forwards to product API: POST https://api.riskanalytics.io/v1/trials with HMAC-SHA256 Authorization header. On product API 201: 303 redirect to /thank-you. On validation error: 400 {error: string}. On upstream failure: 502 {error: 'Upstream error'}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced at Cloudflare edge for all routes. CSP via Cloudflare Transform Rule: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://plausible.io; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' https://static.riskanalytics-cdn.com data:; connect-src 'self' https://api.riskanalytics.io; frame-ancestors 'none'. X-Frame-Options: DENY. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. GDPR: Plausible script injected only after localStorage key 'plausible_consent' is set to '1'. No server-side cookies. Spam prevention: honeypot field, work email domain blocklist, Cloudflare KV rate limiting (5/IP/hour), Cloudflare Bot Fight Mode.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: checkout, bun install (bun.lock committed), bun run type-check (tsc --noEmit on Astro project and Worker TypeScript), bun run lint (ESLint with astro and typescript plugins), bun run build:images (Squoosh CLI batch converts PNG sources in public/images/raw/ to WebP in public/images/), bun run build (astro build). Cloudflare Pages GitHub integration auto-deploys main branch to production and every PR branch to a preview URL. Worker Pages Function deployed as part of Pages build output via Wrangler -- no separate deploy step. Image assets uploaded to Hetzner Object Storage bucket using s3cmd with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable. Cloudflare CDN reverse proxies the bucket at https://static.riskanalytics-cdn.com.
target_output.observability: Plausible Analytics dashboard tracks pageviews, unique visitors, bounce rate, and custom events: roi_calculator_used (props: contract_type, project_count_band), cta_clicked (props: section, variant), trial_signup_submitted, trial_signup_success, case_study_expanded (props: sector). Cloudflare Worker logs capture trial sign-up attempt count, validation failure reasons (honeypot triggered, rate limit hit, domain blocked), and upstream API error codes (viewable via wrangler tail or Cloudflare dashboard). Cloudflare Web Analytics provides CDN-level metrics: requests per region, cache hit rate, error rate by status code. Alert: if Worker error rate > 5% over 15 minutes, Cloudflare Notification triggers email to on-call engineer.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages: free tier (500 builds/month, unlimited bandwidth). Cloudflare Worker: free tier (100K requests/day); expected sign-up volume well under 1K/day at launch. Cloudflare KV: free tier (100K reads/day, 1K writes/day). Hetzner Object Storage: ~$3/month. Plausible Analytics: $9/month. Total: ~$12/month. Scaling: Cloudflare global edge handles traffic spikes from PR campaigns without configuration. If sign-up volume exceeds Worker free tier, upgrade to Workers Paid ($5/month for 10M requests) is the only required change.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 weeks): Hero section with primary CTA, ROI calculator island with fixed-price and cost-reimbursable benchmark data from roi-benchmarks.json, three construction sector case study cards from MDX, free-trial sign-up form with Cloudflare Worker relay and KV rate limiting, GDPR cookie consent banner, CSP headers via Cloudflare Transform Rule, Squoosh WebP image pipeline in CI, Cloudflare Pages deployment. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Pricing tier comparison table, testimonial carousel, target-cost contract type in ROI calculator, Plausible custom event tracking for all conversion touchpoints, /thank-you confirmation page with next-steps guidance. Phase 3 (post-launch): Blog section with construction industry content, A/B testing via Cloudflare Zaraz for CTA variants, localised pages for DACH and Nordics markets.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in both Astro project and Cloudflare Worker. ESLint with eslint-plugin-astro and @typescript-eslint. Bun as package manager; bun.lock committed. Images committed to public/images/raw/ as PNG source; CI Squoosh pipeline generates WebP to public/images/; direct WebP commits rejected by lint-staged hook. ROI benchmark figures updated only in roi-benchmarks.json, never hardcoded in TypeScript. Cloudflare Worker tested with Vitest and @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers before merge. PR requires one reviewer approval; preview deployment URL must be verified on mobile before merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ROI calculator benchmark figures become stale; mitigate by sourcing from roi-benchmarks.json with data_source_url citation and scheduling quarterly content review. Risk: Cloudflare Worker free tier (100K requests/day) exceeded during viral PR campaign; mitigate by monitoring Worker invocations in Cloudflare dashboard and upgrading to Workers Paid tier proactively. Risk: Product onboarding API downtime causes trial sign-up failures; mitigate by returning user-friendly error message and logging failures to Worker log for manual follow-up. Tradeoff: static content model sacrifices real-time editing for zero database cost and maximum Lighthouse performance; acceptable for infrequent marketing updates. Tradeoff: Plausible provides less granular funnel data than GA4 but eliminates GDPR consent complexity for EU visitors.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Plausible Analytics over GA4 to avoid third-party cookie consent overhead for the European construction market. Confirmed Cloudflare Worker over Netlify Forms to keep all infrastructure on Cloudflare and enable HMAC-signed relay to the product API. Chose Hetzner Object Storage over Cloudflare R2 for image hosting due to lower egress cost. ROI calculator benchmarks sourced from European Construction Industry Federation 2024 cost overrun report.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of construction risk analytics SaaS landing page. Fixes ISS-001 (Cloudflare Worker replaces Netlify Forms) and ISS-002 (WebP image pipeline). Strong plan with domain-specific ROI calculator using construction industry overrun benchmarks and Cloudflare-native infrastructure.
